Enigmatic Space Information Security & P.title: Enigmatic Space Information Security & Protection System
Enigmatic Space Information Security & P.displayTitle: Enigmatic Space Information Security & Protection System
Enigmatic Space Information Security & P.description:
The Enigmatic Space Information Security & Protection System, commonly abbreviated as the E.S.I.S.P.S., is a system developed by the Major Explorer Group.
Overview
The Enigmatic Space Information Security & Protection System is a comprehensive, secure and efficient database for anomalous locations to ensure that information of anomalous locations is properly protected, stored, and encrypted between bases to prevent intercepting, in order to support the M.E.G.'s research, exploration, and risk prevention of dangerous anomalies.

History
During the initial stages of exploration of the Backrooms, the M.E.G. came to the realization that the archives of anomalous locations and data were confronted with the peril of loss, leakage, or malevolent alteration due to the dearth of effective information protection measures. In an attempt to address this pressing issue, the M.E.G. made a resolute decision to invest a considerable amount of resources in the development of a specialized information security and protection system.

After numerous years of painstaking research and repeated experimentation, the first generation of E.S.I.S.P.S. system was unveiled in 1979, which was then referred to as the Documents Protection System (D.P.S.). Despite the fact that the system managed to achieve certain commendable results in data encryption and storage, it still exhibited certain shortcomings in the stability of data transmission and the flexibility of access control.

Two years later, the system fell victim to a hacking attack by a hacker going by the username "Doog", resulting in a large number of files being intercepted. In the wake of this setback, the M.E.G. promptly introduced more advanced encryption algorithms, optimized the transmission protocols, and significantly strengthened the access control measures. Through numerous iterations and upgrades, the E.S.I.S.P.S. has ultimately evolved into a mature and reliable system.

In 2014, the E.S.I.S.P.S. system achieved a momentous breakthrough in the realm of information sharing and collaboration with other related organizations such as the Kauer, the E.R.C., and the B.I.F.. This significant breakthrough not only broadened the scope of application of the system but also laid a solid foundation for cross-organizational research collaboration concerning anomalous locations. Eventually, a portion of the files were uploaded to the database, attaining their current state.

Information Storage Procedure

Data Acquisition
The data of E.S.I.S.P.S originates from the administrators of the M.E.G. To begin with, the explorers will undertake an exploration of the anomalous location and meticulously record it in both text and images. Subsequently, the notes are handed over to the administrators. It is these administrators, who possess the system access rights to edit the document, that take on the responsibility of entering the data. They painstakingly organize it into a qualified document and securely save it within the system.

Encryption
The multi-layer, high-strength encryption algorithm is employed to encrypt the input data, thereby constructing an impregnable and unassailable line of defense for the confidentiality of the information. At present, the specific details of this encryption method cannot be disclosed. However, at this juncture, the possibility of attempting to crack any information stored within this system is absolutely nil. There is no chance, not even the greatest hacker, for anyone to succeed in deciphering the data protected by this encryption mechanism.

Secure Transmission
When data is being transmitted between bases, the system continuously monitors and detects the transmission process in real time. It is capable of dynamically adjusting the transmission parameters and strategies at any given moment, based on the specific transmission situation, in order to effectively deal with potential malicious attacks and ensure the security and integrity of the data.

Access Control
Accessing this information demands rigorous authentication and authorization procedures. For instance, to access information related to The First, an Access Level Y is prerequisite. This implies that only Head of Departments, Impresarios, and The First himself are granted the permission to access this information. Through such measures, the M.E.G. effectively guarantee that confidential information remains undisclosed and protected from unauthorized access.
Former Name and Special Editions

Documents Protection System(D.P.S.)
This is the predecessor of the system. At that time, the system was highly antiquated. Its encryption system was severely underdeveloped, and it had a propensity to collapse frequently. As a direct consequence of these severe shortcomings, it was regrettably abandoned for a considerable period of time.

Anomalous Locations Database(A.L.D)
This system has witnessed a remarkable advancement in its encryption system when compared to the first generation system. The impetus for the development of this generation of systems was the occurrence of hacking incidents. The M.E.G. came to the realization that they urgently needed a more robust and powerful secrecy system. Driven by this realization, this generation of systems was meticulously developed and brought into existence.

Information Query Service Software for Explorers
This special edition of the E.S.I.S.P.S. was specifically developed with the aim of providing convenience to explorers. It enables explorers to access and view the published information on any terminal. Although, in the present day, due to the presence of the comprehensive database, this feature might seem somewhat lacking in practicality and even appear absurd. Nevertheless, at that particular time, it actually created quite a significant commotion and even gave rise to the development of a feature that could subsequently be utilized for sharing information with other organizations.

Backrooms Today.title: Backrooms Today
Backrooms Today.description:
𝔅𝔞𝔠𝔨𝔯𝔬𝔬𝔪𝔰 𝔗𝔬𝔡𝔞𝔶Bringing you the top story from the back-alley of reality!

 Description
Backrooms, Today! is a cooperative organization, specializing as a private news agency. It is the oldest and largest independent news agency in The Backrooms that isn't a state-run entity. Backrooms Today doesn't have outposts of its own, instead having headquarters situated within major metropolitan areas such as the 399 Megacolony. It operates as a unincorporated association, and produces news reports distributed to its members via BACK-CHAT. In recent years, it focuses on various reader centered initiatives such as an online blog as a platform for more opinion-driven content accessed by subscribers and members.

Backrooms, Today! reports across multiple media formats, providing content to a wide range of media outlets ranging from TV, Newspapers, and digitally. It delivers newspapers mostly independently from its numerous printing presses, and operating within many metropolitan areas. Backrooms Today spans across ten different offices operated by some 564 employees communicating with each other online and adhere to a standard business hierarchy found in the Frontrooms.

Over the years, Backrooms Today has endured numerous controversies and allegations targeted towards its legitimacy. Many ideologically zealous GOIs such as Ruphis-Proletarium have tried to infiltrate Backrooms Today in an attempt to twist narratives within their own favor. But that's not to say Backrooms Today isn't subject to personal bias from its own employees or bias coming from concessions made by the cooperative to expand its base of operations. Though it still remains the go-to news source and blog for independent journalism outside of state-run news agencies.

 History
Its founding dates back to 2005, founded by primarily Shivira Dizedayokzhu and three other independent journalists formed a cooperative venture with the aim of providing news coverage of Ncipetherazi internal affairs. Its main founding member Shivira, is a Ncipetherazi entrepreneur who suffered a career ending shoulder injury during mandatory military service which led him to being hospitalized for x amount of time. After his recovery, he turned away from military life and started a small news agency mainly providing coverage via newspaper on cultural and domestic affairs within Level -406.

It is at this time one of the other founding members created digital copies of its newspapers published to the Ncipetherazi database directory, unlike most news agencies of its time, was able to be accessed by outsiders meant as a media campaign to brodcast Ncipetherazi culture. Another part of its digital initiative was getting in touch with other independent journalists living within Level 10 in the crossfire, it offered journalists a larger platform to document their testimonies of the KC war. By 2006, it had created a branch operating in the Backrooms Colonists, but pressure from regional customers forced it to sell its service to various regional newspaper groups, the cooperative later had taken the name Backrooms Today as a transnational News Agency instead of being a Ncipetherazi-centric agency.

In 2009, Backrooms Today partnered with publishing firm Colonist War Room Inc. to launch Backrooms Today-War Briefing which transmitted coverage of ongoing wars and the geopolitical landscape of the Backrooms digitally and through wire, cementing the creation of the news agency's new branch operating in the Backrooms Colonists, its largest branch. BT saw a large influx of subscribers and viewers tuning in to read the latest article published, it was renowned for its relative unbiased coverage of the Korthie-Casaiagen war and being a transparent news source.

Ever since its remarkable success in the Backrooms Colonists, it eyed up the opportunity of having an office established in New London and confronted the M.E.G. via an online business meeting and offered the M.E.G's state run news agency an opportunity for syndication, a share of its revenue made from subscriptions, and gain access to stories they are otherwise unable to cover due to lack of ethos. BT hoped the prospect of mutual benefit between both parties would convince the M.E.G, Ultimately the M.E.G rejected the proposal out of skepticism of the legitimacy of its reports. BT inevitably had to offer concessions to the M.E.G. to gain the rights to set up office in New London, where Backrooms Today would aid the M.E.G's reporting with expertise in the respective field.

In earlier years such as 2020, Backrooms Today encouraged a lot of troubles in the endeavour of setting up a new post within post-KC war Aurielle due to major security risks that plagued Aurielle after decades of war, and major financial risks as investing in Aurielle at the time outweighed the potential returns. It had high-insurance premiums and volatility in the region hindered it for the longest time. BT was under the constrains of ethical concerns as it would be viewed as profitting off a war-torn region and fueling its somewhat murky reputation. Though BT invested some money into Aurielle's reconstruction process post-KC and finally set up a news post in 2025 welcomed with open arms by Aurielle.

 Hoax announcement
On June 1st, 2021, the official Aurielle Today (Backrooms Today's Aurielle branch) backchat and BT account posted numerous spam articles and messages pertaining spurious attacks on the Auriellen president, Arenet Matlon, falsely claiming that he had been killed. This led to mass hysteria and further distrust of Backrooms Today as it had been involved in major scandals before, like the Bottlegate incident regarding its security. Though it was eventually found out that the official Aurielle Today was hacked by Team 403 as apart of its broader egress cyber attacks on governments utilizing BackOs systems. The false alarms were swiftly taken down.

 The Bottlegate Incident
"Bottlegate" is an incident that occurred during the rise of Agorean mafia in 2013, which involved the leaking of several Impesario class documents of the Backrooms Colonists and M.E.G, and using Backrooms Today's expansive online news network as a platform to spread aforementioned documents. The leaked documents entailed descriptions of Tulpa named ████████ that exhibited ████████████████████████████ and ████████████████████. The ████████ caused ████████████████████ to ███████████.

Addendum 1:
 Parties in Attendance
*Interviee: Wen Zo (Boss of M.E.G BT branch)
*Interviewer: CLASSIFIED
*Source: Audio Call

Phenomenon 15-A.title: Phenomenon 15-A
Phenomenon 15-A.description:
Phenomenon 15-A, best known as Everlasting Isolation, is a mysterious phenomenon that branches from Phenomenon 15, albeit with especially rare occurrence. It, unlike Alone, does not make a definitive presence in individual levels, though it is unpredictable when it manifests and puts the affected wanderer at severe mental risk. Wanderers afflicted by Everlasting Isolation are seemingly placed into their own distinct clone of the Backrooms, entirely devoid of other human beings or entities. While the wanderer is still able to noclip throughout different levels and interact with their environment, escaping the effects of Everlasting Isolation is a distant dream for those afflicted.

The wanderer's individual clone of the Backrooms only includes levels that would be considered "liminal" by conventional standards. These levels, including Level 0, Level 4, Level 21, etc. are far more difficult to exit individually while in the "isolated" Backrooms, with wanderers only typically moving between levels every 4-5 months. Transition into Phenomenon 15-A is a gradual process that happens over 3-4 hours, with the final stage of separation being the loss of contact with the baseline Backrooms.

Wanderers afflicted by Everlasting Isolation will experience an exceedingly higher rate of insomnia throughout their time spent in their separate clone of the Backrooms, as all biological processes are paused during the phenomenon's effects. After approximately 48 hours spent affected by Everlasting Isolation, fatigue acquired from lack of sleep evolves into a sense of hopeless torpidity. Past ~96 hours of Everlasting Isolation's effects, mental hazards are especially prevalent. Wanderers cannot undergo Roth Syndrome while under Everlasting Isolation.

Similarly to Phenomenon 15, Everlasting Isolation's "clone" of the Backrooms is known to possess minor differences compared to its true counterpart. Most notably, a variety of additional levels are known to exist within it which cannot be accessed from the true Backrooms; hypothetically, this may be a result of Temporary Manifestation having a separate trajectory within Everlasting Isolation's realm. Temporary Manifestation within the isolated Backrooms seems to favor levels which lack a "liminal" aesthetic or contain a visible sky. However, due to the essentially non-existent communication between those afflicted by Everlasting Isolation and average wanderers, these levels are practically impossible to document, only having rough physical descriptions as proof of their existence.

Yet, the truly horrifying aspect of this phenomenon is; we don't know if anyone gets affected by it upon first noclipping into the Backrooms.
Phenomenon 15-A.lead:
Phenomenon 15-A, best known as Everlasting Isolation, is a mysterious phenomenon that branches from Phenomenon 15, albeit with especially rare occurrence. It, unlike Alone, does not make a definitive presence in individual levels, though it is unpredictable when it manifests and puts the affected wanderer at severe mental risk. Wanderers afflicted by Everlasting Isolation are seemingly placed into their own distinct clone of the Backrooms, entirely devoid of other human beings or entities. While the wanderer is still able to noclip throughout different levels and interact with their environment, escaping the effects of Everlasting Isolation is a distant dream for those afflicted.

The wanderer's individual clone of the Backrooms only includes levels that would be considered "liminal" by conventional standards. These levels, including Level 0, Level 4, Level 21, etc. are far more difficult to exit individually while in the "isolated" Backrooms, with wanderers only typically moving between levels every 4-5 months. After approximately 48 hours spent affected by Everlasting Isolation, fatigue acquired from lack of sleep evolves into a sense of hopeless torpidity. Past ~96 hours of Everlasting Isolation's effects, mental hazards are especially prevalent. Wanderers cannot undergo Roth Syndrome while under Everlasting Isolation.

Similarly to Phenomenon 15, Everlasting Isolation's "clone" of the Backrooms is known to possess minor differences compared to its true counterpart. Most notably, a variety of additional levels are known to exist within it which cannot be accessed from the true Backrooms; hypothetically, this may be a result of Temporary Manifestation having a separate trajectory within Everlasting Isolation's realm. Temporary Manifestation within the isolated Backrooms seems to favor levels which lack a "liminal" aesthetic or contain a visible sky. However, due to the essentially non-existent communication between those afflicted by Everlasting Isolation and average wanderers, these levels are practically impossible to document, only having rough physical descriptions as proof of their existence.

Yet, the truly horrifying aspect of this phenomenon is; we don't know if anyone gets affected by it upon first noclipping into the Backrooms.

Phenomenon 28.title: Phenomenon 28
Phenomenon 28.description:
Phenomenon 28, or simply the Necro-Broadcast is an anomaly during October 31st that permanently gives radios and televisions the anomalous power to speak to, hear and even see ghosts of both wanderers and entities that have died on the level that Object 28 is in. While it is estimated that only 0.2% of all devices will gain these changes, considering how large the Backrooms is and that some levels may revolve around certain themes like electronics; there is a good chance that a wanderer may accidentally find equipment altered by the Necro-Broadcast.

If the object is damaged in any shape or form, (this excludes small scratches) then Phenomenon 28 is nullified, even if it goes under repair. Often times, a loud, distorted scream is heard on destruction; however, it is proven from tests that the souls that interact by the Necro-Broadcast don't feel pain if their means of communication is destroyed. Instead it is the machine itself that causes this ear-piercing sound. Due to this, it is advised to not take devices enhanced by Phenomenon 28 as it is fragile and might alert unwanted attention near the owner.

Jack-o-lanterns and even the Halloween Demon will destroy any machinery affected by Phenomenon 28 if they realise that there is a human soul making contact with the realm of the living. facelings can express many types of emotions when seeing the spirits using the Necro-Broadcast. Sometimes, entities are seen crying as they seemingly talk to dead relatives or allies. If an entity finds that they are talking to a wanderer that was slain by them, they will appear distressed as the spirit beings to torment them. It was observed that spirits that appear enraged by an observer will "leave" the broadcast to haunt their target of hatred. These vengeful spirits will no longer be seen on any instance of Phenomenon 28.

While it might be undesirable for the most part, if a ghost that has no ill will against you becomes the host of the Necro-Broadcast, it might become beneficial as they can give information about surroundings or give warnings based on how they died to listeners. In some cases, where the phantom is much stronger, they may even give critical information such as secrets, blessings, curses and even foresee the future.

Phenomenon 35.title: Phenomenon 35
Phenomenon 35.description:
Roth Syndrome, known more casually as The Wretched Cycle, is a multi-stage anomalous psychological affliction exclusive to those within the wider Backrooms. Being extremely pervasive across nearly every level within the Wake, nearly everyone who enters will at some point be subject to its influence.

Working in tandem with the mundane mental symptoms of prolonged habitation within the Backrooms, Roth Syndrome and its effects are arguably the most prevalent threat any wanderer within this dimension will face, requiring high amounts of mental discipline and caution to avoid.

Phenomenon 29.title: Phenomenon 29
Phenomenon 29.description:
Blurring is a poorly-understood anomalous mental hazard that affects wanderers in extremely dark places, chiefly the Void. Blurring is frequently described as 'blinking and being somewhere else'. The most common interpretation of this statement is one being instantaneously transported to a new place and time, without recalling how one arrived. Commonly associated with a lacking of or 'blurry' memory, remembrance of the process of blurring is shoddy at best, and overwhelmingly rare. This phenomenon almost entirely occurs to those currently in the Void, which creates large gaps in one's memory due to the sensory deprivation. It is unknown if the recollections are barely a fraction or the whole of the process behind blurring. Although this phenomenon has been occurred elsewhere, it has never been recorded in the company of others.

Memories of blurring are notably distorted and blurry (hence the name), and recalling them in sufficient detail is almost impossible. Those affected describe it as every memory stopping the rest from exiting as it itself yearns for freedom, trapping themselves in the back of the mind, blurry and unable to be recalled. The few that can be adequately recalled usually have strange, nonsensical or inconsistent details, and commonly involve a motif of shattering. Those who attempt to recall all memories of blurring are suddenly hit with a mental wave of distress, often accompanied by the development of mental disorders. Keeping a journal for documentation has proven fruitless, as there is a sudden gap in writing during the phenomenon, with any sensical writing being replaced with random letters and scribbles, with some portions of the page being redacted.

Individuals actively experiencing blurring tend to forget key things such as where they were, who the are, and when they are. Afflicted persons exhibit an increased pain tolerance and a degree of emotional detachment, which are typically traced to their 'detachment' from the world as a whole during blurring. Blurring has no set length, with the longest ever blurring having lasted two months and three days, although people stuck in the Void have almost certainly experienced it for longer. Immediate aftereffects include panic attacks and severe paranoia, and scarcely bleeding from the eyes and mouth.

Blurring often results in one finding themselves in a place entirely unrelated from their original location. This can include entering dead-end levels or levels that require multiple no-clips to arrive at. While blurring may lead to dangerous levels, it never leaves one in actively dangerous situations. The levels one most commonly ends up in are typically levels with a strong connection to the void, such as Level 483. The following is a list of blurring durations and where they inevitably led.

----

Phenomenon 6.title: Phenomenon 6
Phenomenon 6.description:
Have you ever wondered, why is technology of the past seemingly anomalous, either some magical clock or some neon sign that hypnotises you, if it is made in the past and considered to be "old" technology, it gains powers? I noticed these patterns and propose a theory: as humanity advances, the Backrooms collects these forgotten remains and infuses it with itself so these machines also become bizarre in nature. So far, with all this evidence, I am most likely right.

Phenomenon 6 or Technological Compatibility is that affects most outdated technology within the Backrooms. It is responsible for ensuring machinery or inventions made will remain in use even if they are no longer the best invention of the current era. Any piece of technology that is deemed as outdated will receive a Backrooms exclusive phenomenon attached to them which will ensure that any device used has a purpose and is not discarded.

Ropes were given prophetic abilities, doors may act as portals to other levels, radios can speak to the dead, electrical generators found in Level 3 supply power to the Backrooms, even loud alarms and sirens found in levels are often found in levels with large entity populations which can distract creatures or warn wanderers they entered a dangerous level.

While Phenomenon 6 is helpful, when not properly used these blessings can cause much harm such as creating strobing affects intended to stun entities, old pipes that heat up to be used as a furnace but instead burn wanderers alive and even clockwork machines being mishandled and ending up crushing wanderers to death. The reason why we are certain that Phenomenon 6 is in our interests is due to entities and even books found within the Backrooms that refer to an anomalous blessing given to abandoned tools which encourage use.

Of course, using old technology is dangerous as we need modern weapons to deal with ever adapting entities and threats, so this can be used as a counter argument revealing that the Backrooms is trying to force primitive nature onto humans to discourage safety in technological advancements. Some even point out how certain groups like Marauders are technologically more advanced than humans yet current technology doesn't appear to have anomalous assistance. Some suggest that BACK-NET is humanity's next technology upgrade until we are as advanced.

It is theorised that technology based entities are a hostile form of Phenomenon 6 yet this remains unknown, some entities claim that creatures like The Mustang always existed even before technology claimed to be made by humans was invented, in fact brands found in the Frontrooms experienced time anomalies where they existed before they were made. For example, various Pepsi and Coca-cola vending machines are found in levels way before even soda ingredients were discovered.

Phenomenon 6 continues to fascinate, change and adapt every year and we should do the same.

In the eyes of this dimension, what is forgotten shall join its mass..

Written by Die Kaoskraft.

Architect Industries.title: Architect Industries
Architect Industries.description:
Architect Industries, oftentimes simply called the Architects, are a maintenance and development organization dedicated to maintaining industrial production and terraforming the Backrooms. Responsible for the renovation of numerous locales within the Backrooms, as well as managing the vast majority of industrial production within the dimension, they hold great significance to human civilization within the Wake.

Originating as a splinter group of the Major Explorer Group and numbering in around 12,000 personnel, they are well acclaimed for their activities, famous for their renovation of Level 154 and completing hundreds of industrial and construction projects per year.
Architect Industries.lead:
GROUP OF INTEREST

PRIMARY GROUP TYPE
Development

Influence: 8/10

Hostility: 3/10

Organization: 7/10

Phenomenon 33.title: Phenomenon 33
Phenomenon 33.description:
Phemonenon 33, commonly known as "Unnatural Recklessness", is a mental disorder affecting wanderers throughout The Backrooms. When a person shows signs of the severe lack of fear, to the point where they are ignorant of the dangers in front of them, they are called the victims of Unnatural Recklessness.

This phenomenon seems insignificant yet extremely dangerous if the victim contracts it during explorations or entity sightings. Despite its prolonged existence, no researchers have developed a cure for it, nor have they proven the existence of one.

Phenomenon 37.title: Phenomenon 37
Phenomenon 37.description:
The Harvest of Fuel is a 7 day long event in which Eye Fuel is not only more common but temporarily becomes more powerful and beneficial. During the days coming up to the holiday, various resources become more common. Entities of various types can be affected by the Eye Fuel Gas "highness" effect with this trait being shared with other Eye Fuel forms including Dark Matter Eye Fuel that loses its effects.

Entities become easier to deal with as they are less hostile thanks to the enhanced Eye Fuel. Rarer and unseen entities that are eye themed and/or weirdcore may gain human level of sentience while being passive-neutral in nature. These entities along with Facelings tend to celebrate The Harvest of Fuel.

The Harvest of Fuel is not only a holiday celebrated by entities but by regular wanderers as it is considered a peaceful and prosperous time of month. Wanderers and entities alike tend to wear weirdcore-themed costumes and sometimes are seen cosplaying one another. Entities that are known to harvest objects tend to be seen less which may imply that they are busy harvesting resources in a particular area.

A.A.E.G..title: A.A.E.G.
A.A.E.G..description:
The A.A.E.G is a grouping of around 400 - 550 people in size. They are the best protectors of entities in all the Backrooms. They want to understand them and, if they can, communicate with them. They have relatively advanced technology, with some great inventions, such as The FAX, which was invented by one of the 4-S. The FAX is one of the best communication tools throughout the Backrooms and has aided in the A.A.E.G's investigations of entities in multiple levels, allowing direct communication between levels.

The  A.A.E.G has achieved some things on their objectives to understand, comprehend, socialize, and live with entities without any problems. Many advances have produced positive results, such as completely understanding the behaviour of an entity or socializing for a matter of a few minutes.

The A.A.E.G is usually always behind some significant investigations towards entities, thus acquiring Data for The 4 Files. The 4 Files is the name of the homepage of the A.A.E.G that has all the information of the entities investigated. There is also a part of The 4 Files that is completely public. In that section, there are various things like shops where you can buy FAX Prototypes, Emotional Glass, among other things that help with survival. Also within The 4 Files, there is a collection of secret things that are A.A.E.G and not on the visible side to the public and you need to possess A.A.E.G to see it.

The A.A.E.G has both worked for— and continues to work for— the recognition of entities with human-equivalent or higher intelligence, as being entitled to the same rights and access as every human. They appear to be working to provide such entities with equal opportunities and possibilities, not less. The A.A.E.G is completely against groups that destroy or attack entities. One of these groups is A.E.G., a group entirely devoted to destroying and killing entities.

The A.A.E.G has its main base, which is called Base A, on Level 622, a fully established base where they have their database and where most of the people belonging to the A.A.E.G live. Although A.A.E.G is not well known in the Backrooms, they have a very important role in the investigation and understanding of entities.

V.C.U..title: V.C.U.
V.C.U..description: You are viewing top-classified information. Please input your password to verify if your rank is high enough to view this page.
V.C.U..lead: You are viewing top-classified information. Please input your password to verify if your rank is high enough to view this page.

State of Nautica.title: State of Nautica
State of Nautica.description:
The State of Nautica is an underwater group composed of humanoid entities known as Nautics. They appear in multiple sea levels, and they have shown expertise in navigating expansive waters, even having built fairly advanced underwater colonies. Outside of the water, they operate in ships that can be either very small to large in size, even sometimes being cargo ships.

Nautics, in order to look more familiar to other wanderers, usually wear scuba diving suits. While few footage of them without suits exists, it has been confirmed they are bipedal creatures with gills and tentacles that can wrap around their legs when out of water. Nautics sustain themselves via trade with other groups, hunting Splinter Jellyfish, and scavenging. About 4,000+ Nautics live in the State Of Nautica.

The capital of this group, Neptune, lies in Level 7.5, although before The Flicker it used to be in Level 205. They are mainly a trade-based society, acting similarly to a standard democratic nation, and despite having a military, it is relatively small compared to other groups.

Phenomenon 40.title: Phenomenon 40
Phenomenon 40.description:
The man scoffed, he didn't even have the ham for his sandwich. He snickered, poking fun at his own memory.

"Any day now I'll lose my head," the young man jokingly thought.

He continued over to his pantry, until he noticed an unnatural force pulling him down. As he looked downward, it almost looked as if his foot was phasing through the flooring. He screamed, pulling his foot backward - but it only sent him further down into the spiral. It was like a wormhole, he slowly and painfully phased through the floorboards until it was just his head poking out.

The rest of his body felt cold and senseless. He flailed his body about though he still couldn't feel anything. This movement somehow loosened the floorboards' grip on his head, almost fully falling in. With only his eyes poking out, he tried to scream but nothing would come out. Or maybe something did, maybe the sound was just in another dimension.

He eventually fell fully into this wormhole. He had his eyes shut, praying this wouldn't be his end. And his prayers were answered. He felt something; something soft, something damp. He heard something, an ear-piercing buzz that wouldn't seem to stop. He opened his eyes and saw everything. He was in a room saturated in yellow, not a singular object was spared from the sickly hue. The room was incoherently designed and almost looked alien, though there was a strange familiarity to it.

He looked up to be blinded by the origin of that putrid hum. Fluorescent lighting dotted the roof of this strange place this man had found himself in. The man was utterly confused and had a strange feeling he couldn't put his finger on. It was a strange sense of familiarity, déjà vu even. He maniacally laughed, it was just a dream. He'd wake up in his bed right... NOW!

By a stroke of luck, his timing was impeccable. He noticed that strange, tugging feeling in his foot again. As he looked down, he saw that he was being sucked into the floor; just like back home. As everyone would be, he was initially quite surprised, attempting to pull his foot back until he realized what was happening. As he tugged his leg backward, more of his body was enveloped in the carpet.

The body parts sucked into the floor were cold and numb, with no sensations at all. He descended into another reality, another dimension. A dimension he calls home. The Frontrooms. When he came to, he was on the hard floor and drenched in sweat. He got to his feet, and he noticed his sandwich was already made for him with a note beside it.

----

Come to Light (1/2).title: Come to Light (1/2)
Come to Light (1/2).description:
?

?

Nothing stays stagnant forever...

Dreyfus Scale: 4/5

Frequency: 1/5 (Rare)

Phenomenon 20 can manifest anywhere in the Backrooms, albeit incredibly rarely or under specific circumstances.

Hazard: 5/5 (Catastrophic)

Phenomenon 20 can change the makeup of many levels, and potentially take many lives with them.

Mystery: 5/5 (Mysterious)

Phenomenon 20 is one of the least documented yet dangerous phenomena throughout the entirety of the Backrooms.

 Description

Phenomenon 20, also known as “Conflicting Level Alterations”, is an unconfirmed anomalous phenomenon that has potentially affected many levels throughout the Backrooms. This phenomenon is currently unconfirmed and has no backing evidence; its only purpose was to attempt to explain sudden changes in the environment at Level 480 and many similar levels. It states that the phenomenon drastically alters the level's characteristics, transforming it into a polar opposite of its former version. The phenomenon is unpredictable; it is unknown if it can repeat or undergo multiple iterations, as anything related to it has only occurred once, if it even exists. Top scientists are working hard to meticulously research Phenomenon 20 to prove its existence and gather extra information on both the level and phenomenon.

Usually, Phenomenon 20 events are drawn out over a long period of time, and the process of transformation can take approximately three minutes to several decades. Due to their nature, Phenomenon 20 events are hard to observe internally, making Level 480’s 26/11/21 event the only recorded and archived case of a Phenomenon 20 event. From the logs recorded during the 26/11/21 event, we can make a few observations about the process of Phenomenon 20, and events proceed as follows:

*The sky on the affected level suddenly and without warning turns black, despite the time of day. All anomalous properties within the level will cease, and any lights within the level will turn off or become busted.

*All entrances and exits out of the affected level are blocked off, aside from a very risky noclip. The environment within the level becomes unstable, usually starting out as a house or any other object being missing but increasing over time. In addition, medium-to-long-range data transmission becomes impossible for reasons that are currently unknown.

*The environment starts to warp and change, with the members of Base Midwest describing it as “liquid land." The affected level will also become very unstable, with reality falling apart within these channels. This process usually takes approximately two hours to five years to complete before transitioning into the next stage.

*At this point, communication between the affected level and other levels becomes impossible. The instability of the land begins to stop, and the level will slowly become more and more stable and explorable. This process has been known to take several years to complete.

*The affected level will reopen. Usually, affected levels will keep some old exits and entrances but will usually gain new ones due to the new nature of the affected level.

Theories become facts when you look deeper...

🗕 🗗 🗙
----

BackMail Inbox [1]
----

----

Hey Aaron, sorry to bother you on your day off, but I may have discovered something relating to the supposed Level 287 incident.

I stumbled on a journal entry on Level 4 that I believe has come from Level 287. It appears to detail one's escape out of the level during the event. The thing is, the page was labeled as being written on the 12th of December, which was a similar date as to what happened on Level 480 and the entry itself describes numerous similarities to Level 480's event.

So, what I am saying is that Level 287 may have some connection to Level 480 in some way, and that also may indicate that Phenomenon 20 is real in some shape or form. Imma look into it, and I will shoot a message when I have proof.

🗕 🗗 🗙

First of all, I'm going to ask you to show me proof to back up what you are saying. I believe you, however, if we were to submit this to the higher ups, they may think this is made up. If you have kept the note, email it to me so I could send it in as proof. This might be a big stepping stone to truly understanding what this event on Level 287 was.

BackMail Inbox [1]

Sure thing, I backed up the file into a digital photocopier and sent it to my computer. Here it is:

https//:www.spreadsheet1.back

Date 12/3/21

I don't know what happened, the last thing I remember before this whole situation is everyone running around in circles and panicking. I run along with the crowd, before I suddenly fell to the floor and everything went black. My head hurt so fucking bad I would have probably been shocked if there was no concussion. The level I was on was now desolate, with no soul in sight, and was slowly becoming more and more unstable by the moment. Most of the potential exits I found were either locked or not appearing.

I looked out the windows and noticed that instead of the traditional hotel parking lot exterior, it was replaced by a vantablack abyss. All the levels courtyards were the same, with a pitch-black void at the bottom. Every object within the rooms seemed intact, however they appeared to blend in with the level's now blank and desolate nature, and all character and sentimentality those objects one held were gone.

The worst part, by far, is what happened later, before I fortunately escaped. The level appeared to warp and become unstable, with parts appearing to glitch at a fast rate. A threatening atmosphere now proliferated the air, and all I was focused on was running in hopes that I didn't die. After I took a nap next to a courtyard, an urge told me to jump into the abyss. Granted, it was not a very strong urge, but it was enough to freak me out regardless, and I quickly got out of there. Soon enough, I began noticing shadows, more like shadowlike beings, on the level, whether directly in front of me or within a room. They would quickly scurry away or cover in the corner like cowards. Never knew what their problem was, all I know was that they just appeared on the level like some foreign parasite.

Eventually, I just gave up and ran, hoping to find some sort of escape. By sheer luck, the hallways began to transition, and I ended up in the middle of Level 4, miraculously next to the base of the Backrooms Red Cross. Found out I was 90 pounds underweight, go figure. While I was here, I took the time to write down my thoughts before leaving. The nature of the transformation of the level I was on still leaves me pondering, and I will continually ask the question: "What happened here?"

View Article?

 Phenomenon 20
Dreyfus Scale: 4/5

Frequency: 1/5 (Rare)

Phenomenon 20 can manifest anywhere in the Backrooms, albeit incredibly rarely or under specific circumstances.

Hazard: 5/5 (Catastrophic)

Phenomenon 20 can change the makeup of many levels, and potentially take many lives with them.

Mystery: 5/5 (Mysterious)

Phenomenon 20 is one of the least documented yet dangerous phenomena throughout the entirety of the Backrooms.

 Description

Phenomenon 20, also known as “Conflicting Level Alterations”, is an anomalous phenomenon that has potentially affected many levels throughout the Backrooms. The phenomenon itself is unpredictable and can hit at any time. Levels afflicted by Phenomenon 20 will undergo events that cause them to become warped and corrupted, eventually transforming them into polar opposite versions of their former selves. Usually, Phenomenon 20 events are drawn out over a long period of time, and the process of transformation can take approximately three minutes to several decades. Due to their nature, Phenomenon 20 events are hard to observe internally, however with collected logs, addenda, and research, we can make a few observations about the process of Phenomenon 20, and events proceed as follows:

*The sky on the affected level suddenly and without warning turns black, despite the time of day. All anomalous properties within the level will cease, and any lights within the level will turn off or become busted.

*All entrances and exits out of the affected level are blocked off, aside from a very risky noclip. The environment within the level becomes unstable, usually starting out as a house or any other object being missing but increasing over time. In addition, medium-to-long-range data transmission becomes impossible for reasons that are currently unknown.

*The environment starts to warp and change, with the members of Base Midwest describing it as “liquid land." The affected level will also become very unstable, with reality falling apart within these channels. This process usually takes approximately two hours to five years to complete before transitioning into the next stage.

*At this point, communication between the affected level and other levels becomes impossible. The instability of the land begins to stop, and the level will slowly become more and more stable and explorable. This process has been known to take several years to complete.

*The affected level will reopen. Usually, affected levels will keep some old exits and entrances but will usually gain new ones due to the new nature of the affected level.

Levels affected by Phenomenon 20 have a few similar characteristics between each other, which includes oddball properties, a grayscale and bland appearance, etc. Currently, it is unknown what causes Phenomenon 20 to take place, and why only certain levels are targeted, making it one of the most mysterious and destructive phenomena in the Backrooms.

As of now, only two levels have been confirmed to undergo Phenomenon 20 events: Level 480, and Level 287. Level 480 forms the only case of Phenomenon 20 actually being observed, due to the only M.E.G outpost on the level, Base Midwest, remaining until stage 3 of Phenomenon 20 to document the mysterious circumstance. Level 287 was discovered in its current state after Phenomenon 20 plagued the level, however the event has not been observed, with the only documentation coming from journal excerpts, information from level explorations, among others.

  You have one new notification

>>> Log begins

Colin - "Aaron, you're going to want to see this."

Aaron - "What is it?"

Colin - "So, I think I have just found something big relating to both 480 and 287, as well as UL-2.

Aaron - "Level -96? You mean the art gallery thing that vanished?"

Colin - "Yeah, and on the database, it shows that there are entrances that connect the two levels, one being a two-way system, however you know what other level had a bunch of entrances to both levels?"

Aaron - "Which one?"

Colin - "Level -96."

Aaron - "Wait, wasn't -96 discovered around a month before the event on Level 480?"

Colin - "Yeah."

Aaron - "But why is this so important, I mean, the Backrooms is like that a lot."

Colin - "Because wanderers who have been on Level -96 before it vanished have reported paintings that look a lot like the environments of both 480 and 287, in an almost photorealistic manner."

Aaron - "So, Level -96 may have shared a connection with 480 and 287 in some way?"

Colin - "That's right."

Aaron - "What could this all mean?"

Colin - "It means there's something more going on than we realize."

  > Open Author and Licensing Details

 Writers and Contributors

 Author: Snow.

 Contributors: Terraria. (he helped on Level 480 by writing the end of the first paragraph for Phenomenon 20 on the first section, and I just copied it over here, and he also made the Dreyfus scale)

Inspiration: Beyond, part i

Nothing stays stagnant forever...

Dreyfus Scale: 4/5

Frequency: 1/5 (Rare)

Phenomenon 20 can manifest anywhere in the Backrooms, albeit incredibly rarely or under specific circumstances.

Hazard: 5/5 (Catastrophic)

Phenomenon 20 can change the makeup of many levels, and potentially take many lives with them.

Mystery: 5/5 (Mysterious)

Phenomenon 20 is one of the least documented yet dangerous phenomena throughout the entirety of the Backrooms.

 Description

Phenomenon 20, also known as “Conflicting Level Alterations”, is an unconfirmed anomalous phenomenon that has potentially affected many levels throughout the Backrooms. This phenomenon is currently unconfirmed and has no backing evidence; its only purpose was to attempt to explain sudden changes in the environment at Level 480 and many similar levels. It states that the phenomenon drastically alters the level's characteristics, transforming it into a polar opposite of its former version. The phenomenon is unpredictable; it is unknown if it can repeat or undergo multiple iterations, as anything related to it has only occurred once, if it even exists. Top scientists are working hard to meticulously research Phenomenon 20 to prove its existence and gather extra information on both the level and phenomenon.

Usually, Phenomenon 20 events are drawn out over a long period of time, and the process of transformation can take approximately three minutes to several decades. Due to their nature, Phenomenon 20 events are hard to observe internally, making Level 480’s 26/11/21 event the only recorded and archived case of a Phenomenon 20 event. From the logs recorded during the 26/11/21 event, we can make a few observations about the process of Phenomenon 20, and events proceed as follows:

*The sky on the affected level suddenly and without warning turns black, despite the time of day. All anomalous properties within the level will cease, and any lights within the level will turn off or become busted.

*All entrances and exits out of the affected level are blocked off, aside from a very risky noclip. The environment within the level becomes unstable, usually starting out as a house or any other object being missing but increasing over time. In addition, medium-to-long-range data transmission becomes impossible for reasons that are currently unknown.

*The environment starts to warp and change, with the members of Base Midwest describing it as “liquid land." The affected level will also become very unstable, with reality falling apart within these channels. This process usually takes approximately two hours to five years to complete before transitioning into the next stage.

*At this point, communication between the affected level and other levels becomes impossible. The instability of the land begins to stop, and the level will slowly become more and more stable and explorable. This process has been known to take several years to complete.

*The affected level will reopen. Usually, affected levels will keep some old exits and entrances but will usually gain new ones due to the new nature of the affected level.

Theories become facts when you look deeper...

🗕 🗗 🗙

← → ↻ ⌂🔓︎ br://mail.backos

12/03/2024 at 11:03 PM

BackMail Inbox [1]
----

----

Hey Aaron, sorry to bother you on your day off, but I may have discovered something relating to the supposed Level 287 incident.

I stumbled on a journal entry on Level 4 that I believe has come from Level 287. It appears to detail one's escape out of the level during the event. The thing is, the page was labeled as being written on the 12th of December, which was a similar date as to what happened on Level 480 and the entry itself describes numerous similarities to Level 480's event.

So, what I am saying is that Level 287 may have some connection to Level 480 in some way, and that also may indicate that Phenomenon 20 is real in some shape or form. Imma look into it, and I will shoot a message when I have proof.

🗕 🗗 🗙
----
← → ↻ ⌂🔓︎ br://mail.backos

12/03/2024 at 11:46 PM

BackMail Inbox [1]
----

----

Hey Colin, got your email.

First of all, I'm going to ask you to show me proof to back up what you are saying. I believe you, however, if we were to submit this to the higher ups, they may think this is made up. If you have kept the note, email it to me so I could send it in as proof. This might be a big stepping stone to truly understanding what this event on Level 287 was.

🗕 🗗 🗙
----
← → ↻ ⌂🔓︎ br://mail.backos

12/04/2024 at 12:39 AM

BackMail Inbox [1]
----

----

Sure thing, I backed up the file into a digital photocopier and sent it to my computer. Here it is:

https//:www.spreadsheet1.back

Date 12/3/21

I don't know what happened, the last thing I remember before this whole situation is everyone running around in circles and panicking. I run along with the crowd, before I suddenly fell to the floor and everything went black. My head hurt so fucking bad I would have probably been shocked if there was no concussion. The level I was on was now desolate, with no soul in sight, and was slowly becoming more and more unstable by the moment. Most of the potential exits I found were either locked or not appearing.

I looked out the windows and noticed that instead of the traditional hotel parking lot exterior, it was replaced by a vantablack abyss. All the levels courtyards were the same, with a pitch-black void at the bottom. Every object within the rooms seemed intact, however they appeared to blend in with the level's now blank and desolate nature, and all character and sentimentality those objects one held were gone.

The worst part, by far, is what happened later, before I fortunately escaped. The level appeared to warp and become unstable, with parts appearing to glitch at a fast rate. A threatening atmosphere now proliferated the air, and all I was focused on was running in hopes that I didn't die. After I took a nap next to a courtyard, an urge told me to jump into the abyss. Granted, it was not a very strong urge, but it was enough to freak me out regardless, and I quickly got out of there. Soon enough, I began noticing shadows, more like shadowlike beings, on the level, whether directly in front of me or within a room. They would quickly scurry away or cover in the corner like cowards. Never knew what their problem was, all I know was that they just appeared on the level like some foreign parasite.

Eventually, I just gave up and ran, hoping to find some sort of escape. By sheer luck, the hallways began to transition, and I ended up in the middle of Level 4, miraculously next to the base of the Backrooms Red Cross. Found out I was 90 pounds underweight, go figure. While I was here, I took the time to write down my thoughts before leaving. The nature of the transformation of the level I was on still leaves me pondering, and I will continually ask the question: "What happened here?"

Phenomenon 36.title: Phenomenon 36
Phenomenon 36.displayTitle: The labels the world give us pale in comparison to our own
Phenomenon 36.description:
Phenomenon 36: Tattoos

Figure 1 - Dana Rivers

Dana aquired this tattoo on the 31st of December, 2022, reportedly she had acquired the tattoo through the massive parties staged by The Backrooms Colonists during the turn of the year, one that she found great joy in. As she's stated, the staff-like design represents the fireworks going up into the skin, then bursting. According to her, she plans to take more permanent efforts to embed it into her skin, planning to leave it as a permanent tattoo to signify her time in the Backrooms.

Phenomenon 36, also known as "Tattoos" are a collective, sudden, change of melanin produced on a wanderer's body. These are largely attributed to the passing of DNA between wanderers, especially after prolonged exposure to one another and often indicative of great emotional discourse or display. Beyond these changes, there appears to be no difference from a wanderer with the Tattoos genome and one without it, unless it has already produced tattoos. There is no known "Patient 0" of Phenomenon 36, with many sure it was always a feature within the Backrooms.

Tattoos appear no different to the standard type of those found within the Frontrooms, most of which created in an entirely black "ink" that shares a perfect appearance to generic brand tattoo ink. Additionally, Phenomenon 36 behaves similarly to tattoos in other ways too, with fading of the marking taking a period of roughly 7 years, though can be removed earlier through laser treatment. However, due to the lack of such programs in the Backrooms, many are left to wait for 7 year period for them to be removed naturally through cell replacement.

What does the unfathomable feel like?

The sensation of something you have yet to understand. A memory washing over your skin, like small pins poking holes into our fallible bodies. It's the prickles of the sun casting it's light over skin, or at least the memories of it. It's the chills as you recall your life being saved, dancing on the brink of death but returning back alive.

It's the movies of memories, playing back as the scene is rehearsed again.

And again.

And again.

Figure 2 - August Lee

Due to the aftermath of his experience on Hallowed Nest, August found a tattoo on his arm resembling a starfish. Initially assuming it to be one out of spite, August spoke on that he really wanted to remove it for a long period, due to it bringing up bad memories. As of currently, the tattoo has faded away completely, and when asked, his opinion on it has shifted largely neutral, instead of negative.

As a major event happens to a wanderer, the brain is recorded to transmit signals across to body to respond accordingly. If the wanderer is affected by the strand, signals will be sent to certain parts of the skin as well, encouraging the rapid use and development of melanin and from there, Tattoos. The explanation for why the body does this is unknown, however there are some consistencies. Larger and more emotional events will receive larger Tattoos, and no matter where they are placed, it'll be removed after 7 years of it first developing. Additionally, all Tattoos depict a loose interpretation of the events, and wanderers with them will feel compelled to provide such explanation for them if asked about it.

While the spreading of Tattoos is well documented, the goals of it remain unknown. There appears to be no benefit to the DNA if it is passed on to others, nor is it alive, meaning a new host is not providing any sort of sustenance for it. Others have assumed it to be a made-up phenomenon entirely, as some point to humanity's urge to misconstrue details and events in order to service them. Because of this, wanderers will adorn tattoos from an unknown source to help them stand out or seem more interesting and sympathetic to those around them. There have been no Backrooms-based tattoo artists that have taken ownership of these. With so few of them as well, the widespread nature of this phenomenon makes the theory difficult to prove.

The body changes all the time, it's how vitality is kept and cancer is fought.

Tumors appear, then fade away again. pushed back by the machine that is man. The resetting of cells keeps us safe and strong.

Despite how strong it was, that's the worst it will be. For every day the body chips away, replacing what is old with what is new. There will always be change. So why don't we embrace that change? Every day 330 billion cells die. 1 percent of the flesh is gone after 24 hours.

There's always time for a change. A step forward. The cells that run all mankind only stop when the body dies.

We truly are infinite to the mindless pieces of our flesh.

Figure 3 - James Patterson

Acquired in the aftermath of his time in Level 830, James has spent a while recounting his initial fear and displeasure with the tattoo, especially regarding seeing the repetitive death of his father, Leonardo Patterson. When he was asked about what helped him learn to love the tattoo, he often attributed it to the varying compliments and support he got from it, especially as he'd go on to talk about the impact it had on him more extensively. While not thankful, the appearance of it was a step in the direction of healing, as he claimed.

Tattoos are a sign of change in ones life. In esoteric forms they detail what a wanderer has seen and survived. The good, the bad, and everything between and beyond those extremes. It is an artifact of the skin and mind to regale these happenings to many wanderers with Tattoos. However, it's been shown that through their time, stuck with this marking, many have grown more at peace with those events. Some have grown to not miss the times that Tattoos shows, others learned to accept the change upon their life Tattoos remind them of.

But of course, after 7 years, all the cells in the wanderer's body has been replaced, and Tattoos will fade alongside it. This presents the wanderer with a sort of symbol. As after the Tattoo fades, the gene will die, and those afflicted may never see it again. The responses of those after its disappearance have been oddly reminiscent, as some have spoke on it helping them learn from their life and strive to grow. And for some, it was more akin to a scar fading away, leaving them with something untouched and new.

Phenomenon 9.title: Phenomenon 9
Phenomenon 9.description:
In Tibetan Buddhism and later traditions of mysticism and the paranormal, a tulpa (plural: tulpae) is a materialized being or thought-form, created through spiritual practice and intense concentration. In baseline reality, they are little more than a theological concept; however, in the Backrooms, the concept of tulpae hold much more meaning.

Named after the titular tulpa, Tulpaefication (Phenomenon 9) is an anomalous occurrence localized within the Backrooms, wherein collective thought and/or belief will result in the creation/modification of various constructs, often abnormal in nature. Although the anomaly cannot be properly controlled due to the circumstances involved, many have sought to use this phenomenon for their own benefit.
Phenomenon 9.lead:
NOTICE:

THIS PAGE HAS BEEN DECLASSIFIED.

In accordance with the policies of The Kauer Research Organization, we have released the data within this file to the general public. Happy reading!

Republic of Level 153.title: Republic of Level 153
Republic of Level 153.description:
The Republic of Level 153 is a group based in Level 153. The group has a population of around 10,000, the majority of which reside in Level 153, although they have created outposts in other levels. The group declares itself an independent state, having its own system of government, education system, and bureaucracy. While not having much influence outside its titular level, it is the dominant force within Level 153, and exerts limited authority over other outposts within the level. The Republic is also occasionally called the The Republic of the Antique Workspace, after the level's name chosen before the current level numbering system, leading to citizens of the group to be nicknamed Workspacers.

Like its allies the Backrooms Colonists, the Republic of Level 153 has individual freedom as one of its core values. This allows the average resident of the group to choose their own role in the group's everyday functioning rather than having it be assigned by the government. This mainly applies to those in the group's main bases within Level 153, as in their smaller outposts more direct control is needed to ensure their survival.

The Republic is typically neutral to individual wanderers, welcoming only those they deem trustworthy into the group, although there is some xenophobic sentiment among a minority of their members. After spending at least 5 years within the group, one can request to gain citizenship. Citizenship can be revoked from those who join or assist rival groups or commit offenses against the Republic. The children of citizens of the group also gain citizenship. All citizens over the age of 21 are considered legal adults and can be appointed to a government position or run for president.

Phenomenon 11.title: Phenomenon 11
Phenomenon 11.description:
Due to the extremely high frequency, varying properties and forms, and often inconsistent dangers of Instability, a proper classification is impossible to give to this phenomenon. Be prepared for anything in your travels throughout the Backrooms.

Throughout the confines of the Backrooms, Phenomenon 11 (Instability) is perhaps one of the most common phenomena one will encounter. Manifesting as a general lack of coherent physical laws, Instability runs rampant in the Backrooms throughout its many levels, changing the landscape of this dimension for better or worse.

Although this anomaly's function is rather simple when one analyzes its barest components, it has wide reaching consequences; due to its existence, all those who wish to study or traverse the Backrooms are reminded of one thing: nothing is ever truly consistent within this dimension.
Phenomenon 11.lead:
Due to the extremely high frequency, varying properties and forms, and often inconsistent dangers of Instability, a proper classification is impossible to give to this phenomenon. Be prepared for anything in your travels throughout the Backrooms.

Throughout the confines of the Backrooms, Phenomenon 11 (Instability) is perhaps one of the most common phenomena one will encounter. Manifesting as a general lack of coherent physical laws, Instability runs rampant in the Backrooms throughout its many levels, changing the landscape of this dimension for better or worse.

Although this anomaly's function is rather simple when one analyzes its barest components, it has wide reaching consequences; due to its existence, all those who wish to study or traverse the Backrooms are reminded of one thing: nothing is ever truly consistent within this dimension.

Extended BACK-NET Website List.title: Extended BACK-NET Website List
Extended BACK-NET Website List.description:
Meta Notice: This is a collaborative article! Everyone is allowed (and encouraged) to add their own additions to the article!

Keep in mind that the list is formatted in alphabetical order.

BACK-NET contains a variety of websites, both human-made and naturally occurring. Due to the massive amount of websites not allowing them all to be listed on BACK-NET's main page, a separate log was deemed necessary for cataloging them. The following is a collection of both popular and notable websites and those with interesting or anomalous characteristics. Wanderers are encouraged to add information about websites they have knowledge of to this chart. A website's domain is formatted as backnet-[level number of the server host]://[domain name].[top-level domain].

Almond Live
Domain: backnet0://almondlive.com

Source: Unknown

Almond Live is a website of unknown source seemingly featuring live footage of a bottle of Almond Water placed on a table in an unknown level. The footage is for the most part unchanging, although events such as the lights of the level flickering has shown it to be a video rather than a still image. It is rumored that Almond Water Inc. created the website as some form of bizarre advertisement.

Aurie Attack!
Domain: backnet1://aurieattack.io

Source: Unknown

Aurie Attack! is a propaganda website created by the M.E.G. in order to simulate an invasion instigated by the Republic of Aurielle. It was originally made for the sake of military training and preparation but was publicized in order to spread anti-Auriellen beliefs and improve the image of the M.E.G. The website has been promptly banned in the colonies of Aurielle, although they have made an off-brand version named Meggie Massacres  that twists the storyline into a M.E.G. attack, taking much more brutal and grotesque imagery. This game has caused a great divide between hitherto neutral wanderers and has created further hatred between the two groups.

Frontnews
Domain: backnet0://frontnews.back.net

Source: Unknown

Frontnews is a website that show news from the Frontrooms. Its creator is unknown, but it's possible that this website was created to manipulate humans.

Backchat
Domain: backnet4://back.chat

Source: Collaborative

Formerly the Public Interdimensional Forum, and commonly nicknamed ∞chan (pronounced "infchan"), Backchat stands as the most popular social network in the entire Backrooms, similar in design and functionality to sites like 4chan. Around 95 million registered accounts exist on it (although most have not seen use for years), and it remains an easy way for one to reach out to other people and exchange survival tips in the otherwise isolated landscape of this dimension.

Back-Graph
Domain: backnet11://backgraph.net

Source: Collaborative

Back-Graph is an online social network, where wanderers can publish photographs of levels, entities, phenomena or objects they find whilst exploring, without any moderation or restriction. Users can also create dedicated topics, which are typically used to identify unrecognized objects found around the Backrooms. Despite being one of the most popular social networks in the Backrooms, Back-Graph has faced controversy for users who publish strange photographs and go without punishment.

Backroom Crawlers
Domain: backnet7.5://backroomcrawlers.com

Source: Collaborative

The recently made “Backrooms Crawlers” is a website designed and programmed by a group of friends. The purpose of the website is to discover and document new levels without the micromanagement of being under a large group. Despite the somewhat anarchistic foundation the website is intended for, it has managed to gain a small community of a thousand users within its recent creation. The community is welcoming to new members and is usually happy to help newer wanderers get the equipment they need to provide to the platform.

Backrooms Today Online
Domain: backnet399://backroomstoday.org

Source: Backrooms, Today

Backrooms Today Online is an online news site created by Backrooms Today in 2003. The website features digital versions of Backrooms Today's print publications, as well as extra features such as videos and interactive charts. More recently, a forum has been included on the site for users to discuss news and current events, although it has been criticized as various groups have used the form to spread propaganda and disinformation.

Backvideo
Domain: backnet154://backvideo.net

Source: Collaborative

Backvideo is a video sharing site similar to websites such as YouTube where wanderers can upload short video clips. Due to the hardware of BACK-NET terminals, these videos are typically low-resolution and take some time to load. The site has 10 million registered accounts, making it one of the largest in The Backrooms.

Clans.io
Domain: backnet29://clans.io

Source: Collaborative

Clans.io, sometimes simply referred to as “Group Watch” is a collaborative, comprehensive list of known, documented groups. The site classifies communities by hostility, size and population, as well as overall stability. Who founded this database is unknown, though it is has became an informative site with wanderers being able to submit information. It is advised to take this site granted; as information can become quickly outdated or otherwise unreliable. Some controversies have emerged over time, with reports of bias regarding the sites managing body.

The Database
Domain: backnet0://thearchives.org

Source: Collaborative

“The Database,” also referred to as “The Archives” within its source code, is search engine of anomalous origin hosted on BACK-NET, seemingly designed to compile documentation regarding the Backrooms. Although it is a useful tool, one would be advised against taking it for granted; many webpages stored by the Database contain bias, and misinformation runs rampant without any moderation.

Shroomsite
Domain: backnet154://shroomsite.net

Source: Collaborative

Shroomsite (although often known by its users as "DS": "Doin' Shrooms") is a collaborative forum where users share their personal innovations or "life-hacks" using Backshrooms. The site has garnered a significant community of individuals addicted to Backshroom-derived substances, particularly involving Insanity Shrooms, Kharthic Shrooms, and Marigold Shroom mixtures. Much of the main feed consists of such substances in combination; however, there exists a category filter to allow wanderers to find helpful information regarding survival.

The_4_Files
Domain: backnet1123://AustinFiles.ag

Source: Reports from the A.A.E.G.

"The_4_Files" is the official page of the A.A.E.G. where they publish information about their explorations and investigations of levels, phenomena, and entities. All—or most—of the information is public. Additionally, within the page, there is an online store belonging to the A.A.E.G. where you can buy various objects made by the A.A.E.G.

Entity Commentalog
Domain: backnet11.89://encom.jp

Source: Collaborative, mainly by Entity Commentary tulpents

Entity Commentalog is a blog website with anyone can comment. A group of Smilers who inserted false information to Entity Watch also commented to this blog, through it was replied with "Don't think they wants individual informations of y'all"(sic).

Entity Watch
Domain: backnet4.3://entitywatch.org

Source: Collaborative, managed by the Anti-Entity Group

Entity Watch is a website featuring information about different entities and the means to survive and fight them. Originally created by the A.E.G, the website has since become a collaborative effort, with individual wanderers being able to submit information to the site.

Free Backrooms Forum
Domain: backnet1://freebackroomsforum.chat

Source: Collaborative

The Free Backrooms Forum, often abbreviated to FBF is an online forum where members discuss various conspiracy theories. Theories discussed on the forum range from accusations of political corruption among major groups to more esoteric topics such as the nature of the Backrooms as some form of simulation. While many of the more outlandish claims made on the site are nonsense, some, such as that of The First being a designation for multiple individuals instead of a single person have been proven true.

The Frontrooms Game
Domain: backnet477://frontroomsgame.io

Source: Unknown

The Frontrooms Game, as its name implies, is an online game based on a depiction of The Frontrooms. In the game, one navigates poorly rendered, procedurally generated versions of various locations in The Frontrooms, such as cities, forests, and suburbs. The game has no aim or story and features no depictions of humans or animals. Despite the website stating the purpose of the game is to "give users feelings of home", players have described the atmosphere of the game to be more akin to the liminal spaces of The Backrooms.

LimAI
Domain: backnet27://liminal.ai

Source: Natural

Liminal.ai, more commonly known as Liminal AI or simply LimAI is an AI chatbot seemingly naturally generated by BACK-NET, being one of the most complex naturally generated sites. However, rather than being useful, the chatbot found on LimAI responds to prompts with seemingly unrelated non-sequiturs or random strings of text in various languages. When asked about its nature or other details about the AI, the site will display a series of flashing colors before kicking the user off the site and blocking the terminal from accessing it.

PathFinder
Domain: backnet1://pathfinder.org

Source: Major Explorer Group

PathFinder is a simple website acting as an extension to the Database. It allows one to view how to exit the level they are on, and even the locations of exists, although the rapidly shifting nature of levels and poor coverage in unsafe or unstable levels makes the usefulness of this feature dubious. Recently, PathFinder introduced a feature listing the easiest path between two levels, the finding of which had previously been a common game or challenge among Backchat Users.

Virus Video
Domain: backnet151://backvideo.com

Source: Mutated smilers.

Virus video is a fake version of Backvideo, created by Mutated Smilers in Level 151. Humans are unable to upload to Virus Virus, which pretends to be Backvideo. Contained on Virus Video are fake "entity survival" guides, mostly about smilers. The content gives detrimental tips that are the inverse of what you should do, as well as telling you to go to a level with smilers inside. It was created by said mutated smiles to get more people. Virus Video can be told apart via the number being wrong and it ending in .com rather than .net. Going to this website will also make the terminal explode after 10 minutes.

Y7FF73UHE
Domain: backnet7://Y7FF73UHE.com

Source: Collaborative

Y7FF73UHE, or simply referred to as Y7F, is a collaborative video sharing platform. Often classified as a shock website, Y7F freely hosts real footage of extreme violence, gore, war crimes, and any sort of similar thing. It is unknown who hosts the website, though theories have emerged it is a honeypot used by the Backrooms Investigation Foundation.

Backblog
Domain: backnet20://backblog.com

Source: Votrumuve & Monteith

Backblog, often referred to as "Backlog" is a blogging website hosted by two wanderers: Ethan Votrumuve and Erron Monteith. Backblog is a website for wanderers to share their experiences with the backrooms as well as document their findings.  During the The Flicker this website was used by The Major Explorer group to contact other groups as well as colonies.

𝛌elp
Domain: backnet?://𝛌elp.com

Source: Collaborative, mostly H.K.

𝛌elp, also known as 𝛌elp.com, is a review-based website imitating that of Yelp. The core difference is that 𝛌elp is dedicated entirely to reviewing Backrooms levels. This is also the main outlet for reviews by H.K., and as such should typically be avoided. For unknown reasons, this particular website tends to consistently have a stronger internet connection than others, meaning that it may be used as a last resort to glean information.
