Q is for Quarantined.title: Q is for Quarantined
Q is for Quarantined.description:
Kaikibuyo
Description
With only 1 known case of the phenomenon, information about the illness is extremely limited. What is known is that Kaikibuyo, also known as Steel Heart Disease, comes from an extreme strain of Tetanus, which caused flesh to be broken down and turned into a metallic compound. This bodily response changes the whole body to become more akin to automatons, but due to the tough properties of the metal, the extent of which remains unknown. Over the course of roughly 3 months, these changes take place, eventually turning the victim into a rusted android as well; presumably to infect others by means of physical touch.

Patient 0 is a man named Vinny Kramer, and remains under supervision by the Entity Research Committee at Rental Place. Vinny is deemed safe enough to interact with others considered too hazardous to allow others to interact with him. Anybody found entering Patient 0's chamber must be terminated and their body burned to prevent possible contamination. Entrance into its cell can only be approved of by management staff, and all entering must wear a high-duty filtration mask and cover up all skin with approved protective gear.

Kaikibuyo Log 01

Foreword: By the recording of this log, Patient 0 was still considered safe enough to visit without protective gear. This interview is between Vinny Kramer and Adrian Levine. It is believed that Vinny is in the first stage of Kaikibuyo. This stage only consists of the mechanization of human flesh, seemingly not affecting internal organs of other biological systems. In the case of this recording, Patient 0 has only had Steel Heart Disease for 2 weeks.

Date: February 12th, 200X, 22:42

"Hey.. thanks for asking them, dude."

"Of course, how are you doing?"

"I feel mostly fine, just some irritation on my hand."

Vinny shows his left hand to Adrian, showing the metallic skin now forming over where there was once a cut. Adrian winces.

"Shit that looks bad."

Adrian takes Vinny's hand and moves it closer to his face. There is some non-verbal response from Vinny, but neither party is willing to disclose it.

"Yea, yea. It'll be fine though. They'll get it fixed up, yea?"

"They.. didn't give me a full answer. I'm pretty sure they're gonna look into creating a cure though. I was told that much"

Sigh "I'll take it, I guess."

"Mhm. I'll fight like hell for you. Even if it means I have to get infected."

"No-no, don't gotta go that far. It'd be stupid."

"Cause then neither of us will be able to get help, and we'll both be like this."

"I'd rather suffer with you then not help at all."

Adrian appears to kiss a non-metallic part of Vinny's hand, and lets go. Vinny pulls his hand back and rubs "the kiss" on his face slowly. As it happens, Adrian stretches his arms upward.

"You're so sweet... and so stupid."

"hahaha, thanks."

Vinny is seen moving closer to Adrian, now pressing against his side.

"And hot―but that's for a different day."

Adrian covers his face, the sound of his laughter does not pick up, though he has confirmed so.

"I mean you look better, I don't know why, but you do. Eating better or something?"

"Yea actually, been trying to take in more protein and workout more."

"To the best of my ability at least."

"God it's doing you so many favors..."

"Do you want to see the difference?"

"Oh? Yes please."

Section cut for patient privacy.

"So, how long do you got before you get kicked out of my cell?"

"They didn't give me a limit."

"Fuck yea."

"Would you mind if I kept you here?"

"No, not at all. I'd be happy to."

Log End: February 12th, 200X, 23:17

Kaikibuyo Log 02

Foreword: Due to concerns of infection as a result of Incident █████████, Patient 0 lost the ability to interact with those not in proper coverage. The ruling also came with permanent separation of Adrian Levine from  cell. This document outlines some of the behavior Vinny Kramer engages as it reaches the second stage of Steel Heart Disease.

Date: May 22nd, 200X, 7:18

[7:20]
Patient 0 wakes up from a knocking on the door, with staff member Ezekiel Thorn then opening the door. He places down a tray of food, and Patient 0 is seen covering themselves up further with the provided blanket. No communication is attempted, with Ezekiel leaving shortly after.

[7:38]
Another 20 minutes pass before Patient 0 climbs out of its bedding. The jaw structure which now has replaced its mouth opens as it moves closer to the now cold meal. All movements seen are physically jerky and robotic, and appear to bring it much discomfort. Despite this, it begins to eat.

[7:42]
Stage 2 of Steel Heart Disease seems to begin, as Patient 0 appears to get extremely sick from eating common human foods. A staff member is then brought in to give Patient 0 more mechanical foodstuffs. Though still sick, it eats those instead. No negative effects are observed from this.

[8:53]
Patient 0 stops pacing around its cell, standing perfectly still for multiple hours.

[12:04]
It starts to move again, the movements appear more fluid than previously. X-Rays taken later showed all its muscles now replaced with robotic ones, which caused it to stagnate for a long period of time.

[12:08]
The old trays are removed, with another one replacing it. The tray has both mechanical parts and normal food. Seemingly unhappily, Patient 0 eats the machine parts. It looks towards the camera for a moment, before grabbing some paper provided several months earlier, and started to scribble on it.

[12:10]
After 1 unsuccessful attempt, it writes out a sentence. Patient 0 then takes the paper and moves it to the camera, holding it closely. On it, inscribed in permanent marker, is the phrase "I still love you." The paper is believed to be for Adrian.

Log End: May 22nd, 200X, 12:11

Kaikibuyo
Stages of Steel Heart Disease
Stage 1 immediately starts when a wanderer interacts with an object or individual with Steel Heart Disease. During this stage, all flesh is turned into a metallic compound. This metal, known as KB-1, has similar durability to iron compounds, though is decently more malleable as well. Other bodily affects in stage 1 come as a direct result of the change in skin, with muscles often growing denser and body weight increasing to account for the heavier exterior. All organs and internal functions remain the exact same.

Stage 2 starts days after the first stage is completed. In this stage, internal organs and all other natural matter in the body will become mechanized as well. These shifts are much faster, though stagnation is still common. Stagnation is the locking up of body parts for upwards of 2 hours, often to replace them with metallic parts. In this stage, normal food becomes toxic and can damage the infected. This forces the subject to shift to a mechanical diet, and the use of batteries or other electric sources as the main source of energy.

Stages beyond this remain unknown, as the original patient later escaped ERC housing. Any information on its whereabouts should be reported as soon as possible. Additionally, further victims of Steel Heart Disease now are unconfirmed, with many assuming it to be a rising population.

Kaikibuyo Log 03

Foreword: This log is between Adrian Levine and Hydrolitis' specialist Olivia Patterson.

Date: March 30th, 200X, 13:01

"Do you have any progress on a cure?"

"Well, no, but the research we've been able to obtain from Vinny could be essential to solving other Backrooms viruses."

"What's that supposed to mean?"

"Your friend has a disease that seems nonfatal, which is extremely rare around here. I'm sorry to say, but it's better for us all if he stays afflicted."

"So... you're not going to cure him?"

"Not yet, at least."

"So, I'm fucked, yea? I came here thinking you all could help him."

"We will help him, just, not yet. There's more people who could use this research to survive."

"I promised I'd help him. I promised I'd be there for him."

"I can't even tell him this, I can't fucking enter his cell."

"You still can appeal it, right? Didn't the ruling happen 3 weeks ago?"

"It was denied. So now he thinks I abandoned him and I can't tell him otherwise."

Log End: March 30th, 200X, 13:05

Kaikibuyo
Infection and Theories
The source of Steel Heart Disease remains unknown, as Patient 0 never confirmed where it came from. However passing from humans to humans can cause by long-term exposal to Steel Heart Disease. The minimum time is unknown, largely because of the aforementioned loss of Patient 0. For the same reason, no known cure exists.

Below is a list of theories as to the origin of Steel Heart Disease.

Short Circuited Theory

Originating from the phenomenon found in Level 3, Short Circuited Theory suggests the cause of Steel Heart Disease to be caused by anomalous electricity. The theory points to other electrical phenomenon such as The Flicker and the abnormal quality of power sources found in levels like Level 754 to support this theory.

Sentinel Theory

Sentinel Theory suggests that machine-human entities such as Sentinels and mechanically familiar entities like Crunches being responsible for the spread of Steel Heart Disease. Namely, the lack of reproductive systems for Sentinels lead some to believe they operate on a virus-like system to sustain their population.

Kaikibuyo Log 04

Foreword: Between May 22nd, and June 13th, Patient 0 spent the time writing various sentences and laying them on the ground. As of June 16th, no signs of writing were made. This log is a collection of all those notes.

Date: June 16th, 200X

I MISS YOU, ADRIAN

ITS BEEN 120 DAYS

I COUNTED EACH ONE

HOPING THAT IT WOULD CHANGE

IT NEVER DID

IVE BEEN ALONE FOR SO LONG

MY BODY HAS LOST BEAUTY

I AM NO LONGER VINNY

IM HIS REPLACEMENT

YOU KNOW THAT, DONT YOU?

IS THAT WHY YOU DONT TALK

IS THAT WHY NOBODY TALKS

IM LEFT IN THE DARK

BOTH OF MIND AND BODY

EVERYTHING FEELS WRONG

IM SCARED

BUT NONE ONE COMFORTS ME

YOULL BE HERE WHEN IM CURED

RIGHT?

WILL I BE CURED?

ALL THEY DO IS WATCH ME

IM PROMISED THE WORLD

IS THIS THE PROMISED WORLD?

JUST A DAMN SLICE OF HELL?

PLEASE. COME BACK ADRIAN

I LOVED YOU

I STILL LOVE YOU

Log End

Credits
Author: PortulTrademark

Background: Distressed Walling ‘Palladio’ Collection by Henry van der Vijver
ERC Hazmat: Wasteland Rad Mage by u/luna_delcielo
Q is for Quarantined.lead:
Kaikibuyo
Description
With only 1 known case of the phenomenon, information about the illness is extremely limited. What is known is that Kaikibuyo, also known as Steel Heart Disease, comes from an extreme strain of Tetanus, which caused flesh to be broken down and turned into a metallic compound. This bodily response changes the whole body to become more akin to automatons, but due to the tough properties of the metal, the extent of which remains unknown. Over the course of roughly 3 months, these changes take place, eventually turning the victim into a rusted android as well; presumably to infect others by means of physical touch.

Patient 0 is a man named Vinny Kramer, and remains under supervision by the Entity Research Committee at Rental Place. Vinny is deemed safe enough to interact with others considered too hazardous to allow others to interact with him. Anybody found entering Patient 0's chamber must be terminated and their body burned to prevent possible contamination. Entrance into its cell can only be approved of by management staff, and all entering must wear a high-duty filtration mask and cover up all skin with approved protective gear.

Kaikibuyo Log 01

Foreword: By the recording of this log, Patient 0 was still considered safe enough to visit without protective gear. This interview is between Vinny Kramer and Adrian Levine. It is believed that Vinny is in the first stage of Kaikibuyo. This stage only consists of the mechanization of human flesh, seemingly not affecting internal organs of other biological systems. In the case of this recording, Patient 0 has only had Steel Heart Disease for 2 weeks.

Date: February 12th, 200X, 22:42

"Hey.. thanks for asking them, dude."

"Of course, how are you doing?"

"I feel mostly fine, just some irritation on my hand."

Vinny shows his left hand to Adrian, showing the metallic skin now forming over where there was once a cut. Adrian winces.

"Shit that looks bad."

Adrian takes Vinny's hand and moves it closer to his face. There is some non-verbal response from Vinny, but neither party is willing to disclose it.

"Yea, yea. It'll be fine though. They'll get it fixed up, yea?"

"They.. didn't give me a full answer. I'm pretty sure they're gonna look into creating a cure though. I was told that much"

Sigh "I'll take it, I guess."

"Mhm. I'll fight like hell for you. Even if it means I have to get infected."

"No-no, don't gotta go that far. It'd be stupid."

"Cause then neither of us will be able to get help, and we'll both be like this."

"I'd rather suffer with you then not help at all."

Adrian appears to kiss a non-metallic part of Vinny's hand, and lets go. Vinny pulls his hand back and rubs "the kiss" on his face slowly. As it happens, Adrian stretches his arms upward.

"You're so sweet... and so stupid."

"hahaha, thanks."

Vinny is seen moving closer to Adrian, now pressing against his side.

"And hot―but that's for a different day."

Adrian covers his face, the sound of his laughter does not pick up, though he has confirmed so.

"I mean you look better, I don't know why, but you do. Eating better or something?"

"Yea actually, been trying to take in more protein and workout more."

"To the best of my ability at least."

"God it's doing you so many favors..."

"Do you want to see the difference?"

"Oh? Yes please."

Section cut for patient privacy.

"So, how long do you got before you get kicked out of my cell?"

"They didn't give me a limit."

"Fuck yea."

"Would you mind if I kept you here?"

"No, not at all. I'd be happy to."

Log End: February 12th, 200X, 23:17

Kaikibuyo Log 02

Foreword: Due to concerns of infection as a result of Incident █████████, Patient 0 lost the ability to interact with those not in proper coverage. The ruling also came with permanent separation of Adrian Levine from  cell. This document outlines some of the behavior Vinny Kramer engages as it reaches the second stage of Steel Heart Disease.

Date: May 22nd, 200X, 7:18

[7:20]
Patient 0 wakes up from a knocking on the door, with staff member Ezekiel Thorn then opening the door. He places down a tray of food, and Patient 0 is seen covering themselves up further with the provided blanket. No communication is attempted, with Ezekiel leaving shortly after.

[7:38]
Another 20 minutes pass before Patient 0 climbs out of its bedding. The jaw structure which now has replaced its mouth opens as it moves closer to the now cold meal. All movements seen are physically jerky and robotic, and appear to bring it much discomfort. Despite this, it begins to eat.

[7:42]
Stage 2 of Steel Heart Disease seems to begin, as Patient 0 appears to get extremely sick from eating common human foods. A staff member is then brought in to give Patient 0 more mechanical foodstuffs. Though still sick, it eats those instead. No negative effects are observed from this.

[8:53]
Patient 0 stops pacing around its cell, standing perfectly still for multiple hours.

[12:04]
It starts to move again, the movements appear more fluid than previously. X-Rays taken later showed all its muscles now replaced with robotic ones, which caused it to stagnate for a long period of time.

[12:08]
The old trays are removed, with another one replacing it. The tray has both mechanical parts and normal food. Seemingly unhappily, Patient 0 eats the machine parts. It looks towards the camera for a moment, before grabbing some paper provided several months earlier, and started to scribble on it.

[12:10]
After 1 unsuccessful attempt, it writes out a sentence. Patient 0 then takes the paper and moves it to the camera, holding it closely. On it, inscribed in permanent marker, is the phrase "I still love you." The paper is believed to be for Adrian.

Log End: May 22nd, 200X, 12:11

Kaikibuyo
Stages of Steel Heart Disease
Stage 1 immediately starts when a wanderer interacts with an object or individual with Steel Heart Disease. During this stage, all flesh is turned into a metallic compound. This metal, known as KB-1, has similar durability to iron compounds, though is decently more malleable as well. Other bodily affects in stage 1 come as a direct result of the change in skin, with muscles often growing denser and body weight increasing to account for the heavier exterior. All organs and internal functions remain the exact same.

Stage 2 starts days after the first stage is completed. In this stage, internal organs and all other natural matter in the body will become mechanized as well. These shifts are much faster, though stagnation is still common. Stagnation is the locking up of body parts for upwards of 2 hours, often to replace them with metallic parts. In this stage, normal food becomes toxic and can damage the infected. This forces the subject to shift to a mechanical diet, and the use of batteries or other electric sources as the main source of energy.

Stages beyond this remain unknown, as the original patient later escaped ERC housing. Any information on its whereabouts should be reported as soon as possible. Additionally, further victims of Steel Heart Disease now are unconfirmed, with many assuming it to be a rising population.

Kaikibuyo Log 03

Foreword: This log is between Adrian Levine and Hydrolitis' specialist Olivia Patterson.

Date: March 30th, 200X, 13:01

"Do you have any progress on a cure?"

"Well, no, but the research we've been able to obtain from Vinny could be essential to solving other Backrooms viruses."

"What's that supposed to mean?"

"Your friend has a disease that seems nonfatal, which is extremely rare around here. I'm sorry to say, but it's better for us all if he stays afflicted."

"So... you're not going to cure him?"

"Not yet, at least."

"So, I'm fucked, yea? I came here thinking you all could help him."

"We will help him, just, not yet. There's more people who could use this research to survive."

"I promised I'd help him. I promised I'd be there for him."

"I can't even tell him this, I can't fucking enter his cell."

"You still can appeal it, right? Didn't the ruling happen 3 weeks ago?"

"It was denied. So now he thinks I abandoned him and I can't tell him otherwise."

Log End: March 30th, 200X, 13:05

Kaikibuyo
Infection and Theories
The source of Steel Heart Disease remains unknown, as Patient 0 never confirmed where it came from. However passing from humans to humans can cause by long-term exposal to Steel Heart Disease. The minimum time is unknown, largely because of the aforementioned loss of Patient 0. For the same reason, no known cure exists.

Below is a list of theories as to the origin of Steel Heart Disease.

Short Circuited Theory

Originating from the phenomenon found in Level 3, Short Circuited Theory suggests the cause of Steel Heart Disease to be caused by anomalous electricity. The theory points to other electrical phenomenon such as The Flicker and the abnormal quality of power sources found in levels like Level 754 to support this theory.

Sentinel Theory

Sentinel Theory suggests that machine-human entities such as Sentinels and mechanically familiar entities like Crunches being responsible for the spread of Steel Heart Disease. Namely, the lack of reproductive systems for Sentinels lead some to believe they operate on a virus-like system to sustain their population.

ITS BEEN 120 DAYS

I COUNTED EACH ONE

HOPING THAT IT WOULD CHANGE

IT NEVER DID

IVE BEEN ALONE FOR SO LONG

MY BODY HAS LOST BEAUTY

I AM NO LONGER VINNY

IM HIS REPLACEMENT

YOU KNOW THAT, DONT YOU?

IS THAT WHY YOU DONT TALK

IS THAT WHY NOBODY TALKS

IM LEFT IN THE DARK

BOTH OF MIND AND BODY

EVERYTHING FEELS WRONG

IM SCARED

BUT NONE ONE COMFORTS ME

YOULL BE HERE WHEN IM CURED

RIGHT?

WILL I BE CURED?

ALL THEY DO IS WATCH ME

IM PROMISED THE WORLD

IS THIS THE PROMISED WORLD?

JUST A DAMN SLICE OF HELL?

PLEASE. COME BACK ADRIAN

I LOVED YOU

I STILL LOVE YOU

Log End

Phenomenon 77.title: Phenomenon 77
Phenomenon 77.description:
Phenomenon 77, known as Oneiroprojection or Interdimensional Oneirodynia, is a phenomenon affecting one's dreams. Unusually among anomalous events, Oneiroprojection only affects those within The Frontrooms, with there being no record of an occurrence of it in The Backrooms, although some have attempted to classify similar phenomena such as those on Level 18 or Level 125 as Backrooms-based occurrences of it. Due to this, the phenomenon is difficult to directly study, due to those who are affected by it being unable to be contacted by researchers, leading to most information about it being sourced from the accounts of wanderers who previously experienced it and from posts on Frontrooms blogs and social media accessed through BACK-NET.

During Oneiroprojection, one will experience a dream about a level of The Backrooms, referred to as a projection. No single level seems to have a greater change of appearing in a dream, although levels that resemble commonly seen locations in The Frontrooms or those with a "dreamcore" aesthetic appear more frequently, however this may just be because these are more compatible with ordinary dreams. One typically does not have control or lucidity during the dream, although individuals skilled at lucid dreaming may possess some form of control over the dream.

Dreams containing Oneiroprojection have been reported to begin as normal, and typically contain a positive or nostalgic memory from one's past, typically from when they were a child or young adult. Many have reported having similar, non-anomalous dreams prior to experiencing a projection not featuring elements relating to The Backrooms. During the dream the dreamer would begin to notice minor inconsistencies in the memory, such as the presence of foreign objects or certain things being colored differently. Eventually, more noticeable changes will begin to be seen, such as reality being warped and distorted and people appearing without faces. The perspective of the dream would then shift outward away from the scene that is occurring and toward one's surroundings, which would begin shifting from familiar terrain to that of the level the projection is taking place in as the dreamer departs from the initial memory.

Dreamers have reported that during projections, they experience elements of the level, such entities and structural effects caused by Instability. Appearances of human wanderers are also occasionally reported, although specific information about the wanderers is not clear enough to determine whether these correspond to actual people. Entities and wanderers have shown no reaction to the presence of the dreamer, who is akin to an invisible observer from their perspective. The one exception to this is the presence of the Juju MAn, who seems to be able to perceive dreamers as he does with those in the Backrooms.
Phenomenon 77.lead:
Oneriodynia /oʊˌnaɪrəˈdɪniə/

Noun
1. A violent or disturbed imagination during sleep, as in the case of nightmares and sleepwalking.

Phenomenon 31.title: Phenomenon 31
Phenomenon 31.description:
Phenomenon 31, also called "Reformation" or (most commonly) "Rewrite", is a process that multiple levels in The Database go through upon being discovered by the M.E.G. Said process usually involves the addition of different properties, items, Person of Interests, entities, and more; while Reformations can happen to about any level stored within the Backrooms, the phenomenon itself seems to only take place in levels with little information to it (excluding Level 0).

According to many researchers, Reformation typically occurs if a level has the following:

*A dull or plain background with little notable events,
*Insufficient information enough for the M.E.G. to write so easily about,
*An increasingly high but unexplained danger level,
*A low, boring location without any threats,
*Or a messy, almost out-of-tone level to the nature of the Backrooms itself.

B.L.S.A.I.D.title: B.L.S.A.I.D
B.L.S.A.I.D.description:
B.L.S.A.I.D

OVERVIEW:

The Backrooms Large Scale Anomaly Investigation Department, also known as B.L.S.A.I.D, is a subdivision and department of the M.E.G dedicated to investigating and researching Backrooms-Wide events, in order to either find a safe way to contain it or find a safe way to survive it, allowing the rest of M.E.G to focus on other important priorities such as survival and exploration. The B.L.A.S.I.D was founded in 2016 during the Flicker, allowing for reasonable aid during the times of darkness and contributed to the survival rate of millions within the Backrooms.

While intended to only be a temporary measure during the Flicker, it has now become a substantial part of the M.E.G and officially was instituted as a permanent department in late 2019. Following the Flicker, the B.L.S.A.I.D has become a major part in the M.E.G combating major events and anomalies. Recently, the B.L.S.A.I.D came into the limelight yet again due to the department being put in charge of investigating Phenomenon 20, a Backrooms-wide anomaly shaping and warping levels into desolate and bleak versions of themselves.

Unlike other M.E.G departments, B.L.S.A.I.D does not have a unique outpost, instead it is scattered across the many M.E.G outposts throughout the Backrooms. Their "headquarters" or standard center of operations is located in Base Alpha, with smaller sectors located in Base First Light and the M.E.G Infamart Haven. Despite these circumstances, a project is underway to construct two new outposts within the Void Subcluster specifically for the B.L.S.A.I.D: Outpost Lovehall and Base Outlook.

BRANCHES:

B.L.S.A.I.D consists of two main branches, which work in conjunction to achieve the department's main focus.

RESEARCH

Arguably the most prominent Branch of the B.L.S.A.I.D, the Reasearch Branch handles much of the investigation and experimentation of the department. Some of these includes overviewing reports, looking into anomalies, as well as creating and utilizing devices and machinery. Unlike the other Branch of B.L.S.A.I.D, the Research Branch is more centralized, with the biggest concentration being on Base Alpha. Smaller pockets exists across the B.L.S.A.I.D's various clusters such as within the M.E.G Infamart Haven.

STRATEGY

An equally important Branch, the Strategy Branch handles exploration attempts, project overviews, and evacuation/survival procedures. During the Flicker, the Strategy Branch helped with evacuation protocols, establishing safe checkpoints, and providing aid to many individuals. The Strategy Branch is the most spread out amongst the two Branches, with pockets dispersed across all outposts where B.L.S.A.I.D is prevalent, including Base Alpha, Base First Light, among others.

HIERARCHY:

B.L.S.A.I.D follows a sort of loose hierarchy compared to other M.E.G departments. The differing levels are as follows:

CHAIRPERSON:

The Chairperson is the highest possible elected position in the B.L.S.A.I.D. The Chairperson oversees both Branches and is the final line in approvement for any large-scale operations. The Chairperson also attends high profile meetings with other department heads and has connections to the M.E.G Impresarios. This position is equivalent to Department Heads within the U.S. Government.

Currently this position is held by Ethan Lovehall.

BRANCH LEADERS:

Branch leaders are the second line in the B.L.S.A.I.D hierarchy, belonging to two separate individuals; one leading the Research Branch, and the other commanding the Strategy Branch. Branch Leaders are usually selected and trusted by the Chairperson, and could utilize their branch in any way they please, though have a responsibility as an abuse of their power could lead to their position being revoked. Branch Leaders are usually the first line of approval for large-scale operations, before reaching the Chairperson for the final verdict.

There are many other positions in the B.L.S.A.I.D hierarchy, though it begins to break down after the Branch Leaders. Beyond this point, positions may overlap with each other.

INNOVATIONS:

ESDs

Being one of M.E.G's greatest innovations, the Electromagnetic Space Detector or ESD is a device useful for mapping out the structure of levels, painting a decent picture of what a level's topography, properties, and nature would be before entering them. ESDs work in a similar fashion to a typical radar dish, first shooting out a multitude of electromagnetic waves into a level via a noclip spot, which would bounce off any solid objects, such as a pillar, a tree, or a mountain. From there the signals would be transmitted back to the ESD via the feed antenna located in the center of the main dish, and a specialized device analyses the signals and outputs them into a rendition of what the interior space of a level may look like.

Specialized devices within ESDs are also able to make semi accurate predictions on the rest of the level's layout based on the receiving signals. For example, if a large number of signals were not retransmitted, it could be concluded that the level is either outdoors or infinite in nature. If most signals were received, then the level could be a large open area but not infinite. In total, ESD signals could travel over five miles into a level before dissipating. Although ESDs are commonly large, smaller, portable ESDs also exists, which can also be attached to devices and other objects for easier exploration.

RELATED FILES:

Anomaly List

Hippopotomonstrosesquippedaliophobia (Jo.title: Hippopotomonstrosesquippedaliophobia (Joke)
Hippopotomonstrosesquippedaliophobia (Jo.description:
Hippopotomonstrosesquippedaliophobia is an anomalous psychological disorder that affects wanderers in The Backrooms, causing unhealthinesses among them. Victims of it are said to feel immense fear towards lengthy words overreactively for no apparent or clear reason.

Following the discovery of this phenomenon, long words starts appearing in walls of multiple levels transcendentally. Wanderers claimed to see the words written using black markers, mostly center around one area. Originally, people thought these markings possess unquestionablenesses that they were written by an abominable prankster, but more people made assumptions that they were naturally formed.

Phenomenon 1984 (Joke).title: Phenomenon 1984 (Joke)
Phenomenon 1984 (Joke).description:
Phenomenon 1984, also known as "Literally 1984" is a verbal trigger phrase that spreads to wanderers by sound. Upon acquisition of this virus, those affected will be compelled to use the same trigger when interacting with anything that restrains the individual in some way. The use of the term has spread to any action with a negative response on the wanderer, however it is most present in the case of limitation.

How the outbreak first reached the Backrooms remains unknown, though it appears to be an issue in baseline reality as well. Its first appearance within the frontrooms dates to June 8, 1949, alongside the publication of the dystopian novel "1984" by George Orwell.

Phenomenon 52.title: Phenomenon 52
Phenomenon 52.description:
The Mental Disconnection effect primarily affects those explorers who have been to the Backrooms for significant amounts of time, especially on dangerous levels or those with serious psychological effects. It is a brain reaction against accumulated traumatic moments and acts as an overreactive defence system in real life, which then acts as a lethal trap.

When an explorer contracts Mental Disconnection, the brain completely disconnects from the body, leaving it completely paralyzed. It is as if the brain, in a desperate attempt to protect itself from the constant fear and anxiety of the Backrooms, resorts to "shutting down" the connection to the body, preventing any movement even the slightest attempt to shift a foot or a finger. Despite its apparent intent to safeguard the player, this effect leaves them utterly exposed, and they are easy prey for whatever enemy is lurking in the level.

Its effect passes through three consecutive stages, which become increasingly worse and debilitating.

In the first stage, the explorer will lose physical strength and become severely weaker and exhausted. Dizziness and loss of balance for a short time shall be common, and moving around will be a problem. The phase is, however, very mild, and there isn't any lasting or significant impact in this phase. Most explorers will likely just attribute these symptoms as mere fatigue or stress from their experience in the Backrooms.

Phase two brings in progressively more threatening changes. The adventurer experiences abrupt and unaccountable attacks of unconsciousness, lapsing into fits of sleepiness at random for intervals of seconds to up to 30 minutes. Concomitant with these attacks, he or she also struggles through severe episodes of insomnia and cannot sleep even after total exhaustion. Their perception of the world will also begin to alter—colours will appear pale as if they are looking at everything through a gray, dull filter. These dissociative episodes become more and more frequent, which means the brain is starting to disconnect more and more from reality.

The final and worst phase can strike at any moment without notice. The explorer will suddenly fall into a state of complete paralysis, losing all motor function and sense of the world around them. They cannot smell, feel, or sense anything physically. But, as descriptions indicate, they remain conscious, stuck to observing their paralyzed body from an outside, third-person perspective. This horrific disease is incurable—no way of recovering from it is known. The explorer is isolated, unable to speak to the world, until their body gradually starves, dehydrates, or is damaged from the outside.

It is widely believed that when the body is dead, the explorer's mind also dies. But because no one has ever returned from this last stage to inform us of what occurs, the destiny of the victims of Mental Disconnection is unknown.

Phenomenon 57.title: Phenomenon 57
Phenomenon 57.displayTitle: Phenomenon 57: Achronism / Chronophobia; or, Per Inane Peruersum Tempus
Phenomenon 57.description:
As most who remain in this hell for long enough may know, time works radically differently in the Backrooms compared to baseline reality. Phenomenon 57, known collectively as Achronism, is a term used to describe these occurrences: innumerable temporal anomalies which exist within the Backrooms.

Distressingly common throughout the Backrooms, this anomaly presents various challenges for those traversing this dimension, with a good grip on the realm’s chronology being practically required for long-term survival.

Osmanli Liminali Alanlar Bakanligi.title: Osmanli Liminali Alanlar Bakanligi
Osmanli Liminali Alanlar Bakanligi.description:
The Osmanlı Liminal Alanlar Bakanlığı (OLAB), or the Ottoman Ministry of Liminal Spaces, is an organization that deals with the exploration, colonization, and preservation of Turkic/Ottoman culture. The organization was initially formed in the early 1900s as a secretive imperial project under Sultan Abdulhamid II but continued in "exile" following the collapse of the Ottoman Empire.

Now an independent entity, OLAB works towards preserving Ottoman/Turkish culture within the Backrooms, colonizing levels as they begin to discover the secrets of the Öte Diyâr. With Ottoman symbology denoting their presence and a strict hierarchy governing their ranks, they view their presence in the Backrooms as both a test and an opportunity—seeking to rebuild what had been lost and to make their legacy endure past the Frontrooms.
Osmanli Liminali Alanlar Bakanligi.lead: O. L. A. B.Osmanlı Liminalı Alanlar Bakanlığı

Level 914.title: Level 914
Level 914.lead:
Someone taps you on the shoulder. You wake up from a deep and long sleep and see before you... a figure that resembles a person, but in fact, it is not a person at all. You do not even understand whether this is a real living being or not. Anyway, It disappears after a moment. It seems like this is either a dream within a dream or just a hallucination.

You look about. For a moment, you do not understand how you ended up here because you have been through so much already… but then, this thought is anomalously forgotten. So, you are on the plane. On the same plane, in the same place and at the same time, when your destiny changed forever. But your mind does not currently understand that you are in The Backrooms. It, and therefore you, think that you are at the home. However, you remember very well that there was a suspicious woman sitting next to you who never looked you in the face and vice versa. But…

She is not here. And that family that was sitting behind you, with the cheerful child who wanted to be friends with you, is not here. There is no one here. You are left alone.

This place does strange things to your mind. It made it so that at first you would not realize that you were in The Backrooms. But after you notice that the plane is completely empty, you finally realize that.

"But how does the airplane fly?" — you ask, and go to see if there are any stewardesses or at least a cockpit nearby.

After half an hour of searching, you find nothing. There are no traces that people have ever been here. The plane seemed to take off on its own. You also discover that the cockpit is completely missing.

All you could not check was the business lounge. It was locked up, and you figured there might be other losers in there like you. And then you noticed something metal under the chair. It turned out to be the key! You quickly try to open the door leading to the business lounge, and you succeed!

All you see is a hall frozen in time. There are snacks here, which means there really were people here… maybe. But why are snacks only available in the business lounge, and why in that?

In addition, it looks like the snacks do not past its expiration date, which means you will live a little longer. You are happy about this at least, but suddenly you hear a knock on the porthole. You look into it and see…

Welcome to another nightmare of these places.

Level 914 is the 915th level of The Backrooms which is, for the most part, an airplane with not a single soul. It was discovered in March 21, 2025 by a wanderer who, after falling asleep on Level 36, woke up on Level 914. This level turned out to be the plane on board which he got to The Backrooms. This means that all areas of the level, except for The Apron Bus and the Sky, have common features, but the layout itself can highly vary. Besides, only those who have entered The Backrooms on board the plane can enter the level.

entrances.text: The only known way to get to Level 914 is to fall asleep on Level 36. There may be new entrances as well, as the level itself was only recently discovered.

exits.text: If try to open the door leading to the Business Lounge with what turns out to be the Level Key, a one will be taken to a random level.
Eating a snack that has a small chance of containing a sleeping pill will take a wanderer to Level 610.
If someone somehow manages to escape It at the end of the Apron Bus, he will end up at The End.
P.S. If you are the author of a level that I made into an entrance/exit and you don't like it, you can just message me on the message wall.

International Second Dimension Organizat.title: International Second Dimension Organization
International Second Dimension Organizat.description:
About us

One place where the world's nations comes together to study an alternate world that humanity has never imagined.

The International Second Dimension Organization is a United Nations agency established in 1987, which facilitates cooperation among 122 nations around the world to explore the unknown.

The second dimension is an unknown and dangerous place for humanity, and the public is not yet ready to understand it. Since its establishment, we have been focusing on the human rights issues of supernatural creatures in the second dimension, dealing with supernatural threats, the rational utilization of supernatural objects, and the peaceful development of the second dimension by various countries. With the advancement of technology, humanity is adapting at an astonishing rate to things that cannot be explained by science. In the near future, the second dimension will become a new homeland suitable for human habitation.

Our Emblem
Do you know that the emblem of the International Second Dimension Organizationwas designed by a group of Chinese designers? It was officially approved through Confidential Resolution No. 122 (II) at the ISDO Conference on February 1st, 1988. This symbolizes that the emblem has become the official emblem of ISDO and has since been used in all aspects related to ISDO. This emblem is modified from the emblem of the United Nations. The olive branches on both sides symbolize the peaceful development of the second dimension, while the infinity symbol in the middle not only represents the infinite space of the second dimension but also implies humanity's boundless curiosity about the unknown. It has practical applications and can be used to identify ISDO to all relevant parties in the conflict areas of the second dimension.

SDT
In 1987, 12 nations engaged in the research of the second dimension signed the Second Dimension Treaty. Today, the number of contracting parties to this treaty has reached 122 nations.Full text >>

In 1987, the Second Dimension Treaty (SDT) was signed in Washington, D.C., United States, by 12 founding nations including the United States, the United Kingdom, and the Soviet Union. The treaty entered into force the following year and has since been acceded to by multiple countries, with the current number of contracting parties reaching 122. The treaty contains critical provisions governing the rational development of the second dimension, such as the second dimension may only be used for peaceful purposes(Article I). Serving as a key basis for ISDO's activities in the second dimension, this treaty remains a pivotal document in shaping the progress of its exploration. To advance its objectives and ensure compliance with treaty provisions, Article 7 mandates that all areas of the second dimension, including all stations, installations, and equipment within these areas, shall be open for inspection at all times(Article VII).

Inside
Inside the second dimension, forces of ISDO are always ready to explore uncharted spaces, engage in battles with supernatural entities, rescue those who are trapped, maintain peace, and put an end to conflicts.

Outside
Outside of the Second Dimension, ISDO focuses on occult research, and with our help, the Second Dimension research institutes in various countries are running in an orderly manner.

Members

Our History

Credits

Please note that this page was written by the Head of Information Security Dpt., ISDO  and greenlit by the Database Administrator User:SherlsF.

ABOUT

Tezo's Art Supplies is a small population of creatives within the Backrooms, seeking to continue their endeavors in developing media and resources. The total group population climbs up very slowly over the time of its existence, with the current number of confirmed, non-collaborative members being 106. Tezo's Art Supplies as a whole functions secretly, with only accepted members and certain alliances allowed permission to enter their only base.

This base, affectionately titled Gouache Club, covers roughly 10 square kilometers of Level 114 and houses 83 members alongside 21 collaborators. Hanging signs of the group's logo can be seen as one reaches within 50 meters of the base, indicating their proximity. In most cases, those who appear in Level 114 will be roughly 300 meters away from Gouache Club. No other bases have been established beyond small market posts in other levels, often manned by 1 to 3 members.

Most notably, Tezo's Art Supplies creates art products and supplies, with many of them having some sort of anomalous quality to them. This idea appears to have been brought on largely because of Ethel Tezo, the founder of the group and a metaphysical engineer popular within Backrooms society between 1294 until her death in 1313. With her initial popularity, Tezo's lived on, and would organize into Tezo's Art Supplies early 1316, with the practice of artistic thaumaturgy as its focus.

HIERARCHY

SAVANT
The acting leader of Tezo's Art Supplies is known as the Savant, and holds the duty to keep up treaties and alliances with other parties. While internal affairs may be needed, those are often managed by existing members. Beyond this, the Savant often takes the role of leading advisor for all things developed by the group. They also handle later guidance to newer members on how to use the metaphysical and transmutational tools passed down through the group.

The current savant is Helena "Jill" Kiena, known online as JeffTheJiller, who was voted in April 25th 20XX for her second term as the Savant. The Savant position lasts 3 years with each one able to run for 3 terms, consecutvive or not. In the case of possible corruption, the Savant can be booted out of their position with a 2/3rd vote by the Onsite Expert Commitee, and they will act as a collective ruler to preseve normalcy until the next election cycle. During said election cycle, they also take the responsibility of running the group if voting takes too long.

ONSITE EXPERT COMMITEE
The Onsite Expert Commitee is a collection of Tezo's, Menagerie, Ncipetherazi, Cascadan, and Auriellan upper staff who form in case of issues within the group. Also forming for any issues within The Menagerie, the party preseves normalcy until leadership is reobtained. Depending on the circumstances, this meeting largely consists of those affiliated with Tezos' and Ncipetheraz, though the other groups are free to participate. Translators are often provided at these meetings.

EXPERTS
Any inhouse issues are often brought to Experts first. Experts are a collective of members picked out by various Savants and serve until they resign or are forced to. Because of the loosness of their electoral position, many smaller markets and bases outside of the main one don't have experts with them. This role is not integeral to the security of Tezo's Art Supplies, but provide help wherever. These members have also been known to help pick out and train new possible members of the group.

Provided is an incomplete list of the current experts (as listed by their tags on Backchat): Inku-Tutsu, Froghatter, Katterina, Toftpulf, Deorluv

ARTISTS
Artists are any other member of Tezo's Art Supplies that does not fit into the other categories. In digital spaces, some of them may have stars by their name to signify them as VIP, but this only exists online. Artists are allowed easy access to any of the main outposts or other markets, and are often the ones creating new products or artwork.

CONTRIBUTORS
Contributors are ex-members or otherwise people affiliated with Tezo's Art Supplies who are not considered as full members anymore. Outside of not having the ability to vote for Savants, the role is no different from the Artist role.

Partnerships

Because of the group's non-violent and friendly ethic, Tezo's remains in largely positive relationships with the majorty of Backrooms. Especially aiding to this is Tezo's outreach programs; which help artists and product developers move their focus into working for specific groups. However, some have tighter connections to the group, often forming through history or other mutually beneficial connections. In exchange for talent and other projects, these alliances help Tezo's continue its programs by providing food and supplies.

Ncipetheraz
Formed at the same time as the partnership with The Menagerie, their sustained existence allowed for the three parties to work more closely together. Ncipetheraz became majorly important to Tezo's Art Supplies during times of disarray in the party, as the Ncipetherazi Council would help the group recover after the Great Pause. It is very possible that without support, the group would have fallen apart.

Republic of Aurielle
Aurielle and Tezo's history is significantly shorter in comparison to others but is nonetheless equally important; Aurielle's presence near many of Tezo's bases is not out of coincidence. Experienced Auriellen architects and survivalists helped Tezo's difficult relocation to Level 114, which killed 28 members—survivors remain certain that without Auriellen assistance many more would have died. Furthermore, the Treaty of Friendship and Mutual Cooperation signed in 2019 between the two groups led to Aurielle acting as the main source of military defense for Tezos. Aurielle also assists Tezos through art internship programs for students and job opportunities.

On the opposite end, some groups, while still retaining a non-violent agreement with Tezo's Art Supplies, have consistently opposed the organization. In extreme cases, the two parties have tried to take each other down in a political sense. Within Tezo's current outreach program, these groups are blacklisted from getting members to assist them.

Atrego
Discourse between the two parties often revolves around art as a subject, rather than a conflict of power or influence. The different views on the medium has left Atrego to be very critical of Tezo's as a whole. Their antagonism doesn't reach much further than this, however, as both parties have shown willingness to cooperate.

PRODUCTS / ARTWORK / BASES

PRODUCTS

ARTWORK

BASES

Sigmaballs

Artists are allowed easy access to any of the main outposts or other markets, and are often the ones creating new products or artwork.

CONTRIBUTORS
Contributors are ex-members or otherwise people affiliated with Tezo's Art Supplies who are not considered as full members anymore. Outside of not having the ability to vote for Savants, the role is no different from the Artist role.

Partnerships

Because of the group's non-violent and friendly ethic, Tezo's remains in largely positive relationships with the majorty of Backrooms. Especially aiding to this is Tezo's outreach programs; which help artists and product developers move their focus into working for specific groups. However, some have tighter connections to the group, often forming through history or other mutually beneficial connections. In exchange for talent and other projects, these alliances help Tezo's continue its programs by providing food and supplies.

Ncipetheraz
Formed at the same time as the partnership with The Menagerie, their sustained existence allowed for the three parties to work more closely together. Ncipetheraz became majorly important to Tezo's Art Supplies during times of disarray in the party, as the Ncipetherazi Council would help the group recover after the Great Pause. It is very possible that without support, the group would have fallen apart.

Republic of Aurielle
Aurielle and Tezo's history is significantly shorter in comparison to others but is nonetheless equally important; Aurielle's presence near many of Tezo's bases is not out of coincidence. Experienced Auriellen architects and survivalists helped Tezo's difficult relocation to Level 114, which killed 28 members—survivors remain certain that without Auriellen assistance many more would have died. Furthermore, the Treaty of Friendship and Mutual Cooperation signed in 2019 between the two groups led to Aurielle acting as the main source of military defense for Tezos. Aurielle also assists Tezos through art internship programs for students and job opportunities.

On the opposite end, some groups, while still retaining a non-violent agreement with Tezo's Art Supplies, have consistently opposed the organization. In extreme cases, the two parties have tried to take each other down in a political sense. Within Tezo's current outreach program, these groups are blacklisted from getting members to assist them.

Atrego
Discourse between the two parties often revolves around art as a subject, rather than a conflict of power or influence. The different views on the medium has left Atrego to be very critical of Tezo's as a whole. Their antagonism doesn't reach much further than this, however, as both parties have shown willingness to cooperate.

PRODUCTS / ARTWORK / BASES

PRODUCTS

ARTWORK

BASES

Sigmaballs

Metamorphosis.title: Metamorphosis
Metamorphosis.displayTitle: A Billet-Doux / Metamorphosis / Into the Icarian Sea
Metamorphosis.description:
C:\WINDOWS> open file \windows\desktop\a-billet-doux.doc

> a billet-doux

Dear Reader,

I have been here for around two-and-a-bit years now – if we ignore the six-month hiatus that I took near the beginning of my time here. That is such a small amount of time, less than the blink of an eye on a cosmic level. Yet, I have changed. I do not think I can call the person that I was a few years ago (a younger, immature, and less adept author, I must admit) me. If I were to look into a mirror I would be confronted with an entirely different person – like a living, breathing Theseus' Ship.

It has been an amazing experience. Integrating myself as part of the community has allowed me to bloom, and it has helped me figure out part of who I am, as sappy as it may sound. The nature of WikiMedia and Fandom editing is strange in some aspects, but growing comfortable with it has given me the capability to adapt to a new digital landscape, the quirks of which I have grown to love.

There have been many ups and downs while I have interacted here. That is clear, obvious, expected of such a chaotic site. And yet, between the peaks and troughs, I found a community that gave me a voice. It was empowering to a younger me, especially as I felt as if I was just living, nevermind striving towards anything.

Perhaps that is why I have lingered as long as I have. However, now I feel a gentle tugging in the other direction. I no longer have the interest for writing here anymore, and so I am departing, taking the first flight out to who-knows-where. I have a life to get to.

I do not doubt that I may peek at the community and its state in the future. Curiosity will get the better of me: it is simply human nature. But this page will, most likely, be the last that I write for this site. It is highly personal to an early-2025 me, so I hope that you, reader, enjoy it. My other pages are irrelevant: this is my magnum opus, the culmination of my time here.

Take what follows as you will: a love letter to the community, a nota bene to my time here, or the final scene to my not-so-thrilling act. Take this as arrogance, dramaticism, and immaturity; take this as authenticity, devotion, and intimacy. I expect that few traces of me will remain after a year or two of activity, or lack thereof, anyways, like a mare eroding a terra. If so, the fragments of the person that shimmer on this site will not be the same as what I will become.

I have enjoyed my time here, and I hope that you have enjoyed my time here too. It is time for me to let my light escape into the beautiful.

Yours Faithfully,

Char.

C:\WINDOWS> open file \windows\desktop\phenomenon-111.doc

Phenomenon 111 – otherwise known as Metamorphosis or Shedding, alongside many other names – is a rarely experienced event that results in the absolute transformation of a person into another, distinct individual. it is seen in around one in fifty thousand wanderers, making it a seldom sight. wanderer accounts have described the event as "gut-wrenching" due to witnessing the painful changes that the affected person undergoes, and the phenomenon's potential duration.

in response to increased research of the phenomenon, units dedicated to assisting wanderers through the physical and psychological aspects of Metamorphosis – called rosemary wings by those that work in them – have been established by the red cross. those that are experiencing Phenomenon 111 should be brought to one of these wings to minimise the emotional and physical stress that they have to undergo.

 symptoms

the beginning of the process is difficult to identify, but physical signs include trembling, cold fever, nausea / vomiting, and irritation of the skin, particularly in the hands. mental symptoms include increased anxiety and stress (with no apparent source), burnout, increased irritability, and a sense of self-isolation. insomnia is extremely common, especially as the phenomenon continues, due to other symptoms.

as Metamorphosis continues, skin will begin to shed in flakes and the person will experience hair loss. this may result in noticeable marks on the skin where its pigmentation differs, alongside a discrepancy in hair colour. drowsiness is also reported at this stage, with the individual having to sleep for around twelve hours per day. eventually, the person will enter a deep sleep, from which they cannot be stirred, similar to hibernation. the person's skin and hair will fall off in its entirety, but will be replaced almost immediately by that which has been growing beneath it.

when the wanderer awakens, noticeable differences in their mannerisms and appearance are easily identifiable. amnesia is extremely common, and the person will forget all previous interactions and experiences: these are replied by false memories that emerge shortly after awakening. furthermore, the wanderer will forget having gone through Phenomenon 111, meaning that - due in part to the stress that it entails - no accounts of it exist written by somebody that has experienced the event; third-person reports are prevalent, however, allowing researchers to document the effects that it has on the wanderer's loved ones.

Shedding typically happens over approximately a month, which means that those supporting the victim will typically experience a worsening of mental health due to emotional strain. in addition, intense grief and attachment to the person that wakes up should be expected due to the eventual loss of the victim. it is vital to remember that said person is entirely dissimilar to the same person before Metamorphosis, and that one should not obsess over the wanderer as it could lead to violence if they are restrained by force.

 causes

it is unknown why Phenomenon 111 occurs, but scientists speculate that it is due to the background influence exerted by the achospic plane on the backrooms, though to a lesser degree than roth syndrome and for a longer amount of time. in the past, it was speculated that a pathogen caused the onset of the phenomenon, but it has been disproven through the examination of blood samples of those that undergo the process.

 records

 >> into-the-icarian-sea

There is a certain beauty
in setting the world on fire
and watching from the centre
of the flames.

There is a certain beauty
in setting the world on fire
and watching from the centre
of the flames.

III

"You don't know what you have until it's gone" is one of those proverbs that you never really appreciate until it forces the barrel of hindsight between your eyes. I can feel regret oozing from the hole that the bullet has cored through the bark of my cranium and down onto the ground and into the roots of the people around me. I can feel the pressure of pansophical air as it gasps into the vulnerability and whispers reassuring falsehoods. I can feel her absence: I am left a stem without a flower, a clock without hands, a vessel without a ferryman — destined to wreck on cliffs of isolation and preoccupation.

Back then, when we were coming to terms with the oddities of this place, her enthusiasm stupified me. God knows how much she trudged through to get to Level 4, but somehow she had come out of that scot-free. I remember my first interaction with her, after she had recovered from the injuries she had sustained from several entity attacks. She had that bouncing-off-the-walls vigour that had slipped from most's grasps after falling from heaven. Her energy was infectious: that silky laughter that foamed from her mouth after I had attempted to tell her some poor, easily forgettable joke contaminated me with joy and tranquility. Thinking about it now, I shouldn't have taken that for granted.

And eventually, she got bored: "Nothing ever happens here. Can't we also go on missions?" She had pouted. I kept that at bay for a time, before her energy ground the dam of fear into dust, and I finally gave in. We began helping the Colonists and, to her delight, we became well acquainted with the expanses of Limbo. I still find it strange that such a hostile and adverse environment was so varied and niche, beautiful in her eyes, yet still hostile. We had forgotten that.

One of my fondest memories was looking up at the stars in 239 and tracing the patterns of its constellations, discussing what events immortalised them in the sky. Eventually that dissolved into random conversation, and then further, down into silence. We must have spent hours gazing up before our companions called us back to our tent.

And then it tore her away from me.

II

I started, drenched in icy sweat, heaving with anxious energy. Something bad was going to happen. It was an inexplicable feeling but I knew something had shifted out of place. I attempted to calm myself, but to no avail. I ended up leaving the apartment to visit Selena, closing the door behind me with trembling hands.

Cloud strangled the sky beyond the windows. It wasn't a typical sight, but it felt malicious, serpentine. Hissing rain slithered down the windows.

In front of me was her abode. I sighed, knocked on her door, and waited.

And waited. She did not answer the door. I tried again, and again, before she eventually opened the door.

Selena looked half-asleep, her eyes frenzied and blighted with scarlet. She looked dispirited – ashen grey, as though all the blood had drained from her face overnight. Echoes of sobs and tears and collapse screamed at me through tear marks on her cheeks and the jittering of her hands and the slight depression of her head as her neck tried not to give out.

"Hey Selena—" My eyes widened, and I dropped my voice. "Shit" She did not respond for a long while, her mouth hinging open and shut like a broken door.

"I don't know what's happening to me." She stuttered, her eyes meeting mine for a mere second before hanging her head once more. "I don't know why I feel like this." I looked at her and another tear fled the detritus of her eyes.

Trying to obtain information from her at that time was not the right course of action. Regardless, I went through with it. "Do you want to talk about what's going on inside?"

Finally, she stared at me. I could tell that she did not want me prodding into her troubles, but I felt the dagger of obligation against my throat, which she picked up on despite her deterioration. "Fine."

The place was a mess. Sheets were flung across the room, crumpled in a lone corner. Fragments of porcelain pierced several regions of Selena's muted carpet. The table was flipped, its amputated legs begging for release. Everything was scattered everywhere. The strangest part, however, were the mountain of flecks of skin and clumps of hair that could be seen next to the naked bed. Looking back at Selena, I had began to notice patches of skin and hair missing from her face and scalp, from which emerged a discoloured rind.

She upturned two chairs for us to sit upon.

"You haven't been out in two weeks, Selena. What's happening in there?" I ask, concerned.

She sighs, rubbing her eyes. "I have no idea. I'm being truthful here. These thoughts started to devour my mind, began to whisper to me, saying that I was never good enough, that I didn't deserve you, that you don't deserve me. It's so bizarre. Then I could not sleep, and my skin began to peel off before my eyes, alongside several other nasty things. Now I sleep for extremely long durations, yet I feel malnourished."

"You need to see a doctor. There's one five minutes from here, I can walk you there." I rebuked her, tears in my eyes. "This isn't normal, Selena. I know this is not the Selena I know."

She sighed. "I will be fine." She was always so stubborn; it always drove me up the wall. "You may leave."

"Please, just go and see someone about it, Selena—"

"You have overstayed your welcome. Do you want to take up any more of my time?" Selena snapped. I jerked, as if by whiplash: I had never heard her talk to me in such a tone before then.

"O—OK." I muttered.

I wish I hadn't left. I wish I had stayed and comforted her more. Maybe I could've stopped what was to come.

I

All is quiet. The corpse on the bed is not dead, is not alive. Selena began her hibernation five days ago. No amount of trying would let her to emerge from it.

All I know is that she would wake up a different person. Her skin and hair fall off in large chunks now, which I have to tidy periodically, lest she suffocates from it. I can peer through the cracks in her face and observe what she will become: I can see sharp, sky-blue eyes; her lips are soft and ruby, reminiscent of the sunsets that we watched in 913. Her hair falls in waves of gold — a tapestry of magnanimousness and charity. Her skin is perfectly bronze.

She looks beautiful. Perhaps as beautiful as she looked when I met her on that wonderful day.

I can't move on. My breath is catching, my hands are trembling, my tears fall uniform onto this note as I realise I do not have the will to finish it. I have to overcome this, somehow. I must, lest

 > Open Credits?

Author: Char.

Thanks to Cringe for critiquing the page.

The quote at the top of Into the Icarian Sea originates from here.

This marks my last page. I made it to express my recent feelings about myself and life in general, alongside to justify my departure in some manner. My time here has concluded.

Thank you all for your support. Everyone that I have spoken to has genuinely made the last couple of years blissful.

It has been an honour writing for you.
