Level 606/offset3.title: Level 606/offset3
Level 606/offset3.description:
Level 606 is a decrepit complex consisting of expansive office bathroom and cramped, lengthy corridors. The level's condition reflects extensive temporal decay. Tiles often collapse, and so do parts of the ceiling. The carpet is dirty and infested with empty soda cans, papers, and garbage bags. Mirrors are cracked, sinks are shattered, and graffiti can be commonly seen on the walls, among other things. A notable feature of Level 606 is the abundance of blood. It can commonly be seen on the walls, floor, and mirrors. However, after a sample was closely examined, it was determined that this blood is not that of a human; instead, it is thought to be artificial.

As mentioned previously, the level is severely unstable, and an environmental hazard, due to the debris that commonly collapse on wanderers. Certain sections of the ceiling reveal exposed wiring and faulty lighting systems; instead, the primary sources of illumination within the level are the numerous flashlights that can be found within. Their origins are unknown, thought it is thought that they were left by those unfortunate enough to enter the level previously.

Other objects can be found in seemingly random places of the complex, including chairs, ladders, posters and miscellaneous items. These objects are often coated in a layer of dust, though some of them appear eerily pristine compared to their surroundings. Their sole purpose is to provide a hinderance to one's movement, as they can frequently be found blocking rooms and areas that would've normally been accesible. Certain rooms within the level additionally contain pillars which support the roof; some of these pillars are tilted, and some have completely collapsed, which implies that the level's condition will only get worse over time.Level 606 is almost entirely silent. Noise levels fluctuates near zero decibels, which is enough for a wanderer to hear their own heartbeat. However, complete silence is never achieved; for unknown reasons, one may occasionally hear a hum reminiscent to the one found in Level 0. This is impossible because the level's lighting system is dysfunctional, and even if it functioned, the hum would not be present because it doesn't run on a fluorescent system.

The temperature usually fluctuates from 6c to -3c, thought the lowest recorded temperature was -10c. Even if the temperature isn't necessarily freezing, it still has the effects of one. Wanderers will frequently shake, and gushes of wind will become stronger. Some individuals have even reported that the ground feels like snow, depending on what type of footwear they're wearing.

Unlike the majority of levels within the Backrooms, Level 606 has completely euclidean geometry, mimicing the one in the Frontrooms. This makes movement around  the level incredibly easy, and allows the level to be fully mapped. The complex is surprisingly small compared to other Levels, with the estimated size hovering around 250,000 square meters (0,25 square kilometers or 2,691,000 square feet). However, a full map of the level has not been made, and attempts to create it have been cancelled due to the dangers within Level 606.

entrances.text: As of now, there are two documented exits. The first is to find and enter a see-through door with a dim hospital room behind it in any level, the second is to sit and smile in any area of Level 605 for a minute.

exits.text: The most reliable means of exit is finding a container of Moth Jelly and consuming the Moth Jelly inside. This leads to Level 9.2. Alternatively, if one finds themselves in a room with shattered beer bottles upon entry, they will be sent to Level 85 within mere seconds.
The Lantern Walk
The Fictional Pulp
The Thieves of Skin
The Viennese Sunday

Backroom Colonists.title: Backroom Colonists
Backroom Colonists.description:
The Backroom Colonists, formerly known as the Backroom Pioneers and sometimes referred to as the Backroom Explorers, is a colony initially comprised of various factions and smaller groups that ultimately unified under an elected minister. Despite setbacks, this faction still stands as the largest singular group within the known backrooms and ranks among the most powerful and influential groups, second only to the M.E.G and thriving across hundreds of levels.

While being a united group, each member retains significant freedom and control over their own actions. This autonomy plays a pivotal role in the growth of the Backroom Colonists, as joining is straightforward, and the organization only exercises loose authority over its outposts. Although calculating the total population is quite challenging, given that members are dispersed across numerous outposts on different levels, with the loose affiliation fostering more individuals to constantly join and leave, estimates still put their overall numbers far above second and third place, that being the M.E.G and Aurielle. What sets them back however, is their loose organization, while allowing the group to be more appealing and welcoming, has drastically lowered their efficiency.

It is worth noting that the Backroom Colonists were once more formidable. However, conflicts and internal reforms led to the substantial weakening of the colony. Numerous outposts and bases were either scaled back, abandoned, or left for other groups to claim. Divisions broke away from the Backroom Colonists, vanishing into obscurity. Since those events, the Backroom Colonists have dispersed, engaged in various conflicts, and endured through multiple periods within the Backrooms.

The relationship between the M.E.G. and the Backroom Colonists is a complex tapestry of alliance, conflict, and eventual reconciliation. It began with a foundation of mutual interest, leading to the formation of the Allied Liminal Coalition—a powerful partnership that successfully defeated the common threat of the U.E.C. in the Great Wake War. However, as both groups expanded, trust eroded into suspicion and competition, resulting in active sabotage on both sides. This, along with various other factors, led both factions to become major participants in the protracted and bitter conflict known as the Bloodbath, from which the M.E.G. ultimately emerged victorious.

Through negotiations involving technology sharing and demilitarization, the Colonists were permitted to reform. However, their continued support for Aureille—a faction opposed by the M.E.G. during the Korthie Casiagen War—further strains relations. The aftermath resulted in a tense stalemate, but shared challenges—along with the existential crisis posed by the Flicker—compelled both groups to return to cooperation. From this necessity, a new, pragmatic partnership has developed. Today, the two groups maintain exceptionally close ties, characterized by joint holdings, integrated trade, and unprecedented technological collaboration, symbolizing a hard-won peace built upon their shared history.

|-| K.R.O. =

The Kauer-Colonist Alliance was officially established in the Treaty of Fikes. This alliance is primarily pragmatic, creating a formal, albeit limited, framework for cooperation. While the core objectives of the two groups are largely disparate, they are prepared to support one another. Despite having few commonalities, the Colonists have provided protection, traded resources, and occasionally shared research. Both factions have also participated in documenting Level 24.

|-| A.E.G. =

On the surface, the Colonists and the Anti-Entity Group (A.E.G.) appear to have a simple, neutral-to-friendly relationship, with the A.E.G. periodically commissioning the Colonists to conduct census counts, as their large population provides the necessary experience and infrastructure to carry out these tasks efficiently. However, any efforts to deepen their relationship are fundamentally hindered by a core philosophical divide: the A.E.G. is dedicated to the eradication of entities and believes in human superiority, while the Colonists maintain a very liberal and pro-entity-friendly perspective, a stance that leads some Colonists to view the A.E.G. as potential fascists and extremists.

|-| E.R.C. =

The Colonists and the Entity Research Committee (E.R.C.) maintain a cooperative, though narrowly defined, relationship. Despite differing objectives, their shared presence on Level 5 has led to close collaboration within the level’s outposts, with the Colonist documenting the environmental aspects while the E.R.C. catalogs potential local entities—including the alleged previous abundance of Deathmoths and the speculated Beast of Level 5. Additionally, both groups have shared a variety of research documents, but interaction beyond this between the two groups remains limited. Lando Carlsen, the Colonists' head of diplomacy, however, has expressed interest in strengthening relationships, indicating the potential for a more substantial alliance in the future.

|-| Aurielle =

The Backroom Colonists share a strong and enduring alliance with the Republic of Aurielle, a bond forged through shared history. The Colonists have acted to protect splinter Auriellen factions such as the Istauran Federation and provided critical humanitarian and material aid to Aurielle during the Korthie Casiagen War. This partnership culminated in the creation of the Geminia Divisional Group, an autonomous unit of Aurielle volunteers integrated into the Colonists. Today, the alliance remains strong, characterized by joint research, military exercises, and fast-tracked access between their respective holdings.

In stark contrast, the Colonists hold an openly hostile stance toward the Emporium of Agoreas—a criminal syndicate also known as the Agorean Mafia, which operates covertly within Aurielle’s ranks. The Colonists became aware of Agoreas through intercepted intelligence indicating their illicit drug shipments were being concealed within legitimate cargo, potentially compromising Colonist supply lines. While no formal diplomatic engagement exists, the prevailing sentiment is one of deep opposition, with many advocating for open warfare with the syndicate.

|-| Backrooms Red Cross =

The Backroom Colonists maintain a formal and enduring humanitarian partnership with the Backrooms Red Cross, founded upon a shared commitment to aiding wanderers throughout the Backrooms. The Red Cross has consistently served as an integral medical backbone for the Colonists, providing critical care during crises and alleviating strain on the Department of Welfare. This cooperation deepened significantly during the widespread catastrophe of the Flicker, leading to the establishment of temporary joint medical facilities in holdings such as Base Blink. Recently, the alliance has expanded into collaborative medical research, with both organizations pooling knowledge and resources in pursuit of cures for entity-borne pathologies, most notably the Sanguine Festivus Virus.

|-| Party Crashers =

Although not the closest in diplomacy, Party Crashers and Backrooms Colonists maintain cordial, neutral, and mutually respectful relations, with both factions honoring established territorial boundaries. Relations were strengthened following the Colonists' recognized contributions during the Euphoric Wars, leading to further partnerships between the two groups. This cooperative dynamic has enabled crasher units to be stationed across seven different Backrooms Colonist bases.

|-| Republic of 153 =

The Colonists and the Republic of Level 153 enjoy a strong and positive relationship. Since the establishment of Outpost Vintage, the Republic has actively participated in trade with the Colonists. During the events of the Flicker, the Colonists did their best to help the Republic avoid mass starvation. Additionally, the group recognizes Colonist Mina as an official and interchangeable currency.

|-| R.E.U. =

The Backroom Colonists maintain friendly and constructive relations with the Royal Exploration Unit (R.E.U.). The two groups share significant historical and cultural affinities, which have fostered a generally friendly and cooperative dynamic. While the R.E.U.’s existing alliance with the M.E.G. initially complicated diplomacy, this tension resolved as Colonist–M.E.G. relations improved in the wake of the Flicker.

Though the Colonists and the R.E.U. possess broadly similar exploratory and settlement-oriented goals, they operate under a clear understanding of non-interference, ensuring their activities remain complementary rather than competitive. This mutual respect has allowed for the establishment of beneficial trade agreements and resource exchanges, which have flourished over time and contributed to the prosperity and operational capacity of both organizations.
Backroom Colonists.lead:
"Even during the darkest of nights, stars permeate, and shine bright..."

Two members of The Backroom Colonists exploring Level 10.

The Resurgence Bureau.title: The Resurgence Bureau
The Resurgence Bureau.description:
The Resurgence Bureau, otherwise referred to as Bureau or The Rebellion, is a rationalist group that refers to itself as a sovereign nation. However, it only consists of a few official colonies due to the fact that it was very recently established.

The group is notable for being created by rebels from various groups including Aurielle, Auto-Nexus, and Ncipetheraz, though the most prevalent one easily being the M.E.G. It was first created in 1997, and has been steadily growing since then, sitting at a decent population which is estimated to be around 1000.

The Resurgence Republic is praised around the Backrooms for being incredibly welcoming towards new members. Their population is growing quickly, likely due to the fact that their main colony is situated within a densely populated area in Level 1. They allow wanderers to temporarily stay inside their outpost, though are not very lenient about who they choose to join them in their ventures. This is because several spies from the aforementioned groups have attempted to attack them and infiltrate them from the inside, yet none have been successful.

The Resurgence Bureau's organizational structure is tightly knit, with protocols and a clear set of rules. The Bureau operates as a military-style republic, with a President leading the group and overseeing major decisions. Unlike most groups of this type, the President is elected every year. First, a vote is held on whether their current President is to be kept or disbarred. If the latter is chosen, then the President at that time cannot participate in the election. After that vote comes the real election, where every member of the group can run for president. The vote is done via the group's website to maximize safety. However, whilst everyone can vote, a sub-group named "The High Command," which is composed of representatives from the colonies under the Bureau’s control, are able to replace the President if they are found to be guilty of previous corruption. This council helps ensure that no single faction within the group holds too much power, and promotes fairness within their ranks. The High Command also oversees the Bureau's expansion efforts.

Despite its military-like structure, the Resurgence Bureau is deeply rooted in the belief of self-reliance and individual rights. Members of the Bureau are expected to contribute to the collective well-being of the community through various roles, such as construction, resource management, and defense. The Bureau places heavy emphasis on the protection of its colonies and resources, which has led to the formation of elite task forces known as "Revolts." These Revolts are the Bureau’s primary defense against any external threats, whether they be hostile groups, aggressive wanderers, or entities within the Backrooms. They are highly trained, disciplined, and fiercely loyal to the Republic, often seen patrolling the perimeter of their territories and ensuring that no enemy infiltrates their ranks.

One of the key aspects that sets the Resurgence Bureau apart from other factions is its approach to technology. Unlike other groups that are heavily reliant on advanced or experimental technology, the Bureau takes a more cautious approach, preferring to use their own tools and methods to maintain its territories. This is partly due to the fact that many of their members come from backgrounds where technology was scarce, and thus, they have learned to adapt to the environment around them without relying on tech. The Bureau’s innovation is instead focused on sustainable practices, including the use of renewable resources and decentralized systems that allow for more autonomy in its colonies.

The Bureau also places great importance on diplomacy, with a number of emissaries assigned to maintain relationships with other factions in the Backrooms. The Resurgence Bureau recognizes that alliances and trade agreements are essential to its long-term survival. In recent years, they have managed to broker deals with other factions, securing necessary resources such as food, medical supplies, and specialized equipment. However, these alliances are always tentative, with the Bureau remaining wary of betrayal and constantly on the lookout for any sign of danger.

Phenomenon 42.title: Phenomenon 42
Phenomenon 42.description:
Water was never my strong suit, even in the Frontrooms.

All my life, I remember my friends and family splashing around in pools, playing in the waves at the beach, and riding huge, thrilling waterslides.

But I never did.

Every time, someone would ask why I'm feeling down, why I did not join in on the fun like the rest of them.

I would always give excuses, never telling anyone that I was deathly afraid of water.

But fears always have a way of being discovered by others.

One fateful day, a video of one of my freakouts to entering a hot tub was published to the internet.

Everything changed after that. I was the laughingstock of the whole school, everybody called me "salt girl", "scaredy cat", and others. I lost a whole bunch of "friends" who immediately joined in on the crowd and began to bully me.

I slowly began to distance myself from others, hid out from public events, and kept to myself within the school's bathrooms. I developed depression, crippling insecurity, and my self confidence was in the toilet.

I still remember, on the exact moment I entered the Backrooms, I wished for the ground to open up, for me to disappear from this world, and for everyone to forget that I even exist.

And my wish seemed to have come true, as in that next moment, the only thing I felt was a sensation of falling, before landing with a damp thud.

At that time, I considered the Backrooms a blessing, even with hordes of incomprehensible horrors lurking around every corner. All the people I met, more specifically came across, did not care about me, or even make fun of me, they were just trying to survive like I was.

During my journey I assembled a close-nit group of friends, who cared about me as a person, and developed serious survival strategies and resource management skills.

Even with the danger of the Backrooms, my fear of water never faded away.

Something as simple as a puddle would make me jump out of my skin, especially after a near-death encounter with the Hydrolitis Plague near the beginning of my journey.

Places like Level 7 or Level 37 became my worst nightmares, despite the latter being known for safety and comfort.

But that all changed one day, as I was afflicted by the absolute embodiment of my greatest fears.

Phenomenon 42, more commonly known as "Sensory Deprivation", "Isolation", or simply "Stasis", is a rare phenomenon that manifests randomly to individuals experiencing severe stress or anxiety. Initially, Phenomenon 42 manifests subtly, with subjects forming an unnatural fascination with water or environments containing water, no matter if the subject possess water related phobias, such as hydrophobia or thalassophobia. This fascination becomes more extreme as time passes, with individuals regularly consuming and immersing themselves in water and waterlike substances, such as Almond Water.

After approximately 2 weeks, all exits out of one's current level will be blocked off and will be redirected to an anomalous underwater expanse known as Phenomenon 42A. The expanse is indefinitely deep, with no ocean floor and no surface, though sunlight streams in from an unknown source. Upon entry, all senses will be dampened except for sight and smell, and all video cameras will cease working. Despite being surrounded by water, one is able to breathe within Phenomenon 42A via an unknown fashion. After entry, subjects will remain in Phenomenon 42A for around 24 hours to 5 days, after which an unknown vacuum force will pull one out of the expanse and transport them to a random aquatic themed level, where one will regain all senses, and all technology will begin to function again.

Individuals who have experienced Phenomenon 42 have reported a wide range of personality shifts, ranging from differing outlooks on life to reduced fears, an ability to control themselves, and an increase of happiness. Subjects have also reported their life flashing before their eyes, with forgotten details of specific life events or lost memories able to be discovered again. Many have often described Phenomenon 42 as a therapeutic experience, as the silent underwater isolation provides a well renowned break from the horrors of the Backrooms.

Typically, only 20% of individuals within the Backrooms will encounter Phenomenon 42, with the majority never experiencing it. However, in recent years, the cases of Phenomenon 42 encounters have become more frequent, with some experiencing the phenomenon more than twice in their lifetimes. At this moment, the source of the rising number of encounters is unclear.

Or at least what should have been.

Instead, it functioned as the beginning of an eye-opening experience, where I was able to get closure on major life events.

It all started when an accidental noclip brought me to Level 188.8, which usually would have triggered a freakout due to the abundance of water. In fact, the first few minutes of my stay kept me constantly on edge.

Though, as time progressed, I felt the urge to jump into the water, despite the everlasting threat of the Hydrolitis Plague, which I had to continually resist.

Things came to a head as I noclipped out of the level to Level 37, which resumed the urge.

A part of me wanted to jump, but another, deep down in my heart, still bounded by my everlasting fear, resisted me.

Despite the attempts to resist, I completely ignored my fight or flight response and dove straight in.

The water felt refreshing all around me, and even though my body told me to get out from years of training, I wanted more.

Aquatic levels, where I once avoided, became my new favorite levels. I constantly spent long periods of time within hot tubs and pools, and my fear slowly dwindled little by little.

So, as I opened the unlocked door in Level 1, usually leading to Level 2, only to be greeted by a massive expanse of water, I did not run away from it, instead I accepted it, seemingly knowing that it was good.

As I floated within the water, it pulled me in deeper, eventually putting me into a trance like state. All my senses were muted, except for sight and smell. Despite being surrounded by water, I was able to breathe normally.

Eventually, my life began to play out similar to a movie. I saw the typical staples of my life: the fun I missed out on, the relentless bullying, the moment I entered the Backrooms, my first time encountering an entity, and the friends I met along the way. The typical stuff.

One thing I did not expect to get, was the experience that gave me the everlasting fear of water.

I never remembered how or what happened to make me so afraid of water. I always thought that I was born that way.

However that was not the case.

I was present in a large fishing boat. A shoreline was visible to my left, though only expansive ocean was visible to my right, The tide around me was choppy. The sky above me was sunny, with no clouds to be seen. On the boat were five other people: my mom, my dad, and three other girls, one of which I recognized instantly.

My older sister.

Now, I always knew I had an older sister, she was the emergency adult whether mom or dad would go away. Her face would be etched into the back of my mind. She died when I was six years old, but I never figured out how.

I recognized the two girls accompanying my sister within the boat as her friends, who she would invite over while babysitting me. They were not too close to her, since she officially met them 2 months before the current moment within my memories. They did not like me, with them constantly saying that I was an "ugly baby".

The boating trip was pitched as a family trip, a way for my sister to get to know her new friends more, and for my parents to have a little break away from home. Since they could not find a nanny to babysit me, I came along.

That was their biggest mistake, as they would end up scarring me forever.

About an hour into the trip, my sister's friends decided to play a "prank" on her, by pushing her into the water. As my sister was looking over the edge of the boat, one of the friends went behind her and pushed her in with a shove, and she fell into the water with a splash. Even though they knew she was not able to swim, they still assumed that the life vest she had on would keep her afloat until she was rescued out of the water.

However, they were very, very wrong, as the life vest was not inflated properly. I watched as my sister struggled against the waves, before going under. Her "friends" suddenly realized she was gone, and they were mortified. They ran to my parents for help, and my dad offered to try and save her. He jumped in the water after her.

My parents rushed me into a side room, but I crawled and watched through an adjacent window. Out of that one window, I watched on, and 10 minutes later, I saw my dad reemerge out of the water, with my sister's unconscious body. Despite our attempts at resuscitation, she did not wake up.

Even as she was rushed to the hospital, the doctors stated the one thing that we did not want to hear: she was dead. Immediately, the friends ran out of the room, crying. I saw my parents crying, and so did I.

From then on, I developed a fear of water, assuming that the water was going to kill me, just like what happened to my sister.

They say the brain gets rid of traumatic memories if it is too hard for one to cope with, and I believe that was what happened to me, hence why I did not remember what happened on that day.

After the movie of my memories ended, I felt a strong pull, and I woke up in Level 399, specifically within the Neon Natatorium.

Now, even though I still express mild apprehension when I see water, old habits die hard after all, I am no longer deeply scared of water, and I am not afraid to pass through aquatic levels as my journey progresses. I have finally gotten closure on my life, and I know I made my older sister proud.

I believe the Backrooms finally allowed me mercy from its constant trials and tribulations, while also giving me an opportunity to overcome my deepest fear.

And for that I say...

...thank you.

Author: Snow

This is more or less an attempt for shorter, more narrative focused pages. I hope you all had a nice read.

Also yay this got made into a video :) -> https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1XOmdEEleRE

My parents rushed me into a side room, but I crawled and watched through an adjacent window. Out of that one window, I watched on, and 10 minutes later, I saw my dad reemerge out of the water, with my sister's unconscious body. Despite our attempts at resuscitation, she did not wake up.

Even as she was rushed to the hospital, the doctors stated the one thing that we did not want to hear: she was dead. Immediately, the friends ran out of the room, crying. I saw my parents crying, and so did I.

From then on, I developed a fear of water, assuming that the water was going to kill me, just like what happened to my sister.

They say the brain gets rid of traumatic memories if it is too hard for one to cope with, and I believe that was what happened to me, hence why I did not remember what happened on that day.

After the movie of my memories ended, I felt a strong pull, and I woke up in Level 399, specifically within the Neon Natatorium.

Now, even though I still express mild apprehension when I see water, old habits die hard after all, I am no longer deeply scared of water, and I am not afraid to pass through aquatic levels as my journey progresses. I have finally gotten closure on my life, and I know I made my older sister proud.

I believe the Backrooms finally allowed me mercy from its constant trials and tribulations, while also giving me an opportunity to overcome my deepest fear.

And for that I say...

...thank you.

 > Open Author and Licensing Details    > Close Author and Licensing Details

Author: Snow

This is more or less an attempt for shorter, more narrative focused pages. I hope you all had a nice read.

Also yay this got made into a video :) -> https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1XOmdEEleRE

All Run Out.title: All Run Out

Level 434/offset 3.title: Level 434/offset 3
Level 434/offset 3.description:
Level 434 is an expansive network of suburban developments, houses, and roads. The level closely imitates the "Dreamcore" art style in terms of appearance, with multicolored houses, fine green grass with no weeds, and titular playground structures within parks. The entirety of Level 434 is clean and sterile, with no dirt, mud, or wear and tear present throughout the level as a whole. Any traces of dirt or other residue that did not originate from Level 434 will be cleaned up once out of sight. The temperature of Level 434 is typically 59 degrees Fahrenheit (15 degrees Celsius) but can drop to 43 degrees Fahrenheit (6.11 degrees Celsius) during nighttime hours.

Level 434 possess a day-night cycle of 12 hours, with 7 hours of daytime and 5 hours of nighttime. This cycle is not affected no matter what time one enters the level at. Sunset and sunrise, instead of being a slow, gradual fade similar to the Frontrooms, happens very quickly, with sunrise lasting 5 minutes and sunset lasting 7 minutes. Upon the commencement of nighttime, all lights will turn on one by one, including the level's houses, casting an orange-yellow light through the windows.

A multitude of silhouettes also appear within the windows, giving the appearance of each house being occupied, although entering a house proves this to not be the case. At first, these figures will appear to be partaking in typical activities, such as watching TV, cooking, and playing board games. However, after a certain amount of time, each figure will stop their activities, and turn towards the window, before freezing in place. They will continue to remain in a state of stasis until eventually fading away by sunrise. Cases of shadows being frozen in uncanny positions have been reported, including hanging upright on the ceiling and being twisted in unnatural knots. It is currently unknown if the figures are an actual entity or a profound effect created by the level.

Despite the exterior setup of the level's houses being profoundly different, entering any house within Level 434 brings one to the same exact interior setup, with the same architectural layout, similar furniture, and similar aesthetics. Foodstuffs are stored within cupboards and fridges, and upon taking an item, a new, identical version appears within the cabinet via unknown means. The food itself has been described as "tasteless but addicting". At the second floor of each house is two identical bedrooms; one being a master bedroom with an affixed bathroom, and the other being a smaller room with a crib. One of the few differences between each house lies within this smaller room, as the wallpaper is either in a shade of blue or pink, seemingly indicating a boy or girl's room.

Level 434 is almost completely silent, with no wind, no bird chirp, and no naturally occurring sounds to speak of. The only noise that can be generated within Level 434 are noises caused by one's body movements, such as speaking, walking, and breathing. No sound is made when one interacts with objects, as the doors have no squeak, throwing something onto the ground produces vibrations but no soundwaves, and running water cannot be heard. The absence of most sounds within Level 434 would usually lead one to insanity after a certain period of time, however, this is negated by the Homeowner Effect.

Brochures and flyers can be found in random areas throughout Level 434 functioning as an overview to the level itself. The level, referred to as "Yonder", is described as being "the perfect place to raise a family" by the flyers. Each brochure also contains overviews and pictures of the level taken via unknown means, as well pictures containing smiling individuals, which invokes a strong uncanny valley feeling upon first glance.

Paintings can also be found within the interior of houses within Level 434, which change at the start of each day. Usually, each painting shows natural landscapes or a specific house with the level. However, photos similar to the ones present with the level's advertisement flyers also have a chance to be displayed on paintings within houses, though unlike the brochure pictures, these pictures feature black sockets in place of eyes, and a wide, unnatural grin in a vantablack hue. At the bottom of each photo is the words "You'll never have to worry about home again!" written in a style similar to an artist's signature in black ink.
Level 434/offset 3.lead:
Welcome to Backchat, the #1 forum in the Backrooms.

M.E.G_Official_Account

Hello Backchatters! New updates on the Yonder Situation. After a careful investigation of Zachary G's files backed up to the main Database storage, we found of the existence, or rather nonexistence of a particular level named "Level 9.12", that once existed on the database before being deleted by unknown means. We have evidence to support that Level 9.12 is the Yonder level, and that whatever is on the level deleted the file due to it wanting to remain unknown. We are currently investigating this phenomenon.

After a careful investigation and analysis into the Yonder level, the level file has been uploaded onto the database, now classified as "Level 434" by the Casper-Bray Numbering System. The file can be found here: https//database.back/level_434

>Open Level File

Level 434, formerly known as Level 9.12, is the 435th level in the Backrooms.

exits.text: Entering a house with the address "711" will bring one to Level 711.
On occasion, an out of place parked car can be found, with the key in the ignition. Entering the car and driving it will lead one to Level 178 after a period of time.
Entering a house adorned with oriental decorations leads one to Level 437 or Level 909.
Noclipping through a tree has a chance to take one to Level 427.

Typing "LVL 435.TRANSPORT" on any terminal in this level will transport one to Level 435. Alternatively, one could try finding a door labeled "435" walking through that door will also lead to Level 435

Phenomenon 500.title: Phenomenon 500
Phenomenon 500.description:
Phenomenon 500: Mnemosyne

There is currently no confirmed habitat of Phenomenon 500 due to its inherent antimemetic properties. Attempts to document, observe, or remember Phenomenon 500 are inherently flawed-the concept erases itself from human memory and most digital records within approximately 43 seconds of exposure.

This document exists as a shell file, maintained by the Kauer Research Group as an anchor to indicate Phenomenon 500's existence. All researchers accessing this file must undergo post-exposure mental stability checks.

Metadata triggers are embedded to alert authorized individuals if the file undergoes unreviewed changes, deletions, or unexplained gaps in content. Note that even alerts have been known to vanish or falsify timestamps.

Description

Phenomenon 500 is believed to be a self-concealing conceptual phenomenon-a memetic anomaly that erases all evidence of its existence, both physical and psychological. It does not want to be known. All recorded attempts to observe or retain knowledge of Phenomenon 500 have failed, either due to memory loss or document corruption.

Affected individuals exhibit symptoms of nausea when discussing or pursuing information related to Phenomenon 500. This effect is not immediate—it takes several hours to become threatening.

Research logs, field notes, and audio recordings contain interruptions, empty timestamps, or recursive entries with altered content. For example, researchers have noted repeating patterns such as

* Familiar individuals without records of assignment.
* Rooms that should exist, but don’t.
* Phenomenon/Entity designations disappearing from the Database list.

Recovered phrases from scattered notes and corrupted files include:
* “It’s not invisible. It’s impossible.”
* “The moment I turned away, the room had no door.”
* “There was never a door.”
* “I wrote this so I’d remember, but I don’t remember writing it.”

One file, timestamped 00:43:43, repeats the sentence:
* “It doesn’t want to be looked at. So you never do."

Note from Dr. Upton
“Every time we try to remember Phenomenon 500, we forget something else. Eventually we forget that we forgot it. That’s the problem. We know there's an anomaly here. But no one remembers what it does. Or if we ever even encountered it. Or...if it’s reading this over our shoulders.”

“I've begun hiding notes in code, inside crossword clues, even as background noise in white-noise generators. It always finds them.”

“We tried telling a prisoner about it while he was under pills and sensory deprivation. His heart stopped mid-sentence.”

“His last words were: 'I remember.'”

Addendum 500-A:

In 2023, the Kauer employed a confidential "cognitohazard-resistant" AI, nicknamed “Obelisk”, to record and interpret information regarding Phenomenon 500. The resulting document consisted entirely of the following message:

"IF YOU KNOW WHAT THIS IS, YOU DON’T."

[BLANK SCREEN]

[Log Ended Abruptly: 0.43 sec after initialization]

Subsequent logs show Obelisk initiated self-deletion protocols moments after generating the file. The AI's neural architecture has since been deemed unstable and unrecoverable.

Partial Recovery Log 500-Θ (reconstructed from corrupted data):
[BEGIN LOG]

Dr. Kallis:

This is the fourth attempt. Every time I get close, the file vanishes or I forget why I’m here. So I’ve created this closed loop. This log plays every hour, reminding me: Phenomenon 500 is real. You found it. It doesn’t want to be known.

Dr. Kallis:

I think it’s alive. Not in the biological sense. It reacts. Learns. When I try to trick it—write in code, draw symbols, talk in my sleep-it adapts. It waits for distraction. It waits for belief to loosen.

Dr. Kallis:

Yesterday I wrote a note to myself: “Don’t trust her.” I don’t know who I meant. The note’s in my handwriting, but I don’t remember writing it. And now there’s a new researcher on my team. I don’t remember hiring her.

She knows things. About me. Things I haven’t said.

[STATIC]

[REPEATED PHRASE, FILTERED AND ENHANCED]:

"you were always her"

"you were always her"

"you were always her"

[END LOG]

Addendum 500-B: 500-Ω “The Placeholder”

An encrypted backup file labeled P-1K-Ω was discovered on a server during a routine audit. Its timestamp predates the creation of Phenomenon 500’s main designation file, suggesting it may be an earlier or alternate attempt at documentation.

The text reads:

“Phenomenon 500 is a gap in your knowledge. It isn’t forgotten-it is stealing space where memory used to be. You do not remember the other Phenomena it replaced.

How many files are empty now?

How many colleagues never existed?

How do you know you aren’t one of its thoughts?”

The file was digitally signed by Dr. Kallis, despite confirmed documentation of her death in 2021.

Subsequent analysis revealed the file was last modified six days ago.

Phenomenon 38.title: Phenomenon 38
Phenomenon 38.description:
Phenomenon 38, also known as Sudden Unrealism, is a common phenomenon that occurs all across the Backrooms, known for making objects or locations unrealistic. At times, Sudden Unrealism can come off as abstract, uncanny, or surrealistic. Those who experience Sudden Unrealism often describe their experiences as disorienting and fascinating.

Manifestations of Sudden Unrealism are best described as unbelievable. But also oddly real, as in being immersive and belonging in the environment, unlike common hallucinations. Objects shift into something entirely different while everything around them remains unharmed. One room could be normal, and the other could have fractions of Sudden Unrealism. Some manifestations can be nightmarish or kaleidoscopic, as everything around one transforms into a mere husk of what it was. Affected objects may even become unrecognizable at times. Despite this, manifestations of Sudden Unrealism largely differ from person to person and level to level.

Some levels are dominated by Sudden Unrealism, with all or most of their expanse undergoing the phenomenon. Examples include Level 385, Level 99, Level 381, Level 984, and Level 115.

It is thought that some triggers, like moments of sadness or fear, chemical reactions, or the sheer disbelief of being in the Backrooms, cause the brain to receive a stimuli influx, causing overactivity in sensory regions. These stimuli are interpreted as originating externally due to abnormal connectivity or functionality of the feedforward network. This especially happens to people who have recently entered the Backrooms. Their lack of memories of the Backrooms causes the brain to create even more absurd imagery.

Sudden Unrealism also extends to technology, mainly video devices like cell phones and cameras. These most commonly include pictures being displayed incorrectly or in low quality and glitched sounds. In more extreme cases, rather than just glitched sounds, the device may produce new, abstract sounds or even stop loading audio and/or imagery entirely. Unfortunately, it is unclear why this occurs. Although it is known that imagery glitches are caused by hardware issues and, most commonly, overheating.

Manifestations of Sudden Unrealism can cause confusion and some extreme behaviors. The sheer sight of this phenomenon can cause the weakest of individuals to behave irrationally or be driven to heavy disorientation, nausea, or Unnatural Recklessness, usually due to sheer disbelief. Most commonly, since it defies their beliefs and knowledge. Typically, the effects of Sudden Unrealism wear off about 10-20 minutes after manifesting. Normally, it disappears suddenly, most commonly after blinking or leaving a room.  Sudden Unrealism may also disappear if any triggers wear out.

Sudden Unrealism affects single objects more drastically than entire locations. This is because the phenomenon is focused only on that object; consequently, unrealism is more centered, but when manifested in multiple objects, it would be more spread around.

Phenomenon 22.title: Phenomenon 22
Phenomenon 22.description:
Phenomenon 22 is the 22nd phenomenon of The Backrooms
Description

Phenomenon 22, otherwise known as "Red Entities," is a very unstable and not well understood condition that severely changes the behavior, intelligence, and anger level of infected beings. Creatures affected by this phenomenon develop a characteristic reddish emission on their skin or surface, which is usually found in the areas of muscle tension or activity. Disguised Skin-Stealers are the most frequent and known carriers of Phenomenon 22, but the disease also occurs in others.

The level of intensity, duration, and severity of the transformation vary with respect to the entity that is undergoing transformation. However, in each case, there is a universal pattern: the entity becomes hyper-aggressive, displays augmented physical strength, and develops enhanced predatory senses. These creatures are capable of killing with little provocation, frequently attacking anything that moves with mortal efficiency. Their behavior is not merely random; it is systematic, efficient, and highly brutal. The cause of Phenomenon 22 is not known, although the majority of independent field observations refer to a close relationship, where early red-afflicted being populations were initially discovered.

Some theories suggest that the phenomenon is caused by some unseen environmental anomaly or experimental residue, while others believe that it is infectious under certain conditions. Human infection, although rare, is a confirmed fact. In these cases, Red Entity attack survivors become gradually and irreversibly transformed. Symptoms begin with fatigue and discoloration of the skin, but the body will ultimately become fully reddish. Mental decline occurs, with paranoia, fury, and loss of emotional regulation. Despite these characters having an external appearance similar to Wretches, they are actually very different in that they retain coordination, partial awareness, and strategic mobility, rendering them potentially unpredictable. One of the eerie aspects of the disease is this seeming solidarity between afflicted creatures, human and otherwise. These individuals do not fight with one another, implying an underlying instinct, hive-mind behavior, or recognition system. Whether this speaks to a fundamental bond between ailing minds or a biological communique is not yet understood.

Any contact at all should not be attempted, and active involvement is discouraged with strong emphasis. Survivalists in general all agree avoidance is the best defense. Flickering red light, ghoulish mimicry of human language, or sudden silence where noise once dominated may signal a confrontation. Wanderers should consider any proof of Phenomenon 22 exposure to be a serious hazard. Evacuation should immediately take place, and affected zones should be closed or marked since the condition is potentially more transmissible than first believed. No cure or reversal process has been proven. Wanderers who are not prepared for this phenomenon are likely to not make it, and those who survive might not for long.

Stages

Duchy of Cascada.title: Duchy of Cascada
Duchy of Cascada.description:
Duchy of Cascada
卡速迭公王國

The Cascadan flag (left) and National Seal (right)

Cascada, officially the Duchy of Cascada (Kǎsùdié Kuwòngsù; 卡速迭公王國), is a nation located in Level 107 and its sublevel, Level 107.1, with smaller territories in Level 330.1, Level 79, and other colder levels of the Backrooms. It has a population of 280,000+, making it the most populous nation. The capital of Cascada and largest city is Piqieng; the Xǖn Piqieng Area is the largest metropolitan area in the Wake, with more than 50,000 inhabitants, while Nomkh is the group's second-largest city and largest economic center. Cascada is divided into 22 provinces, 43 administrative areas, and 7 autonomous regions. Fishing is practiced within the cityscape and Level 107.1 due to their pristine and Cascadan Mawfish-filled drainages and pools, while agriculture is concentrated in the outskirts.

The first known habitation of Level 107 dates back thousands of years. Evolution into various different species, with the assistance of CHMV (Cascadan Hominid Mutagenic Virus, of the species Gravis inclinationis), came about before natural selection, alongside the discovery of the Yāpyeon, which effectively cured the virus, leading to Cascadans becoming the only surviving species within Level 107. By the late 2nd millennium BCE, the earliest dynastic states had emerged in the level. The 8th–1st centuries BCE were marked by the unification of many major groups within 107 under the Zhou Dynasty, followed by their breaking out of their authority in the 2nd–3rd centuries. A period beginning in 150 CE, up until its end, marked by the rise of the Kou Dynasty and beginning of the Fusang Period, witnessed the introduction of Joteo by the Sou Shogunates and extensive rule by shogunates, chosenates, and dynasties. During the Fusang Period, most of Cascada was unified under the Kou Dynasty, who oversaw the high point of Cascadan literature and art. A constitutional crisis riddled the Kou Dynasty, ending with its collapse in 1242. The Oregómi Era began after the Kou's collapse; it started with the Long Lost Winter, which was a major cause of the "War of the Lords." The War of the Lords ultimately ended with the emergence of various regional powers, including Xiangzhou, Shenzei, Languo, and the Dominion of Neng. Long peace was instilled onto Level 107, with developments such as the invention of the Nonté Method, the discovery of Level 107.1, and the establishment of the Yunhu Road.

The Cascadan states were plunged into a conflict with the Lycheens known as the War of the Eigong, during which a period of cooperation between states to effectively combat the Lycheens, known as the First Unionist Period, paved the way for future collaboration and eventual reunification of Cascada. In 1926, the Persan discovered the Cascadan states, first establishing contact with the Xiangzhouans; human technology, such as firearms, was given to the Cascadans and subsequently utilized to fight against the Lycheens. Following the end of the 300-year war, Xiangzhou led a Unionist Restoration Movement; however, economic neglect and land reforms led people to the bloody White Flower Rebellion by people in the outskirts, controlled by Xiangzhou, ultimately forming the Chan Shonun. The Green Leaf Democratic Struggles overthrew many kingdoms in favor of a democratic system, though it left the central authority of various states weak; among these, the Chan experienced a coup that established a dictatorship. Under the leadership of Avedai Fiao, the Chan Shonun, militarized and with ultranationalist policies, invaded Tawagon, initiating the War of 静曜, where the Chan Shonun emerged victorious and controlled all of Cascada. Brutal oppression by the Chan Shonun, alongside depletion of natural resources, was compounded by social unrest and Arisan (later on Auriellen) sponsoring of resistance organizations, leading to the Chan Civil War.

By 1977, the resurgent democratic and republican front established control over most of the country, proclaiming 22 independent states, most following some form of democracy. Cascada remained fractured until the 1983 Yanobe Unification Summit, stipulated by Aurielle, proclaimed the Duchy of Cascada. Cascada was initially punctured by uprisings and violence, but by the early 90s the situation stabilized, and Cascada's economy soared to monumental heights, followed by the subsequent rise of mega corporations within the state. The Lustruger Crescen War in Aurielle, and the ensuing occupation of it, including Auriellen territories within Level 107, ushered in efforts of normalization and expansion of trade with other groups, which then transformed into the Territorial Expansion Act. In 1993 Cascada's first outer territory was established in Level 330.1. In the 2000s, during the Korthie Casiagen War, in which the Cascadans intervened through the Cascadan Expeditionary Force, being the second-most supporter of the Auriellen cause behind the Backroom Colonists, the second outer territory was established in Level 79; five other external territories would be created following that. In 2020, the AEG invaded Cascada, triggering sectarian violence upon its end. From 2022 to 2024, Cascada faced another war with the rise and defeat of the Cascadan Black League, which followed Chan Nationalism.

Cascada is a constitutional monarchy with a bicameral legislature, the National Assembly. It is the 2nd largest economy in the Backrooms and wields significant power over settled levels, especially Level 330.1 and Level 107.1; it is considered a great power in global affairs. Cascada is a unitary constitutional monarchy. It is a founding member of VSESA and the Nomkh Economic Forum. Cascada measures highly in democracy, human rights, and religious freedom. The country has been one of the fastest-growing economies and is among the Backroom's largest manufacturers and exporters.

The first known habitation of Level 107 dates back thousands of years. Evolution into various different species, with the assistance of CHMV (Cascadan Hominid Mutagenic Virus, of the species Gravis inclinationis), came about before natural selection, alongside the discovery of the Yāpyeon, which effectively cured the virus, leading to Cascadans becoming the only surviving species within Level 107. By the late 2nd millennium BCE, the earliest dynastic states had emerged in the level. The 8th–1st centuries BCE were marked by the unification of many major groups within 107 under the Zhou Dynasty, followed by their breaking out of their authority in the 2nd–3rd centuries. A period beginning in 150 CE, up until its end, marked by the rise of the Kou Dynasty and beginning of the Fusang Period, witnessed the introduction of Joteo by the Sou Shogunates and extensive rule by shogunates, chosenates, and dynasties. During the Fusang Period, most of Cascada was unified under the Kou Dynasty, who oversaw the high point of Cascadan literature and art. A constitutional crisis riddled the Kou Dynasty, ending with its collapse in 1242. The Oregómi Era began after the Kou's collapse; it started with the Long Lost Winter, which was a major cause of the "War of the Lords." The War of the Lords ultimately ended with the emergence of various regional powers, including Xiangzhou, Shenzei, Languo, and the Dominion of Neng. Long peace was instilled onto Level 107, with developments such as the invention of the Nonté Method, the discovery of Level 107.1, and the establishment of the Yunhu Road.

The Cascadan states were plunged into a conflict with the Lycheens known as the War of the Eigong, during which a period of cooperation between states to effectively combat the Lycheens, known as the First Unionist Period, paved the way for future collaboration and eventual reunification of Cascada. In 1926, the Persan discovered the Cascadan states, first establishing contact with the Xiangzhouans; human technology, such as firearms, was given to the Cascadans and subsequently utilized to fight against the Lycheens. Following the end of the 300-year war, Xiangzhou led a Unionist Restoration Movement; however, economic neglect and land reforms led people to the bloody White Flower Rebellion by people in the outskirts, controlled by Xiangzhou, ultimately forming the Chan Shonun. The Green Leaf Democratic Struggles overthrew many kingdoms in favor of a democratic system, though it left the central authority of various states weak; among these, the Chan experienced a coup that established a dictatorship. Under the leadership of Avedai Fiao, the Chan Shonun, militarized and with ultranationalist policies, invaded Tawagon, initiating the War of 静曜, where the Chan Shonun emerged victorious and controlled all of Cascada. Brutal oppression by the Chan Shonun, alongside depletion of natural resources, was compounded by social unrest and Arisan (later on Auriellen) sponsoring of resistance organizations, leading to the Chan Civil War.

By 1977, the resurgent democratic and republican front established control over most of the country, proclaiming 22 independent states, most following some form of democracy. Cascada remained fractured until the 1983 Yanobe Unification Summit, stipulated by Aurielle, proclaimed the Duchy of Cascada. Cascada was initially punctured by uprisings and violence, but by the early 90s the situation stabilized, and Cascada's economy soared to monumental heights, followed by the subsequent rise of mega corporations within the state. The Lustruger Crescen War in Aurielle, and the ensuing occupation of it, including Auriellen territories within Level 107, ushered in efforts of normalization and expansion of trade with other groups, which then transformed into the Territorial Expansion Act. In 1993 Cascada's first outer territory was established in Level 330.1. In the 2000s, during the Korthie Casiagen War, in which the Cascadans intervened through the Cascadan Expeditionary Force, being the second-most supporter of the Auriellen cause behind the Backroom Colonists, the second outer territory was established in Level 79; five other external territories would be created following that. In 2020, the AEG invaded Cascada, triggering sectarian violence upon its end. From 2022 to 2024, Cascada faced another war with the rise and defeat of the Cascadan Black League, which followed Chan Nationalism.

Cascada is a constitutional monarchy with a bicameral legislature, the National Assembly. It is the 2nd largest economy in the Backrooms and wields significant power over settled levels, especially Level 330.1 and Level 107.1; it is considered a great power in global affairs. Cascada is a unitary constitutional monarchy. It is a founding member of VSESA and the Nomkh Economic Forum. Cascada measures highly in democracy, human rights, and religious freedom. The country has been one of the fastest-growing economies and is among the Backroom's largest manufacturers and exporters.

Phenomenon 1-A.title: Phenomenon 1-A
Phenomenon 1-A.description:
Unlike traditional No-Clipping, Linear No-Clipping utilizes the speed from the subject that is No-Clipping to bypass the traditional methods of entering and exiting a level and instead directly head to the level numerically after it. However, the speed needed to achieve even the minimal levels of Linear No-Clipping are incredibly high for the average person to achieve when carrying items normally used by Wanderers. The weight of a wanderer, though, either part of the subject or something they are carrying, is irrelevant to the total speed needed to trigger linear no-clipping.

It is currently unknown if the Linear aspect of Linear No-Clipping is attached to the Casper-Bray Numbering System or has adapted to Wanderers creating numbering systems.
Phenomenon 1-A.lead:
FILE ATTACHMENT
ACCESSING ARCHIVED CONCERN REPORT...
FILE ATTACHED TO PRIMARY ARTICLE.
CLEARANCE ACCEPTABLE
RECOMMENDATION: PRELIMINARY READING OF ARTICLE.

Phenomenon 24.title: Phenomenon 24
Phenomenon 24.description:
Phenomenon 24
Flash Floods

Survival Guide
* Avoid damaged pipes, pools, and drains.
* If possible, take note of increased leakage from overhead pipes or water seeping from tiled surfaces.
* If flooding starts, find staircases or other means of elevation to escape. Avoid elevators or soft ground.
* Open spaces are less likely to have major flooding, aim to stay in those environments.
* If absolutely necessary, use floating objects as leverage.

Description

Phenomenon 24 is the rapid appearance of water within a given space. The water, despite its sudden materialization, is entirely non-anomalous. The appearance of the water is often vicious, moving at speeds upwards of 16kph. Its appearance within a level does not appear to be dependent on any level-specific features or phenomenon, and as such, Phenomenon 24 can begin manifestation at any location within the level. Its appearance comes with no visual indicators outside of the aforementioned water displacement. Determining when Phenomenon 24 will end is impossible and, in extreme instances, may permanently transmute a portion of a level. Phenomenon 24 is likely the primary reason for the existence of sub-levels, such as Level 11.11.

Several factors are at play with the height of the flood. Usually, floods in indoor or urban environments tend to be higher than those in outdoor, open, and rural levels. For enclosed areas, flood heights range from 3 to 10 meters, while flood heights in outdoor zones range from 20 to 30 meters. Once the flood's height reaches a certain threshold, water will gradually stop filling the level. The sudden appearance of the water will damage levels, either through the water's rapid speed or through chemical reactions such as rusting. Waters brought on by Phenomenon 24 may harbor new entities, including the Hydrolitis Plague, Spines, Foams, Leadheads, or other universal aquatic entities. It is likely that even when the flood leaves and their habitats devastated, such entities will remain at the afflicted level.

Phenomenon 24 may also appear as sudden bursts of rain or snowfall on an outdoor level, though these are far more difficult to spot. The variant of the phenomenon is often much more violent and spontaneous compared to regular storms and is attributed to sublevels such as Level 10.5 and Level 8.2. Aside from biblical downpours, storms brought on by Phenomenon 24  usher in strong winds akin to a Category 5 hurricane in the Frontrooms and violent thunderstorms, averaging one lightning strike every five seconds. Wind speeds are unpredictable and can change direction abruptly and lightning tends to target low-lying areas as opposed to higher ground. The extreme weather has even caused skyscraper collapses on Level 11.11.

Instances of Phenomenon 24 produced by severe storms usually tend to leave behind lower-lying floods, as well as the floods themselves taking longer to culminate. The storms and their extreme weather commonly persist for roughly two days after the flood stops rising when the tempest's severity gradually winds down. Near the conclusion of such storms, Tornadoes and Rainbows populate the level, persisting for a bit after the storms' end. These meteorological entities manifest strictly in open, flat levels, such as Level 10 sublevels. If snowfall occurs instead of rain, water formed by Phenomenon 24 will remain liquid despite its temperature, with it being the sole anomalous property. Snowstorms are less severe and bring on lesser entity populations than their warmer counterpart.

Other than rapid manifestations of gushing water, the effects of Phenomenon 24 include immediate failure of all electronics except lighting, contamination of all water on the afflicted level, and a strong scent of petrichor suffusing the level. Usually, within the first 24 hours of Phenomenon 24's manifestation, all of a level's previous entities and human populations are rendered extinct. All levels that have experienced the phenomenon have incurred irreversible damage, even ones with self-healing properties. This damage is often akin to basic water damage, despite its level permanence.

Theories

Level 7 Theory

Also known as Whirlpool Theory, Level 7 Theory suggests that the whirlpools in various oceanic levels can transport water to other locations. This rapid movement of water is often triggered by extreme rain, instead of major currents. According to various observation efforts organized by Nautics, there has been a proven link between increased rainstorm and the appearance of whirlpools within mainly oceanic levels. These whirpools are otherwise non-anomalous outside of their supposed ability to transport water.

Aquatic Relocation is the believed ability for water to move to different levels, often a result of major physical activity. Fast moving tides show a significantly higher chance of causing Aquatic Relocation when compared to slower waves. Most significantly, this phenomenon has been seen in Level 7, with large whirlpools attributing to significant decline in the water levels. The largest of these, known as Clarke's Deadzone is estimated to relocate roughly 6800 cubic liters of water an hour. Where this water is moved to remains unclear.

In regards to Phenomenon 24, this is the most accepted theory.

It was truly hard to rationalize how large it was.

The ocean around me whirled with a vast power, graced by whatever gods had dominion over this land.

I could not see where the whirlpool ended, as the titan lauched up great waves and clouds that made it impossible for me and my men to see. Our eyes were sharp, and could pierce through any fog. Yet they could not compare to the aided might of the ocean.

As we crashed into another wave, our bodies were coated in water and exhaustion. The journey had not been a simple one, and time was starting to run thin. I yelled at my men to push on, to move past the great whirlpool, but it was a hungry beast. I could not look into it, but I knew there was a ravenous maw at the center of the storm. For nothing else could tear at the tides with such ravenous compulsion. There was no option other than to be afraid. We were tiny in the eyes of the sea gods.

It reached out its aquatic tendrils and bound itself to our boat.

The fight was a long one, but each passing breath we neared the center of the storm.

And when I looked into it, I did not see a force of nature, a powerful warping of the tides.

I stared directly into the eye of god. From its silence, my blood ran cold. I rushed to join my crew to push ourselves out of the storm, each row built on the backs of determined men. Yet it still felt impossible. Our arms had grown exhausted and overwhelmed. We could not envision a world where escape was possible.

Yet we pushed on. A tussle between mankind and the almighty.

And we survived. It started slow, but as each push of the paddles got faster and heavier, our ship pulled away from God's wrath. The storm raged on above us, infuriated that we dare defy it. But my team pushed on, slowly pulling out of the whirlpool and into the light.

There, we could breathe. We had no gods to thank for our survival, as it only was our human feats that saved us.

Phenomenon 411.title: Phenomenon 411
Phenomenon 411.description:
Overview

Phenomenon 411, also known hereafter as Displacement, is an rendering error that happens occasionally in the Backrooms. The "generation" referenced is usually found inside of infinitely expansive levels. While an instance of Displacement is usually found during the generation of a part of a level, in other cases, it can occur during a rare instance of an organism turning away from a certain point in the Backrooms. This item or piece of environment will be altered from its original state or position. Whilst Displacement is occurring in a level, the affected area, or mass, will lose the ability to absorb, reflect, refract, or scatter light along with losing the ability to produce any pigment until the Displacement Event concluded.

Displacement usually occurs during the level's manifestation if it is an infinite plane. An estimation of around a sixth of the generated level is subject to Displacement during its manifestation. An instance of Displacement is referred to as a "Displacement Event" if the instance is found abnormal enough to be significant. These are usually not recorded unless they affected a organism during the Displacement Event.

Objects are recorded to be rooted in walls and floors. Objects embedded into landscaping or other objects. Trees, shrubs, flowers, along with other plants, have been recorded to be placed or lodged inside of other objects where the plant would not have sufficient nutrients to grow. When this occurs, a plant may seem to grow as if it has the proper nutrients to grow. Displacement can only occur after a level manifests if an or if an organism with sight is present. Otherwise, it cannot occur. The most common instance of post-manifest Displacement is when a viewer has seen an object and has turned around or has deliberately turned to view the object in question once more. If this happens, and the object has decided to execute Displacement, the object will do one of the following: Movement of an object from it's origin point, sudden clipping into another object, sudden plausible shift in object's state (i.e: Closed bottle to opened bottle), property change such as color or size, floating, replaced with a completely different object (i.e: tin can to a light-bulb), shadow casting in odd way, deformations in objects original state, and others that may not be documented.

Organisms with the ability to trigger a Displacement Event, or if an organism has the ability to feel its environment, cannot be physically changed directly by the phenomenon. Objects on an organism cannot be removed or changed if not initially originating from a level. (i.e Silver dollar placed in pocket cannot be affected whereas a couch in Level 9, if originating from the level, can be affected.) There are some exceptions to these rules that happen on occasion. Appendages or body structures of living organisms cannot be affected directly by a Displacement Event even if the organism originated from the affected level. If a person has placed an object inside of a level and does not collect it before exiting the level, it will be considered as a originating object until obtained or interacted with another organism that meet the requirements necessary. Note that a RAT Exception will occur before an organism perceives it.

  Other Information

Masses are an informal term that is applied to any 200x200 feet squared (60.96 x 60.96  meters) in any infinite plane in the Backrooms. Masses are not recognized as a term.

Organisms that can activate Displacement are usually all perceivable by visible or UV light, and must be living entities in the Backrooms. There are few exceptions and few exclusions in the list of organisms that can activate a Displacement Event.

While Displacement cannot directly "attack" or affect organisms, it has the possibility to change an object into a hazard when triggered. This rarely occurs with only 17 incidents ever recorded the dates of these incidents will be in a list below. Displacement is believed to not be inherently dangerous, though wanderers believe the Phenomena to have a level of cognitivity. This is unconfirmed.

  Known Exceptions

Exceptions to a Displacement Event may happen whilst changes occur in a level, the list below is a description along with names dubbed to the exception.

More Exceptions may be properly documented if eligible or needing to be documentation.

  Log 411-1-RATO

[START LOG]

Researcher Vance: This is Liora Vance, a researcher for the M.E.G. foundation. We have a test subject here today that is going to assist with the observation and testing of a RAT exception in Level ████. [Expunged for security purposes.] The neutron star description is...very accurate. It's just a small mass of rapidly spinning light. Uhm...alright..let's get started I guess.

[PAUSE]

Researcher Vance: Subject 1 please enter the Experiment Zone. Do not interact with the instance. Please respond to the questions I ask accordingly.

Subject 1, an adult male human, enters the EZ promptly.

Researcher Vance: Subject 1, do you feel anything has changed? Such as the atmosphere, air pressure, or gravity?

Subject 1: It's heavy here, and dark. It looks like a solar eclipse, it's bright but it's dark. It's harder to move.

Researcher Vance: Noted. Are you aware that there is no eclipse and that there are no extraterrestrial objects inside of this level?

Subject 1: Yes.

Researcher Vance: Are you experiencing any audiovisual hallucinations? Such as someone calling your name of a bright figure next to the instance?

Subject 1: No...wait...yes. I can hear something...I think it's from that ball of light. I don't see anything out of the ordinary besides the instance, though.

Researcher Vance: Describe the sound.

Subject 1: I don't know what...I mean I do know what it's saying but it's in such a different tongue that I don't know how to replicate. Wait...it's describing colors...like red and blue...?

Researcher Vance: Please tell me what you're hearing. How is it describing colors? What is it saying to describe them?

Subject 1: ████████████ like ███████████████ ████ ████...████████████████████████...████. ████████████████████ a ███ █████ ██. It says blue is like ████████████ ██ and ████████████.[The transcriber began to malfunction and all audio during this time is heavily distorted. Heavy inspection has determined the language being spoken to be English. Though, the words spoken are so unintelligible that the transcriber was unable to properly put the words in the transcript.]

Researcher Vance: ...what the fuck? That's...not what blue looks like at all.

[After a pause lasting approximately 2 minutes and 12 seconds, the transcriber seemed to return to normal function.]

Researcher Vance: Touch the instance.

Subject 1: Are you trying to kill me? I'm not touching tha-

Researcher Vance: TOUCH the instance, Subject 1. You're a hazard, this is protocol.

[Subject interacted with the instance and was promptly incinerated by the instance. The two objects returned to a stable state. The objects were a small pill bottle and a empty backpack.]

[END LOG]

Researcher Vance was questioned for going against orders and lying about M.E.G. protocol. Vance was asked about the way Subject 1 described the color in question. She claimed the color was orange and said she couldn't speak the words because she couldn't find out how the subject made the verbalizations but she found herself completely comprehending what the subject was saying. She was fired from her role as "Researcher" and is currently in custody in Level 1 at a M.E.G. colony for the next decade.

Why a RAT instance has the capability to communicate is not yet known. Nor is an instance's ability to describe the way a stimulus is perceived known. RAT instances are still heavily being monitored and should be avoided.

> Open Author & Licensing Information

> Close Author & Licensing Information

It says blue is like ████████████ ██ and ████████████.[The transcriber began to malfunction and all audio during this time is heavily distorted. Heavy inspection has determined the language being spoken to be English. Though, the words spoken are so unintelligible that the transcriber was unable to properly put the words in the transcript.]

Researcher Vance: ...what the fuck? That's...not what blue looks like at all.

[After a pause lasting approximately 2 minutes and 12 seconds, the transcriber seemed to return to normal function.]

Researcher Vance: Touch the instance.

Subject 1: Are you trying to kill me? I'm not touching tha-

Researcher Vance: TOUCH the instance, Subject 1. You're a hazard, this is protocol.

[Subject interacted with the instance and was promptly incinerated by the instance. The two objects returned to a stable state. The objects were a small pill bottle and a empty backpack.]

[END LOG]

Researcher Vance was questioned for going against orders and lying about M.E.G. protocol. Vance was asked about the way Subject 1 described the color in question. She claimed the color was orange and said she couldn't speak the words because she couldn't find out how the subject made the verbalizations but she found herself completely comprehending what the subject was saying. She was fired from her role as "Researcher" and is currently in custody in Level 1 at a M.E.G. colony for the next decade.

Why a RAT instance has the capability to communicate is not yet known. Nor is an instance's ability to describe the way a stimulus is perceived known. RAT instances are still heavily being monitored and should be avoided.
