Level 405.title: Level 405
Level 405.description: Level 405, also known as the "Perennial Galleria", is a dual-subsection level featuring both indoor and outdoor sections, housed within a large, well disguised industrial complex.
Level 405.lead:
Level 405 is  the 406th level of The Backrooms.

| per·en·ni·al ( /pəˈrenēəl/ ) : lasting or existing for a long or apparently infinite time; enduring or continually recurring. |

[ Please play this audio file for a more entertaining reading experience! ]

exits.text: Descending an escalator on Level 405 with a sign reading "Going Down..." on it leads to Level 524.
Going deep into Level 405 can possibly lead to anywhere in The Backrooms.
Exiting a "Staff Only" door in the food court can possibly backtrack to The Delta Mall.
Jumping into a fountain with a translucent white bottom noclips one to Level 37.
Exiting through a tight vent in the exterior facade walls lead to Level 194.
Noclipping through a fountain in sends one to Level 0.8.

Phenomenon 60.title: Phenomenon 60

Phenomenon 9-A.title: Phenomenon 9-A
Phenomenon 9-A.description:
> Open Credits

> Close Credits

Written by Roddy.
little gif thingy also by me (roddy) :>
Concept and tale by Abhi.
"Now, create a phenemenon where humans sometimes
have the ability for things they imagine to become real.
You'll make the description, I'll make the tale."

|| OVERVIEW ||

Thoughtformation is a very rare, wish-fulfilling phenomenon, that has only ever been documented a handful of times. Thoughtformation, or Phenomenon 9-A, is a phenomenon where depending on the state of mind of a wanderer, their will can bleed into reality, creating a manifestation of their desire. Any matter that may be created from this phenomenon originates from The Broken and its manifestations can take on many forms depending on the interpretation of the affected wanderer's thoughts, similar to a prophecy of sorts. A determined wanderer on the brink of death from walking the winding roads of Level 91, in a moment of desperation, may unintentionally manifest a bottle of Almond Water. But occasionally, the phenomenon could instead cruelly summon a hound in the face of a wanderer wishing to no longer be alone. Phenomenon 9-A is largely thought to be entirely random in when it occurs, but there have been wanderers and supposed experts in the past who claimed to be able to harness the phenomenon as their own, and even control how their constructs were interpreted and implemented. However, most claims of control over Thoughtformation have eventually been disproven or have otherwise remained unconfirmed.

Thoughtformation has close echoes to The Kauer Thesis, but the phenomenon can never affect anyone within the The Frontrooms, due to its reliance on psychic energy that cannot permeate through the void. Its rare and often localized effects have led to Phenomenon 9-A's classification as a variant of Tulpaefication, but while Tulpaefication can create rather powerful entities with the collective belief of many, the upper limits of what Phenomenon 9-A is capable of are not fully understood. Its ability to manifest in many different ways, and on a variety of scales, makes the potential of this phenomenon seemingly endless.

|| CAUSES ||

Similarly to Phenomenon 9, psychic radiation is the main component to Thoughtformation. While Tulpaefication is a passive effect of psychic radiation build-ups within the Achospic Plane, Phenomenon 9-A acts as an active effect of build-ups that occur when a large amount of psychic radiation is suddenly dumped in a place that already has an excess of it. This excess energy is quickly expended in order to return the balance, which triggers a chain reaction leading to a Thoughtformation event.
This makes the phenomenon quite rare, due to psychic energy often spreading evenly throughout the backrooms and Achospic Plane, making the conditions that must be met for a Thoughtformation event—very situational.

|| CASES ||

While the phenomenon is exceedingly rare, there are still many profound cases and tales of Thoughtformation, and it is even been theorized that The Flicker was caused by a major Thoughtformation event triggered by a desperate researcher within Level 6.5

On the day of April 17th, 2023, Backrooms Today published a feature detailing the story of a woman going by the moniker, "Rosemord." Word quickly went around of the wanderer who had become the only survivor among 75 victims after miraculously escaping the Red Rooms. This feature, and Rosemord's account of the level, would prove invaluable in the research of the Red Rooms, except for one detail that was amiss. The woman hadn't actually found an exit-rather in a moment of vitality and will, she had created one.

In an interview conducted by Scut Wakante, Rosemord detailed her experience within the Red Rooms.

Scut Wakante : "How did you exit the Red Rooms, it's a dead-end is it not?"

Scut Wakante : "Do you have any idea what happened leading up to your escape?"

Rosemord : "My memory was fuzzy for a while after I left, but I think now I can recall most of it."

Scut Wakante : "Take your time, Ms. Rose."

Rosemord : "Hmph. . .I remember being very thirsty; coulda' drank a river. I was slouched over on the ground, just waiting for it to be over, when I saw an older faceling boy standin' right in front-a me."

Scut Wakante : "This faceling, did it seem familiar?"

Rosemord : "Hell if I know. He didn't seem threatening-he just. . .looked down at me, like he felt bad. He offered his hand, and I had to muster all the strength I had left to take it."

Rosemord : "When I did, I felt this rush of energy and I woke up. I practically ripped my hair off the carpet as I sprang up, and I just started runnin'. I don't know where I was runnin'. I scraped up my arms, my legs—my whole body, as I pushed myself through the holes in those walls over and over."

Rosemord : "Every time I felt like stopping, I imagined the door was just around the corner, just one more room, one more crack. But the last thing I expected, was to eventually turn that corner again, and see it."

It is thought that within the small area of the Red Rooms, by concentrating on the idea of the door, a successful interpretation of Rosemord's exit was created, allowing her to leave. But due to the localization of the event, the thoughtform was quickly dispelled once Rosemord exited, making her the only wanderer to ever be able to exit the Red Rooms.

Ms. Rose has since made a full recovery and now resides in a colony within Level 10.

Recovered Log

Date: February 1, 2016

[Slow, labored breathing, background static noise]

Alex: This is Alex Hoffman. Recording. . .My observations of Level 6.5. This is my second day. My recording equipment and other electronics are beginning to fail due to excess electromagnetic radiation.

Alex: I have. . .

[GAG, VOMITING]

Alex: Fuck!

Alex: . . .I'm fearful that if I do not find a way out of this place, I will soon die.

[QUICK BREATH]

Alex: It's getting hot, I've drawn too much attention to myself...

Alex: The walls have changed again, I can't tell where I am...

[APPROACHING FLAMES, HYPERVENTILATING]

Alex: No, no, no.

[THUMP]

[BANG]

Alex: That's the door right? Where's the handle?

Alex: I can't see! No! I should've never left the room! I don't want to die!

Alex: It's too bright! Make it stop! Please!

[BANG, SCRATCHING]

Alex: No! No!

[QUICK PULSATING]

Alex: No!

[GARBAGE NOISE]

[BANG]

Alex: NO!

[THUD, LOUD STATIC]

[Sound of heavy breathing and digging through a bag]

Alex: It's dark, I appear to have exited back to Level 6, but the floor is carpeted, am I in another sublevel?

[CLICK]

Alex: Wait. This is Level 4.

Alex: So why is it so. . .

Dark?

Phenomenon 18-A.title: Phenomenon 18-A
Phenomenon 18-A.description:
Overview

Resting Places are rare phenomena appearing within most enclosed non-Euclidean levels. They have been observed in 218 levels to date, with only one Resting Place appearing per level. Only one wanderer can be in the same instance of a Resting Place, with wanderers being sent to their own copy of the area on entry.

The entrance of a Resting Place appears as a plain wooden door on the wall of any enclosed non-Euclidean level. This door is commonly decorated with stickers to resemble the level, for example, the Resting Place within Level 0's door is decorated with stickers matching the level's wallpaper. Touching the door while outside of the Resting Place will noclip the wanderer into the Resting Place. Touching the door while inside of the Resting Place will noclip the wanderer into a random location within the level the Resting Place is within.

In all accounts of Resting Places, they appear as rooms matching the level's appearance, with a single plastic chair in the middle of the room. Sound in the room is muffled, and any noise made will comfort the wanderer inside. Within a Resting Place, all physical ailments afflicting a wanderer are cured. In addition, mental hazards lose their effect on any wanderer within a Resting Place.

Resting Places will only appear if a wanderer is near death, or has a high risk of death within the next hour (for instance, if one is being chased by a large number of entities, or is exhausted). Upon the appearance of the door leading to the Resting Place, the wanderer will find themselves irrevocably drawn toward the Resting Place.

Wanderers may spend up to 24 hours within the Resting Place, after which they will be ejected out of the Resting Place and sent to a random location within the level as if they had touched the door.

Appearances are not as they seem, however. These innocent locations are not peaceful. If you see the door... well, let's just say it would be better to die another way.

Resting Hyperinsomnia

Resting Hyperinsomnia is the end result of any wanderer's entry into a Resting Place. It is a disease with three phases, similar to Roth Syndrome and other Backrooms illnesses. Resting Hyperinsomnia is invariably fatal.

Resting Hyperinsomnia: Phase 1

The first phase of Resting Hyperinsomnia begins 2 to 4 hours after leaving a Resting Place. During this phase, the wanderer is incredibly tired, yet cannot sleep for longer than four hours. This has an extreme negative effect on wanderers' mental health and sanity.

Resting Hyperinsomnia: Phase 2

The second phase of Resting Hyperinsomnia begins 24 to 30 hours after leaving a Resting Place. Effects on wanderers afflicted by Phase 2 Resting Hyperinsomnia include extreme rage, a tendency toward violence, and partial to complete insanity.

Resting Hyperinsomnia: Phase 3

The third phase of Resting Hyperinsomnia begins seven days after leaving a Resting Place. Initial effects of this phase include incredible anger and rage. As the phase continues, afflicted wanderers become increasingly despondent, eventually losing all sanity and transitioning into Roth Syndrome.

A.D.F..title: A.D.F.
A.D.F..description:
The Another Dimension Foundation (A.D.F.) is a powerful and highly organized group operating within the Backrooms, dedicated to the containment, analysis, and weaponization of anomalous entities and objects. Another Dimension Foundation has established itself as both a scientific authority and a strategic trade partner in the anomalous ecosystem.

With a membership of approximately one thousand individuals, the group is structured with precision. A significant portion of its force is deployed in the field; roughly a third of its members are explorers and containment agents, who are tasked with locating, securing, and documenting threats and curiosities across the levels. An equal share of the organization is composed of scientists, who focus on research, experimentation, and the understanding of non-Euclidean physics and unstable matter. Engineers make up a quarter of the group’s membership, who work tirelessly to design useful and effective weapons, containment units, and transport systems. The last fraction consists of trained guards, who provide both internal security and field support during high-risk missions.
A.D.F..lead:
GROUP OF INTEREST

PRIMARY GROUP TYPE
Containment / Research

Influence: 3/10

Hostility: 3/10

Organization: 8/10

Phenomenon 106.title: Phenomenon 106
Phenomenon 106.description:
Phenomenon 106, also known as Belphegor's Prime, is a rare phenomenon that may occur only in any levels labelled with prime numbers. Regardless of whether the affected level is normally deemed safe or not, any area affected by this phenomenon will instantly experience a spike in instability, as well as generating many level-wide hazardous or undesirable effects that can hinder and often harm any wanderers which happen to be in the level at the time. However, the exact nature of such manifested perils varies drastically based on the level being affected by Belphegor's Prime, ranging from the manifestation of multiple landmines artfully concealed beneath pools  to earthquakes seismically mighty enough to tear buildings apart. Additionally, the rarity of the phenomenon seems to depend on how innately safe the affected level is, with such areas as Level 37 and Level 71 boasting only one recorded occurrence in the past 500 years.

Due to the phenomenon's innate rarity, not much is known about Belphegor's Prime. It is presumed that it is somehow intrinsically linked with Casper-Bray Numbering System|Numerical Patterns bestowed onto levels by their respective level keys, though the exact connexion present between these two phenomena is yet to be discovered. Likewise, the reason behind this phenomenon only affecting levels numbered with a prime remains unknown; a common theory is that these levels act as fundamental building blocks to which all levels owe their existence — much like the nature of prime numbers themselves — but, with nothing to hold their own weight in the infinite abyss of the Void, these prime levels are infinitely more susceptible to being destabilised, an occurrence which manifests as Belphegor's Prime. Where Level 0 and Level 1, numbered as well with the building blocks of mathematics which yet are not prime numbers, fit into this hypothesis is unknown, albeit some researchers believe that they may be subject to a variant of Belphegor's Prime far more severe yet far more uncommon than the regular, considering their respective numbers' sheer fundamentality.
Phenomenon 106.lead:
BELPHEGOR'S PRIME

Note that Belphegor's Prime is an extremely rare phenomenon, happening to most levels only once in an average of 200 years. Do not attempt to start false rumours about upcoming instances of the phenomenon in any of the affected areas, as it is unlikely that any of these will happen in one's lifetime.

Phenomenon 12-A.title: Phenomenon 12-A
Phenomenon 12-A.description: This phenomenon has been given the nickname "Flickering Aftershocks." Due to the nature of this phenomenon, it has been classified as Phenomenon 12-A. It is heavily advised for any M.E.G. officials reading this to be briefed on the nature of Phenomenon 12, also known as The Flicker. All levels, including those with neon protection, experience random manifestations of Phenomenon 12-A, albeit with less impact. When this phenomenon is active, all sources of light in that area of the level, manmade or anomalous, will begin to flicker rapidly. After approximately 10 seconds, the entire area that the phenomenon affected would be plunged into darkness. After an unknown period of time, the light would come back on.

Radiophobia.title: Radiophobia
Radiophobia.description:
Phenomenon 9-B, also known as Red Herrings, is a dangerous and life-threatening phenomenon which occurs within the Backrooms, a branch-off of Tulpaefication. It usually involves the manifestation of a tulpa around a wanderer experiencing trauma or depression, though it may appear unduly under other circumstances as well, which has made its nature extremely difficult to understand. Typically, the tulpa takes the form of an anthropomorphic figure in a red pinstripe suit and several other garments — all with a strange acorn motif — and a head which resembles a common technology in the day and age of manifestation.

These tulpae have collectively been christened "Ratatosk the Reaper" by the wider community (every instance of the phenomenon has been known to call itself by the same name as well), and is often considered some sort of "god" who rules over the Achospic Plane. As anyone from the Antulpaic Order can tell one, this is a frankly absurd idea: as intrepid tulpologists, our members have never once reported a specimen of Phenomenon 9-B that was not in the gestation period. Why exactly the tulpa always appears assuming a similar form is unknown, though may be due to this "mythical entity's" widespread infamy across the Backrooms.

Instances of Phenomenon 9-B have been known to begin as mere intrusive whispers in the heads of their victims, which eventually leads to negative thoughts of self-hatred and self-doubt; eventually enough negative psychic energy will be collected for the tulpa to appear with a physical form, typically by emerging from a unit of technology somewhere near its prey — thus giving the illusion that this "mythical entity" of sorts is able to traverse instantaneously through units of technology all about the Backrooms.

At this stage, the tulpa, radiating an anomalously strong aura of psychic radiation, will begin to feed more negative energy into the victim, either psychically or verbally, eventually driving its prey insane — though its purpose for this is largely unknown. Various instances of the phenomenon have been known to tell their victims "to relax their mind" and "not to resist"; this is likely a trick to make the victim more susceptible to this mind-breaking psychic radiation, thus simplifying the tulpa's job. Various records have detailed several unlucky wanderers falling prey to this deception, though many were able to resist the radiation for long enough that our task forces were successfully able to dispatch the tulpa in time.

A final note: it seems that several specimens of Phenomenon 9-B have shown signs of being completely docile, only wishing to converse with their would-be victims. This tends to happen with "prey" that are less mentally unstable, and may be a sign that the phenomenon is not entirely malicious; of course, more research is needed for this statement to be fully confirmed.
Radiophobia.lead:
I hate the Backrooms.

My name is [REDACTED].

When I was younger, but a carefree child up there...

My father was killed in a car crash.

As an only child, I watched my mother's mental state unravelling.

I did my best to help her, but nothing worked.

Then one day, I awoke in these yellow halls.

I had been separated from my mother...

When she needed me most.

I spent so long wandering these halls in despair.

My mind kept telling me I should just give up and die.

That I wasn't enough for my family.

I could have saved my father.

I could have saved my mother.

I just didn't care enough.

At least, I thought it was my mind saying all that.

That all changed one day, when I met a faceling girl.

We fell in love, and were married.

I joined the Backroom Colonists.

But nothing happy lasts in this damp labyrinth...

On our first anniversary, her blood ran clear.

I spiralled into depression again, and quit my job at the Colonists.

Those thoughts returned to my head...

I really thought I could be good enough.

I thought I could start over.

But redemption isn't a thing.

I will never be good enough.

Not for my wife, my mother, my father.

I deserve to die.

No, I told myself, I must find the will to continue living, even with this grief. I must push through. I cannot die here.

But still, the thoughts consumed me...

On my lowest day, he came to me with an offer.

He told me that he was the one putting all those thoughts in my mind.

He told me that I should stop resisting him.

He told me that this was the only way to achieve salvation.

At that point, I was already too broken to care.

Instead of fighting it, I let his words wash over my body.

It's strangely calming.

I close my eyes.

This is the end for me...

But what does it matter?

I never deserved to live anyway.

ENTITY NO.
222

THREAT LEVEL:
SQUIRREL

Habitat:
The Void

Aggression: 4/5
Attacks on a whim

Intellect: 6/5
Godly levels of intellect and cognition

Danger: 5/5
Attacks are fatal without fail

Entity 222, also known as Ratatosk the Reaper, is perhaps one of the most enigmatic and terrifying beings in the entirety of the Backrooms.

You are worthless...

You don't even deserve to be alive...

What good do you bring to the table?

You're just a speck of dust floating in a vast cosmos.

Backrooms Health Organisation.title: Backrooms Health Organisation
Backrooms Health Organisation.description:
The idea that everyone, regardless of religion, group, or level should be able to enjoy free, accessible healthcare has guided the BHO's mission for over 40 years.

About the Backrooms Health Organisation (BHO)

Dedicated to the wellbeing and health of all wanderers, the Backrooms Health Organisation is at the forefront of giving everyone, regardless of group or level orientation, the opportunity to live a healthy and quality life. The BHO leads efforts to expand free and quality multilevel healthcare coverage, and coordinates responses to interlevel health emergencies and crises. The idea that everyone, regardless of religion, group, or level should enjoy easily accessible healthcare has guided the BHO’s work for over 40 years. The idea that everyone, regardless of religion, group, or level should enjoy easily accessible healthcare has guided the Backrooms Health Organisation's work for over 40 years.

Since its conception, the BHO has brought together the Backrooms’ top medical scientists and healthcare practitioners, paving forth the common goal of a healthier Backrooms. These range from the Saint Luke Disease Index (SLDI), which enables groups and wanderers to use a common standard in identifying health risks and diseases in specific levels, to the treatment and diagnosis of a myriad of pathologies, ranging from trauma to malaria, to Hydrolitis Plague Infections and liquid pain toxicology. The BHO employs a wide range of highly skilled healthcare professionals, including doctors to ensure everyone has the same access to quality healthcare, wherever and whenever crisis hits.

Backrooms Health Practitioner Authority (BHPA)
The Backrooms Health Practitioner Authority is a specialised agency created in conjunction between the Backrooms Health Organisation and other major groups working together to ensure that prospective health practitioners throughout the Backrooms are suitably educated, and qualified to practice. The BHPA ensures wanderers are able to access quality and safe care by regulating and certifying prospective practitioners within the Backrooms, setting standards and policies all healthcare professionals must meet. The Backrooms Health Practitioner Authority further oversees and accredits multilevel medical schools and hospitals, ensuring medical centres both affiliated and non-affiliated with the BHO provide quality and compassionate care.

The BHPA further serves on behalf of Backrooms Health Organisation to accredit interlevel health/medical centres and clinical schools throughout the Backrooms, ensuring they have the capacity to provide safe and quality care. The BHPA, for some time, was further tasked with the establishment of several clinical training programmes, most notably the Liminal Acute Care Programme (LACP), licensing and educating Backrooms foundation and core trainee doctors on both common, frontroom pathologies and enigmatic diseases.

Liminal Health and Wellbeing Initiative

The Liminal Health & Wellbeing Initiative was a program launched by the Backrooms Health Organisation centred around the pathophysiology, research, diagnosis and treatment of enigmatic diseases. In conjunction with the BHO Department of Clinical Science, the Liminal Health & Wellbeing Initiative has attempted to establish guidelines and policies, interwoven into traditional BHO Advanced Life Support (ALS) programs. Ranging from the diagnosis and treatment of Entity 19 infections, to the ingestion of liquid pain and cashew water, the Liminal Health & Wellbeing Initiative is the main agency under the BHO charged with the clinical education and treatment of Backrooms-related illnesses or trauma.

The Liminal Health and Wellbeing Initiative further coordinates healthcare programs under the BHO, hosting a wide range of multilevel healthcare and medical schools run under the BHO and allied groups. Through the program, the BHO aims to educate and prepare candidates towards healthcare careers in the Backrooms for those interested in joining the BHO, or gaining the proper degree and qualifications to join a multitude of other Backrooms groups.

----

You do not need to ask for permission to include this group in your own levels/writings.

Phenomenon 50.title: Phenomenon 50
Phenomenon 50.description:
Phenomenon 50, or the Bloodthirsty Moon, is an extremely rare phenomenon that manifests within outdoor, and open-sky levels. When it occurs, the moon rapidly grows into a massive scale and glows a crimson-red, which cast the environment in a scarlet hue. Soon after, a dense red mist/fog moves across the terrain, and heavy, and slow clouds of the same colour form overhead, which obscures much of the surrounding environment.

During this state, entities across the level become unusually common and pose far greater hostility and unpredictable behavior. People caught within the phenomenon experience a strange condition of imperceptibility: a person nearby cannot see them, as though they have been erased from existence. This invisibility does not affect entities, which seem able to track and pursue people regardless of the effect.

The environment is filled with strange audios. Low, and distorted horn blasts echo from untraceable locations, with unsettling sounds that suggest movement or distant machinery. Radios and digital devices are immediately disrupted, their functions are overridden by an aggressive red static. These devices play sounds between broken white noise and faint repeating ballroom music.

The true danger is in the moon itself. Individuals who allow curiosity to move their gaze upon the moon become paralyzed after 4-6 seconds, and their eyes start burning as the sclera turns a violent red. Tears of blood flow out, then the moon begins to extract bodily fluids in a gruesome process: blood seeps from the eyes, ears, nose, mouth, and any open wound. The phenomenon continues until the victim dies from exsanguination or is forcibly prevented from looking, which usually requires external intervention.

You have been walking for hours through the endless cityscape of Level 11. A sprawl of concrete, glass, and smog that never ends, and never changes. Each building exists in static silence with dark windows that look like hollow eyes. Your boots echo through an empty four-lane street with decayed streetlights.

That’s when you notice it; it suddenly turned night-time. And a crimson light creeps across the asphalt. You look upward, expecting a neon sign or a broken traffic light, but what you see makes your breath stop. There is a massive moon. Larger than it should be. Like a red circle positioned between the crooked skyscrapers, with the color of blood.

A deep hum rises from nowhere, and vibrates through your ribs. Then the mist arrives. A red haze spills in from every alley, and it crawls along the pavement until the whole city is in its glow. The air thickens. Distant horns moan through the fog; distorted, and muffled.

You try your radio; desperate to reach someone. The screen flashes red static. From its speaker a warped melody subtly comes out: ballroom music, cheerful, but broken, it plays beneath layers of static. The notes twist, reverse, and melt into screams you can not quite hear.

You feel something pulling your attention upward again. Curiosity eats you; that feeling of instinct to look. You guessed you should not, but your eyes rise up anyway to the crimson light like a moth to flame.

While your gaze is being casting upon the moon, your body locks. You cannot move. You cannot even breathe properly. Your eyes start to burn, with agonizing pain spreading deep behind them. Tears spill down your face, but something is wrong; they are not tears; they are warm, metallic, red. It is blood. It drips down your chin, which darkens your collar. The hum grows louder, and pressing against into your skull.

You can now feel it; the strange pull. Blood floats toward the moon itself, coming from your nose, your ears, your mouth. Your heartbeat slows, still your veins continue to scream. You try to look away and close your eyes, but they refuse to obey. The moon drinks. You are weightless and heavy all at once. Now you've become a puppet strung by gravity and pain combined.

Your radio starts emitting things again; the ballroom lullaby returns, but slower. It echoes through the streets like a funeral dirge. The red mist swirls around you. Shapes move in it; tall, and wrong silhouettes who is stalking just beyond your sight. You want to scream, but only a gurgle came out.

And then as your strength fades out, you hear the whisper beneath the static. “Don’t look up.” You fall to your knees. Darkness creeps in from the edges of your vision. The red mist thickens until even the buildings become invisible. The last thing you see before the world claims you is the moon; vast, bloody, and patient.

----

Rewrite AuthorNeverOrignial AuthorNuksssIA

Phenomenon 1-B.title: Phenomenon 1-B
Phenomenon 1-B.description:
Phenomenon 1-B, also known as 'No-Clip Disorder', is an extremely rare anomaly that manifests in select persons as an almost complete inability to utilize Phenomenon 1-A. Individuals with the disorder, upon entry to the Backrooms, will find traversing between levels far more difficult, as they are rarely ever able to no-clip and therefore must rely on other methods, such as Elevators. Ironically, Phenomenon 1-B is considered somewhat of a blessing in the Frontrooms (and is theorized to be preventing most of its population from no-clipping in the first place), but because typical travel methods are unreliable in the Backrooms, it becomes a severe handicap. Phenomenon 1-B often prevents wanderers from ever traveling beyond Level 0, and may account for the majority of deaths in said level, though this is unconfirmed.

Of course, the mere existence of Phenomenon 1-B raises the question of how said wanderers ended up in the Backrooms to begin with. If one cannot no-clip, then it should be impossible to ever leave the Frontrooms. Due to the rarity of the disorder in the Backrooms (and its inherent survival challenges), specialists do not yet have all the answers. If you believe you may have No-Clip Disorder, please contact the MEG to be escorted to a safe testing site.

Exactly what causes the manifestation of Phenomenon 1-B is currently unclear. Theoretically, it should only affect oneself; however, the disorder has been shown to consistently affect group no-clipping attempts. Additionally, it may even dull or negate other anomalous phenomena. Afflicted wanderers have reported significantly lower rates of both resources and entities Spawning. While a direct correlation cannot be proven at the moment, this would also contribute to the high death rate of individuals affected by Phenomenon 1-B, since they could not naturally encounter objects like Almond Water and would be at increased risk of starvation or insanity.

The Halloween Party.title: The Halloween Party
The Halloween Party.description:
The Halloween Party (sometimes referred to as The Hallowe'en Party, THP, or simply The Party) is a terrorist and arsonist organisation within the backrooms, known for its operations throughout many inhabited levels which most commonly end in either untimely deaths, or perhaps worse fates, for their unfortunate victims.

They have many tactics to carry out optimal destruction, such as raids, invasions, even down to mere vandalism, though the primary danger posed to civilians is for their twisted version of trick-or-treating. This involves patrols of around 25 of their members going around the backrooms, searching for human residences or lone wanderers and demanding supplies from them. These supplies can occasionally be as simple as sweets or chocolate, but more commonly they demand weapons such as knives and firearms, or even cannons. Should the wanderer(s) refuse, the Party will punish the wanderer, usually by burning their home down and either killing them or kidnapping them to be tortured back at one of their outposts. In rare instances, unlucky wanderers can be asked to give The Halloween Party entirely unrealistic items such as nuclear warheads or a live tyrannosaurus rex, essentially ensuring their demise.

Most members of The Halloween Party are Jovials and tamed Jack-O-Lanterns, though they have occasionally been known to employ smilers, Glowfolk, and even some human gangs. The B.I.F. has previously attempted to infiltrate their ranks using spies posing as an arsonist gang, though even without being caught they ended up being eaten by cannibalistic Jovials. It is for this reason that even if one supports their ideals, it is not recommended to join the group.

Very few wish to voluntarily join The Halloween Party without indoctrination, which takes different forms depending on species. Jovials do not require much, since much of their control is induced by Telepathic Suggestion, but to make absolutely certain that they never falter, they go through a seven month conditioning course, dubbed by The Party as "The Blasphemous Briefing". This course ranges in length, being only two months for Jack-O-Lanterns and up to two years for Smilers and Glowfolk, and anything in between for any other entity members.

Humans are the only exception as most bypass the course altogether, instead being recruited and indoctrinated by a sub-group:
The Halloween Party.lead:
"Hell need not be a a lake of fire. It's a concept, a state of mind. With the diverse lives each of us lead, hell can take many forms. Hell will test us, break us, physically and emotionally, past anything we've seen in the world we call our own. Perhaps, in our modern, and increasingly isolated world, all Hell need be for some of us is an empty, yellow office.

What Hell is not, is a playground for idiots. It is not another mere sandbox, waiting to be butchered by the farmer, it is not another battleground for us to conquer and burn to the ground. We had those chances with our old home, and just like the, greedy, cruel, disgusting little vermin you are, you took it. And that is why you are here. Here, there will be no more of your greed. There will be no more of your evil. And, as retribution for your crimes against all that is good, there will be no more of your happiness.
And that is where we come in. We see your attempts to colonise and comfort yourselves in this prison, and look upon you with distain. You look around, see that this world hates you, see how hostile it is to you, see how every single surface wants you dead! And what do you do? You try to fight it. Do you call this justice? You need to learn that you have failed your lives. And since you have no intent to follow the order that this place demands, we have taken it upon ourselves to get the job done for you."
-Clyde Nelson, extract from his speech at the Crimson Carnival, 2018.

WELCOME TO HELL
YOU WON'T ENJOY YOUR STAY

Level 9.99.title: Level 9.99

class.label: 3e - Environmental
class.entityLevel: High numbers of tornadoes and few entities.

Level 9.99.description:
It is recommended to read this page in dark mode for better readability of the addenda and the page side tool!

Enable Background  Disable Background

Level 9.99 is a sub-level in between Level 9 and Level 10 of the Backrooms.
Description
Level 9.99, also known as “Stormy Suburbs” or “Tornado Lands,” appears to be a stormy suburban complex. Its characteristics are the same as those of its parent level, Level 9. Level 9.99 is constantly shrouded in rain, consisting of a 48-hour cycle. Stores and other buildings can be found in certain locations throughout the level. These stores are usually empty or filled with remnants of dangerous objects or food. Some stores may have an infestation of pink larvae and other insects/entities due to the moist environment. The level presumably stretches out infinitely.

Every house within the level is completely furnished, with furniture that is in a perpetual state of cleanliness and always in prime condition. Additionally, every house contains an abnormally strong scent of detergent. The layout and design of these houses constantly change.

These houses always seem to have 2 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, 1 living room, 1 office, and 1 kitchen. The conditions within the basements are inhospitable for life; all drones will cut off if they go near the basement, and sometimes the basements are claustrophobic. There is close to no food in the house, making it bad to search.

The weather on this level is completely unstable, with temperatures changing erratically from -10 to 17 degrees Celsius, and the weather may change to be hotter or colder depending on where you are. The rain is heavy, rarely letting up, almost never. The precipitation on this level is unpredictable, with no signs of warning before a storm forms. The level's terrain is at risk of collapsing due to the constant heavy rain that has been falling since its formation. Some parts of this level have already collapsed and are considered abandoned. Tornadoes are common within the level, usually occurring every 2 to 3 months.

The level has a central region, known to many as “The Center,” which functions as the heart of level 9.99 due to its safe and stable infrastructure. There is less risk of collapse in this part of the level, but it is not entirely safe, as some tornadoes have hit the Center a few times, causing little damage even though it is an NS5 Category. Winds blow eastward, and many tornadoes usually form in the south, north, and east, with few in the west. These tornadoes that form in the west are usually a danger to the Center, as they mostly head eastward and end up hitting the Center. Several wealthy stores with reliable infrastructure can be found here, and many wanderers take shelter here, causing this area to suffer from overpopulation. The food in the Center is reliable and free of mold, fungi, and larvae, and is safe for consumption. The same is true for the water, which is uncontaminated and filtered. Unfortunately, many people die here due to the lack of space, air, and human causes.

In this central region, there is another central region, in this case, the “Central Cone,” known by many as such because it is a cone, and many believe that it was because of this cone that the level was formed.

 Research conducted by numerous organizations has revealed a major breakthrough-the level orbits a massive blackhole. The black hole that level 9.99 orbits around has been named "Abyssus". The perpetual rain has been caused by a phenomena unique to level 9.99. "Abyssus" seems to emit high levels of energy in the form of bolts of condensed energy, which causes massive storms to form. It is currently unknown why the energy produced by the black hole is converted into such a form of matter. Due to the effects of the black hole, the level experiences many chronological anomalies.

Other research indicates that the atmosphere at this level is unstable, and the black hole resembles a quasar, as it shines brightly but emits almost no radiation. Furthermore, when a type of rocket camera (a camera designed to photograph above the atmosphere at this level) was launched, it found several stars, named “Gamma-Gycnus,” “Zelphegur-Gycnus,” “Nunk-Gycnus,” “Saphragor-Gycnus,” “Wügiu-Gycnus,” and “Dalmata-Gycnus.” They form the constellation Gycnus, until then the only known constellation that can be seen from the level, even though it is impossible to observe it directly from the level. Still, many people mention it and wonder what it would be like, among other things. Abyssus is also part of this constellation, even though it is far away. The stars that make up this constellation orbit this black hole along with the level.

Tornadoes are common within the level; however, their formation is linked to the black hole's gravitational pull. Though the level's estimated distance from the blackhole is immense, the gravitational pull of the black hole is so strong that the air currents near the top of the level's atmosphere can be manipulated by the blackhole at any time to form strong cyclones or tornadoes. Below is the “Noire Scale” that classifies how dangerous a tornado formed in level 9.99 is.

The “Noire Scale” was created by Boston Noire, who had his name on the Noire Scale as a tribute to his creation. The full history of the Noire Scale itself is unknown, as Boston Noire was introverted and did not like to talk and share information with other people. He died in 2002 of pneumonia. Even so, some information has been revealed, namely why he wanted to do this, as he ended up discovering the level and describing it as a “realistic home” because it looked very similar to where he lived in The Frontrooms. He had a nature similar to [REDACTED] and was passionate about tornadoes. He had Ted Fujita, a human from the frontrooms who became well known for the Fujita Scale, so he decided to create his own, with “NS” as the acronym and classification, which works based on destruction, wind, and how long it lasted. It has been improved over time and already has several photographs of tornadoes that were marked at level 9.99. Several wanderers tried to replicate the Noire Scale, but they did not gain popularity, as they work based on the point of view of the wanderer who created them. Several wanderers also tried to create a story to satisfy their children's curiosity. Many believe their parents' stories, but do not know the true story itself.

Sirens have been installed throughout the level to warn wanderers of an incoming tornado.

Tornadoes are extremely dangerous and unpredictable, so caution must be taken, although never enter basements in houses.

Addenda
Addenda are a source of records of tornadoes that have attracted attention and serve only to increase knowledge about what has occurred in recent times.

> Log #1 - Joystick.
Those suburbs...
NS5 Tornado, named Joystick in 1952 - The first NS5 Tornado registered

16:55 - Strange spiral in the sky begins to form

16:58 - Storm is getting stronger.

17:01 - Sirens begin to sound.

17:06 - A column of air begins to form in the sky.

17:11 - Tornado touches down.

17:14 - Tornado begins to grow larger

17:15 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado is NS4.

17:18 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado is NS5.

17:22 - Tornado reaches 1.6 km in diameter.

17:27 - Storm Chasers Twixtaxing begins to locate and record tornado NS5, named Joystick.

17:33 - Tornado begins to wreak havoc and reaches 3.9 km in diameter.

17:35 - Joystick has killed 4 wanderers so far.

17:39 - Joystick reaches its peak diameter, 4.4 km in diameter.

17:40 - Twixtaxing sends a photograph of Joystick to the meteorologists at IStocklogister.

17:44 - Joystick begins to lose strength.

17:45 - Joystick has reduced diameter to 3.6 km.

17:47 - Diameter reduced to 2.2 km.

17:49 - Diameter reduced to 643 meters.

17:50 - Diameter reduced to 341 meters.

17:52 - Joystick begins to fall apart.

17:53 - End of Tornado.

17:59 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado was indeed NS5, caused the deaths of 22 people, occurred at night, destroyed 13 neighborhoods, tore up foundations, and sent debris flying into the air.

05/25/1952
----

> Log #2 - Ostu.
Those suburbs...
NS3 Tornado, named Ostu in 1954 - NS3 Tornado registered

13:08 - A large storm cloud forms.

13:11 - Storm is getting stronger.

13:12 - Sirens begin to sound.

13:13 - A column of air begins to form in the sky.

13:19 - Tornado touches down.

13:20 - Tornado begins on NS2 class.

13:22 - Strong winds begin to blow.

13:25 - Windows explode because of strong winds.

13:30 - Tornado begins to devastate two entire neighborhoods.

13:31 - Storm Chasers Twixtaxing begins to record the NS3 tornado, named Ostu

13:34 - The tornado ended up hitting another neighborhood.

13:40 - Tornado begins to weaken.

13:42 - Twixtaxing sent a photo to the meteorologists at IStocklogister when it was weak, before it dissipated.

13:43 - Tornado ends up dissipating.

13:44 - Meteorologists indicate that the tornado was indeed NS3, caused the deaths of 5 people, occurred during the day, destroyed 3 neighborhoods, and scattered debris across the three neighborhoods.

12/12/1954
----

> Log #3 - BridgeSky.
Those suburbs...
NS5 Tornado, named BridgeSky in 2014 - Last and Largest NS5 Tornado registered

04:33 - A large storm cloud forms.

04:41 - Storm is getting stronger.

04:42 - Sirens begin to sound.

04:47 - A column of air begins to form in the sky.

04:50 - Tornado touches down.

04:52 - The Tornado barely touched the ground and destroyed two houses.

04:53 - Strong winds begin to blow.

04:57 - Tornado begins to gain strength and size.

05:07 - Tornado has already destroyed 7 neighborhoods and killed 74 wanderers.

05:10 - Tornado reaches 7 km in diameter.

05:11 - ZeusFuriosoT's car is caught and kills 4 members.

05:13 - Twixtaxing named it BridgeSky (Bridge because it looked like a bridge in the sky, due to it being a twin tornado, i.e., two tornadoes on either side when viewed from a distance.) (Sky because, as mentioned earlier, it looked like a bridge in the sky.)

05:18 - Tornado reaches 9 km in diameter.

05:21 - IStockLogister indicates that the tornado's diameter is expected to decrease and dissipate in less than 1 hour.

05:30 - Unsuspecting wanderers die without even knowing what happened.

05:32 - Tornado reaches 6 km in diameter.

05:39 - BridgeSky finally dissipates, leaving behind a trail of catastrophic destruction where the twin tornadoes passed.

It was an NS5 tornado, which had not occurred since 2001, causing mass destruction, which in The Frontrooms would cause $3.6 billion in damage.

----

It occurred on May 21, 2001.

05/21/2001
----

Colonies & Outposts
Groups at this level are often referred to as storm chasers, meteorologists, wanderers, group bases, etc. They are designed to help wanderers and study phenomena at this level.

Twixtaxing is the leading group of storm chasers, having been formed in 1947; it is the first group of storm chasers at level 9.99.

Its history comes from the brilliant mind of Theodore Muutsky, who was a tornado hunter in the frontrooms, who ended up falling into the backrooms. He hunted tornadoes at level 10.4, but unfortunately had the misfortune of noclipping into a gray wheat field, so he reached level 9.99. Realizing that there were more people at that level, he was happy. So in 1947, he founded Twixtaxing with other storm chasers. His journey takes place here. Boston Noire was also part of this group. Realizing that he could no longer stay, he was the first person to leave Twixtaxing. The group became better known for reporting and photographing the Joystick and Ostu tornado. Today, with new technologies within this level, they are able to photograph and study level 9.99 tornadoes better.

Today, if we talk about how the group has grown, it is incomparable to others, as they are generous and accept anyone who has good experience in the niche of storm chasing. With 55 members, the group has now become well-known at level 9.99, as IStockLogister and several wanderers now trust Twixtaxing.

ViewTwisters are a group of storm chasers who aim to photograph tornadoes up close, hence the name ViewTwisters.

Their goals are clear, as they are more focused on photographing tornadoes. Unlike other groups of tornado hunters, their photographs are the best, as they know how to position the camera very well, including making videos. This group has 38 members. To be part of it, you must be the best at photographing tornadoes, maintaining stability in the best possible position.

Their priorities are cameras and car fortifications, as they focus more on photographing tornadoes, so they study based on what they see in the photographs.

One of the newest storm chasing groups, GazGam Tornado-Record (GGTR), has 19 active members and 6 inactive members, for a total of 25 members. It is also the only group that has not had any members killed to date.

The group is characterized by its focus on safety. Since several wanderers have been rescued from debris by GazGam Tornado-Record, they rarely take photos, and when they do, it is more to show the danger than to provide information.

This prioritization of safety at GazGam Tornado-Record has meant that no members have died to date. They also warn people about the danger if they do not want to leave their homes and seek a safe place.

ZeusFuriosoT, Portuguese for Furious Zeus and T for Tornado, is a group of 12 storm chasers and the one with the fewest members.

ZeusFuriosoT does not have good cars for hunting tornadoes. After BridgeSky, ZeusFuriosoT lost 4 of its 16 members, leaving it with 12 members. As a result, they began to take more precautions when hunting tornadoes. These drastic measures were necessary for them to avoid losing more members.

However, ZeusFuriosoT began a new phase of testing, creating new cars and starting to modernize things.

IStockLogister is a group of 43 meteorologists specializing in tornadoes, landslides, and climate change. Their work is usually displayed on various posters within the level, typically in the center.

IStockLogister also serves as a photo gallery, as several photographs of tornadoes are stored in the IStockLogister database; these photographs are carefully analyzed, noting what it was, whether it was a mere storm, a subcyclone, cyclone, or a tornado.

Its base is located underground, in a tunnel in the “Central Cone” of the level, ensuring that it is very safe.

Wanderers who enter Level 9.99 are recognized as tornado wanderers. This is not a group per se, but it functions as a civilization.

Even though the level is class 3 - Environmental, many wanderers consider it safe, as there are almost no entities, and due to the numerous active sirens on the level, it ends up being safe. This is also because the level has several tornadoes, so everyone must be very attentive at the very least.

In addition to wanderers who are natives of regions where tornadoes occur frequently, there are several wanderers who pass through the level to relax. As mentioned earlier, wanderers who are natives of regions where tornadoes occur frequently or are more accustomed to them usually call this place “Realistic Home.”

Entities
Entities are hostile and passive presences. Few entities are present at this level.

Pink larvae appear in foods that are more moist than normal. If you are unaware, foods with pink larvae should be eaten with caution and, if possible, avoided at all costs.

It is worth noting that all foods infested with pink larvae are found in regions other than the Center. As mentioned earlier, the food in the Center is clean, monitored, and the water is safe to drink.

If you find or suspect food that may have pink larvae, it is recommended that you throw it away so that your body does not become infested with pink larvae.

Residents are a unique entity at this level.

Their appearance resembles that of a person close to the wanderer.

Their behavior is very predictable and easy to identify; they always stand at a window observing level 9.99.

Houses containing residents are always locked, sometimes with characters in another language.

This language that is found probably came from long ago; its words are different, but it seems that when spoken, it sounds like Russian. These characters found in these houses look like a combination of Russian, Japanese, Roman, Vietnamese, and indigenous peoples' characters (found in The Frontrooms).

Tornadoes are one of the most common features on the level; however, almost none of them are entities, but rather phenomena on the level.

To know if a tornado is an entity, make sure that it is mostly purple and that you can see a type of kinetic energy in the center of the tornado.

Environments
Environmental hazards or Environments are NOT phenomena or anomalies in themselves, as they are part of the ecosystem of the level and formation, and are also one of the environmental characteristics of the level.
Level 9.99.lead:
It is recommended to read this page in dark mode for better readability of the addenda and the page side tool!

Enable Background  Disable Background

Level 9.99 is a sub-level in between Level 9 and Level 10 of the Backrooms.
Description
Level 9.99, also known as “Stormy Suburbs” or “Tornado Lands,” appears to be a stormy suburban complex. Its characteristics are the same as those of its parent level, Level 9. Level 9.99 is constantly shrouded in rain, consisting of a 48-hour cycle. Stores and other buildings can be found in certain locations throughout the level. These stores are usually empty or filled with remnants of dangerous objects or food. Some stores may have an infestation of pink larvae and other insects/entities due to the moist environment. The level presumably stretches out infinitely.

Every house within the level is completely furnished, with furniture that is in a perpetual state of cleanliness and always in prime condition. Additionally, every house contains an abnormally strong scent of detergent. The layout and design of these houses constantly change.

These houses always seem to have 2 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, 1 living room, 1 office, and 1 kitchen. The conditions within the basements are inhospitable for life; all drones will cut off if they go near the basement, and sometimes the basements are claustrophobic. There is close to no food in the house, making it bad to search.

The weather on this level is completely unstable, with temperatures changing erratically from -10 to 17 degrees Celsius, and the weather may change to be hotter or colder depending on where you are. The rain is heavy, rarely letting up, almost never. The precipitation on this level is unpredictable, with no signs of warning before a storm forms. The level's terrain is at risk of collapsing due to the constant heavy rain that has been falling since its formation. Some parts of this level have already collapsed and are considered abandoned. Tornadoes are common within the level, usually occurring every 2 to 3 months.

The level has a central region, known to many as “The Center,” which functions as the heart of level 9.99 due to its safe and stable infrastructure. There is less risk of collapse in this part of the level, but it is not entirely safe, as some tornadoes have hit the Center a few times, causing little damage even though it is an NS5 Category. Winds blow eastward, and many tornadoes usually form in the south, north, and east, with few in the west. These tornadoes that form in the west are usually a danger to the Center, as they mostly head eastward and end up hitting the Center. Several wealthy stores with reliable infrastructure can be found here, and many wanderers take shelter here, causing this area to suffer from overpopulation. The food in the Center is reliable and free of mold, fungi, and larvae, and is safe for consumption. The same is true for the water, which is uncontaminated and filtered. Unfortunately, many people die here due to the lack of space, air, and human causes.

In this central region, there is another central region, in this case, the “Central Cone,” known by many as such because it is a cone, and many believe that it was because of this cone that the level was formed.

 Research conducted by numerous organizations has revealed a major breakthrough-the level orbits a massive blackhole. The black hole that level 9.99 orbits around has been named "Abyssus". The perpetual rain has been caused by a phenomena unique to level 9.99. "Abyssus" seems to emit high levels of energy in the form of bolts of condensed energy, which causes massive storms to form. It is currently unknown why the energy produced by the black hole is converted into such a form of matter. Due to the effects of the black hole, the level experiences many chronological anomalies.

Other research indicates that the atmosphere at this level is unstable, and the black hole resembles a quasar, as it shines brightly but emits almost no radiation. Furthermore, when a type of rocket camera (a camera designed to photograph above the atmosphere at this level) was launched, it found several stars, named “Gamma-Gycnus,” “Zelphegur-Gycnus,” “Nunk-Gycnus,” “Saphragor-Gycnus,” “Wügiu-Gycnus,” and “Dalmata-Gycnus.” They form the constellation Gycnus, until then the only known constellation that can be seen from the level, even though it is impossible to observe it directly from the level. Still, many people mention it and wonder what it would be like, among other things. Abyssus is also part of this constellation, even though it is far away. The stars that make up this constellation orbit this black hole along with the level.

Tornadoes are common within the level; however, their formation is linked to the black hole's gravitational pull. Though the level's estimated distance from the blackhole is immense, the gravitational pull of the black hole is so strong that the air currents near the top of the level's atmosphere can be manipulated by the blackhole at any time to form strong cyclones or tornadoes. Below is the “Noire Scale” that classifies how dangerous a tornado formed in level 9.99 is.

The “Noire Scale” was created by Boston Noire, who had his name on the Noire Scale as a tribute to his creation. The full history of the Noire Scale itself is unknown, as Boston Noire was introverted and did not like to talk and share information with other people. He died in 2002 of pneumonia. Even so, some information has been revealed, namely why he wanted to do this, as he ended up discovering the level and describing it as a “realistic home” because it looked very similar to where he lived in The Frontrooms. He had a nature similar to [REDACTED] and was passionate about tornadoes. He had Ted Fujita, a human from the frontrooms who became well known for the Fujita Scale, so he decided to create his own, with “NS” as the acronym and classification, which works based on destruction, wind, and how long it lasted. It has been improved over time and already has several photographs of tornadoes that were marked at level 9.99. Several wanderers tried to replicate the Noire Scale, but they did not gain popularity, as they work based on the point of view of the wanderer who created them. Several wanderers also tried to create a story to satisfy their children's curiosity. Many believe their parents' stories, but do not know the true story itself.

Sirens have been installed throughout the level to warn wanderers of an incoming tornado.

Tornadoes are extremely dangerous and unpredictable, so caution must be taken, although never enter basements in houses.

Addenda
Addenda are a source of records of tornadoes that have attracted attention and serve only to increase knowledge about what has occurred in recent times.

Object 200.title: Object 200
Object 200.description:
Niraloka Black, formerly known as Matte Black, was a unique type of black paint. It absorbed 100% of all light with no margin of error, creating a pure black surface. It is currently 'extinct', but was found in Level 9, Level 6, and Level 0.23, alongside a temporary manifestation as its home level. This temporary space was generated by The Cross, simultaneously creating the object.

|-| Veil Ink=

Veil Ink, also known as God Blood, was the primary core of Niraloka Black. Veil Ink consists of three main chemicals: Sodium Hydroxide (15.24% abundance), Bromine (21.53% abundance), and Liquid Pain (63.23%). This makes Veil Ink an extremely corrosive substance. This chemical has only been separated from Niraloka Black, as it has not been found elsewhere. Whilst it has been hypothesized that Veil Ink can become a solid, there was no definitive proof.

|-| Negative Charge Converter=

The Negative Charge Converter was an intangible barrier, separating the Negative Particle Ring and the main core of Veil Ink. When passing through the Negative Particle Ring, the light's positive charge was influenced and converted to a negative one. The Negative Charge Converter converts this negative charge back to a positive charge, causing that light to be suspended between the Negative Particle Ring and the Veil Ink, as both carry a negative charge. The Negative Particle Ring has a much stronger charge. As a result, the light was slowly pushed to the Veil Ink, where, through unknown means, the photon particle was destroyed. No light escapes once it has touched the paint. It was comprised of Sha-Particles.

|-| Negative Particle Ring=

The Negative Particle Ring was a ring composed of Zhe-Particles, the sole purpose of which was to use its negative charge to repel light within its barriers. It was intangible and anomalously had no charge on its exterior, allowing light to pass through freely. However, when light passes through and reaches the Negative Charge Converter, the Negative Particle Ring becomes negatively charged on the interior. These particles that make up the ring are hypothesized to be variations of Noble Gases, as they have no radioactivity.
Object 200.lead:
Notice
Object 200, known as Niraloka Black, has dematerialized, and no reports of it have come in. Therefore, it is safe to say that the object no longer exists, or in other words, is 'extinct'. This was in conjunction with the dematerialization of  Level 9.9. While reading this page, keep in mind that Object 200 no longer exists.

Nullification/Nullification Standstills.title: Nullification/Nullification Standstills
Nullification/Nullification Standstills.description:
» NULLIFICATION STANDSTILL «

  ✎ Overview
Nullification Standstills are rare occurrences in which the process of Nullification within a level slows down dramatically. This phenomenon typically occurs in levels that display unusually high structural or dimensional stability prior to exposure to The Void.

During a Nullification Standstill, The Void remains active within the level, whether around or within. However, its consumption process is delayed thanks to this phenomenon; the rate at which Nullification happens is reduced by several magnitudes.

The cause of this phenomenon is unknown. Current hypotheses suggest a connection to entropic reduction, wherein the process of disorder within the level's framework is decreased. Since Nullification is a process that leads to disorder, this is affected as well.

Level 860 is regarded by many as a highly unusual case of this phenomenon, with the process of Nullification within said level appearing to be nearly halted. This case of Nullification Standstill is thought to have persisted for many decades, and is regarded by many as The 860 Effect.

Curious, indeed. It appears as though some levels have been offered a moment of grace.

Others perish quickly, while those few are able to persist for longer.

But how much longer, will this time of respite last?

And when this mysterious spell wears off, what will happen then?

Oh, but I don't know. I'm like one of you.
