Backrooms Botanist Authority.title: Backrooms Botanist Authority
Backrooms Botanist Authority.description: The Backrooms Botanist Authority is a group dedicated to botany, also known as phytology, which is the study of plants.
Backrooms Botanist Authority.lead: The Backrooms Botanist Authority is a group dedicated to botany, also known as phytology, which is the study of plants.

Level 151.title: Level 151
Level 151.description:
A recent discovery proved that the antennas are living organisms, deeply rooted into the entirety of the Backrooms. Currently, it is known to only inhabit Level 151.

As stated in the level's description, they appear as large antennas with satellite dishes, made out of concrete and metal. There are many cracks, likely from aging of the being, presumed that the more cracks they have the older they are. Another indicator of age is in their size; the larger ones are newer and the older ones are smaller, similar to mountains of reality. The satellite dish has a small yet unnoticeable opening, which is where it emits the signals.
Level 151.lead:
They were driving down a back road one night.

In the driver's seat sat a man named Soren.

In the passenger seat next to him was a man named Timothy.

The snow on the road was starting to melt away.

The sun was setting.

Soren's eyes were heavy. He had been driving for more than 10 hours with no progress.

Timothy was distracted, staring out the window aimlessly.

.

.

.

"Where are we even heading?" Timothy asked, still looking out of the window.

"To Kraków. Why are you so forgetful? What did you eat yesterday?" Soren asked Timothy, staring at him, waiting for the answer.

"Nobody keeps track of that!"

"It is very normal to remember what you did yesterday, Tim."

.

.

.

"Tim, will you change the channel?"

"No, they are telling the news just now. I know you don't like them–"

"It is in another language."

"Oh... I forgot about that."

"You were just looking out of the window for way too long."

"You caught me."

Tim looked down at the stereo. It was collecting dust due to the age of the car, not to mention the fact it hadn't been cleaned in months.

He reached down to the stereo...

The shift between the channels was distorted, skipping over two stations.

"Station... 151BR? That's odd for a channel name. Soren, have you heard of that?"

"No."

It played a melody that was distant and haunting, yet beautiful.

"so beautiful..."

Soren said, almost like he was dazed.

Almost like he was hypnotized.

His pupils dilated as his hands left the steering wheel.

They were going to crash.

Tim's heart slammed against his ribs.

"Soren, put your hands back!"

But Soren did nothing. Just staring into the road.

"SOREN THE STEERING WHEEL!"

The music was growing louder every single second.

"SOREN— "

But it was too late.

The two were never seen again.

----
----
Two Men Vanish after Late-night Crash on an Isolated Backroad
----
----
Kraków County, 2004 12 26
----

----
Photograph of the scene
----
This morning, an elderly woman spotted the car while walking along the road. She immediately called the police in a nearby telephone booth, prompting them to conduct a thorough investigation of the area. She said that there were no nearby footprints to indicate the location of the passengers, who went missing.

The police's investigation gave way to more questions than answers. The only sign of passengers were the tire marks that the car left, and blood on the airbag likely from a nosebleed caused by whiplash. K-9 units were used to find the passengers using the car's scent, but that led nowhere.

Interviews from the nearby village also indicated two occupants in the vehicle, displaying odd behaviour. An elderly couple exclaimed: "Inside the car were two men. They were going in a loop, passing out house three times before going onto the back road.". Despite locals describing seeing the car yesterday, it's state indicating a much older crash.

The authorities are requesting anyone with any other information to please contact them at:██████████████
----

TERMINAL
Station 151BR

FREQUENCY
Occasional

LOCATION
Global

DESTINATION
The Backrooms

EXTRA
The channel can manifest in any car, trapping the victim once played.

Station 151BR is a terminal discovered in January 2005, due to various wanderers claiming to have been sent to the Backrooms via its transmission. Where these wanderers first appear when transported remains unclear, as the interviewed wanderers have given mixed or otherwise incomplete responses. The current theory goes that most wanderers affected by Station 151BR are sent to a level with a vehicle-centric layout.

The terminal is a series of radio waves that can be distributed without knowledge of any local or international radio station. This waves originate from a level now known as Level 151. Its appearance can only happen in cars that can freely switch between radio stations. When selected, Station 151BR plays a monophonic, adagio tune. It has been described as feeling distant and haunting, along with it slowly rising in volume over its playtime. The sound remains confined to the car's interior, and cannot be adjusted auditorily over its runtime.

Listening to Station 151BR triggers a hypnotic effect upon the driver. This hypnosis will then force them to let go of the steering wheel, while still pressing the gas pedal. In every instance, this has lead to a crash, and everyone inside the car being transported to The Backrooms. Information about its transmission and hypnotic properties is under investigation.

A new level has been discovered
----

>>Level 151 // "Radiowaves"

Level 151 is the 152nd level of the Backrooms, discovered on the 23rd of January, 2009.

entrances.text: Going through a door that is placed unnaturally in Level 10.
A certain corridor in Level 120 which has lockers on its sides.
Level 422 will eventually transition into the level.

exits.text: Turning off equipment inside of radio stations can lead to Level 41, Level 89 and Level 289.
No-clipping into a tree will lead to Level 6.1
Finding a patch of wheat, and staying in it will lead to Level 10.
Digging down through the snow has a small chance of coming across a broken crack, fit enough to enter Level 960, which will lead one to that level.

Level 19.2.title: Level 19.2
Level 19.2.description:
Level 19.2 is a sublevel of Level 19.

「 Level 19.2 is a sprawling complex of endless, somewhat derelict hotel halls plagued by faulty lighting. Wanderers always enter through a roughly average sized hotel room, though the specific wallpaper differs for each individual. Lighting throughout the level flickers unpredictably, sometimes sparking. Unlike most levels, lights are never mounted on the ceiling. Doors vary in material, ranging from wood to steel. Steel doors are uniquely dangerous because they have a tendency to fall over, crushing wanderers beneath their weight. Exit signs can be found occasionally throughout the level, usually turned off or flickering. 」

「 Remaining in one place in Level 19.2 may cause severe and irreversible sanity drain, so it is advised not to stay long. The environment is unsafe and unstable; heavy ceiling tiles collapse to reveal exposed wiring, walls may cave in without warning, and floors can give way beneath wanderers. These hazards make the level extremely dangerous and generally avoided. 」

「 Conditions within the level are notably hostile. The temperature fluctuates wildly from around 20°C to -40°C. This, alongside the unstable architecture, causes major environmental risk. To add on to this risk, supplies are scarce, with only the occasional Almond Water or doggie bag of snacks. The wallpaper is highly temperature-sensitive; sections without wallpaper are usually frigid, so give those areas a wide berth. Barricaded doors occasionally may hold the exit, but their metal is filled with raw electricity, so it is unwise to try. It is believed these doors are the main power system. 」

「 The hotel rooms other than the one wanderers spawn in are usually fully furnished, but with a distinct absence of supplies. Any attempt to break or damage a painting results in the painting spontaneously combusting, usually killing the wanderer. After a few moments, the painting will regenerate in a strange, unnatural way, restoring itself as if nothing happened. Heaters appear rarely but are extremely unstable, sometimes exploding due to the excessive flow of electricity from nearby wires. Hotel rooms beyond the initial spawn point are far more dangerous, frequently containing coolers, puddles of Liquid Pain, and temperature-based anomalies. Survival within this environment requires extreme caution and constant movement, as nearly every feature carries hidden threats. 」

entrances.text: Entering abnormally cold hotel rooms in any hotel-based level (ex. Level 5, Level 350) and falling asleep will lead one to wake up here. Entering a vent in "The Lobby Area" of Level 960 also leads one here.
Notably, in Level 634, a door affixed with 'HOTEL' scrawled on in faded grey sharpie may appear; opening it will knock one out and cause them to wake up in the level.

Level 19.2/Offset1.title: Level 19.2/Offset1
Level 19.2/Offset1.description:
Level 19.2 is a sublevel of Level 19.

「Level 19.2 is a sprawling complex of endless, cleanly hotel halls with ornate lighting. Wanderers always enter through a large, plush hotel room; however the specific details vary. Unlike other levels, lighting throughout the level stays constant, never flickering. Unlike most levels, lights are never mounted on the ceiling. Doors vary in material, ranging from wood to steel. Exit signs can be found occasionally throughout the level, usually turned on and granting the wanderer a deep sense of peace. The environment is uniquely stable throughout the level. While other levels may be non-Euclidean and have unstable architecture, Level 19.2 is uniquely linear and is quite stable. 」

「 Conditions within the level are notably temperate. The temperature fluctuates wildly from around 21°C to 32°C. This, alongside the stable architecture, makes this level highly suitable for human life. To add on to these benefits, supplies can be consistently and frequently, such as Almond Water and Work Juice. The wallpaper appears to positively affect the wanderer, as being near the walls fills the wanderer with a sense of security. Locked steel doors appear across the level, offering an exit; however these exits usually lead to peaceful but lower-quality levels like Level 4. It is believed these doors are direct holes in the void to transition so seamlessly.

「 The hotel rooms other than the one wanderers spawn in are always fully furnished, and contain significantly more supplies. Any attempt to break or damage a painting results in the painting instantly regenerating and a small plastic bag of non-anomalous snacks appearing on the floor, as if it were an offering. Heaters appear rarely, but become more often in the winter months, providing sufficient heat to stay warm and cozy. Hotel rooms beyond the initial spawn point are far more beneficial, frequently containing coolers with beverages, and pantries stocked with snacks and other foodstuffs. Survival within this environment is uniquely easy, as minimal threats exist within the level. 」

entrances.text: Entering abnormally plush and well-kempt hotel rooms in any hotel-based level (ex. Level 5, Level 350) and falling asleep will lead one to wake up here. Entering a vent in "The Residual Space" of Level 960 also leads one here.
Notably, in Level 634, a door affixed with 'HOTEL' scrawled on in sharpie may appear; opening it will knock one out and cause them to wake up in the level.

exits.text: Doors affixed with a 19.2 will lead to 525252525252525252525252.
Doors affixed with a brass number '6' will lead to the aforementioned level.
Doors affixed with the phrase 'Front Desk' may lead to one of the check-in counters in Level 187.
Doors affixed with the phrase 'Hotel Pool' may lead to Level 330.

Phenomenon 7.title: Phenomenon 7
Phenomenon 7.description:
Phenomenon 7, coined as the term "Item Materialization" describes the spontaneous occurrence of seemingly randomized objects appearing throughout the Backrooms. While it is still unknown whether or not this phenomena is a materialization of some sort or an unique no-clip of items belonging to that of baseline reality, this phenomenon is one of the few phenomena that has truly benefited humans in the backrooms.

Phenomenon 7 is also the singular most common source of firearms and ammunition for many factions and lone wanderers alike (the scavenging of Entity 25 is another common cited source). Crates of food or medical supplies are also highly valued among wanderers with factions being known to send dispatch teams for the sole purpose of finding useful drops and stock supplies. While the phenomenon is quite common to come across in comparison to many of the phenomena recorded across the database, it is still uncommon in the grand scheme of things; especially considering that the objects spawned in is completely randomized, rendering truly useful items being only a fraction of probability within encounters.

While theoretically Phenomenon 7 should exist across all Backroom levels, due to the lack sample size, it is unknown if that is truly the case; despite this, the phenomenon does seemingly appear in numerous levels with seemingly no logic or quantifiable categorization. With limited data, some scholars have also proposed that the phenomenon usually appears on safer levels, or that the frequency/probability of Phenomenon 7's manifestation is tied to the survival difficulty, although skeptics point that the data of dangerous levels is simply skewed because of the frequent deaths of explorers, which significantly lowers the chance of recorded encounters.

While commonly harmless and beneficial, there have been instances where Phenomenon 7 puts people in danger. Below is a comprehensive list of harmful cases recorded by the database.

* Two wanderers were sprayed of acid after opening a pressurized can of soda on Level 4, both were found and hospitalized by the S.R.C., resulting in 1 death.
* An Aurielle member was killed after pocketing an grenade defect that exploded a few minutes after due to its unstable nature, what was left of the corpse was later discovered by the M.E.G. on Level 117.
* A Backroom Colonist was fell severely ill after opening a crate bursting of anomalous unsanitary content on Level 300, she was subsequently hospitalized by the Backrooms Red Cross and is currently recovering.
* A boat found on Level 7 started to collapse with seemingly no apparent reason, resulting in the death of one Entity Research Committee member.
* Several crates that contained hostile entities such as Clumps and Hounds were discovered on Level 20 by the A.E.G., although any similar instance was never recorded again.
Phenomenon 7.lead: Firearms are rare and valuable commodities generated by Phenomenon 7.

Level 188.65.title: Level 188.65
Level 188.65.description:
Level 188.65 manifests as an infinite series of hotels. The wanderer starts at the same center hotel every time, with a handful of exceptions. These hotels are typically 5 to 6 floors high, and consist of two areas, the Hallways and the Rooms. The Hallways are simply hallways connecting all the rooms on each floor together. These halls usually have minimal decoration, save for chandeliers and abstract paintings. Each hallway has its own unique color scheme. Notably, certain hallways may have stairwells, these connect each floor together. The Rooms are the hotel rooms that permeate the hallways at regular intervals. These are often calming if bland. They have a small kitchenette, grey carpeting, a mini fridge, a queen-size bed with white furnishings, a few chairs and tables of different materials, and a large window with grey curtains. While the Rooms are certainly mundane, they pose no threats to wanderers, being safe areas to rest and relax. Rarely, hotel rooms may have severe deviations. Typically, these deviations involve the combining of multiple rooms or the furnishings of rooms becoming more advanced. These rooms are referred to as 'deluxe rooms' by locals. All rooms share the same effect, which causes supplies within the rooms to slowly replenish over a 24 hour period. This allows extended stays and potentially larger and self-serving colonies, although supplies within rooms are usually enough for only three people, and for larger colonies requires foraging further and further away.

In the center of each and every hotel is a large, pentagonal-shaped courtyard. Each courtyard has 5/9th of the space dedicated to greenery, with four concrete walkways headed perpendicular to each other against a small wooden platform in the center. The remaining 4/9ths is brick floors, with a small circular section against the wall for more greenery. These courtyards are open and wide, with an area of roughly 3,066m2. They are hotspots for wanderer activity, notably hosting centers of commerce. The courtyards' ceiling is not open air, but a painted blue sky with a defined brick pattern. Each hotel is roughly ≈1.07 acres.

Entity 2-A, frequently known as Peripherals, are an entity only found to inhabit Level 188 and its sublevels. This entity manifests as a shadowy humanoid, frequently described as roughly 7 feet tall. This entity targets the peripherals of a given wanderer's vision. This entity sticks to the peripherals, not displaying itself in any way other than at the edge of one's vision. If a Peripheral stays in one's vision for too long, affected individuals will begin having feelings of anxiety, paranoia and scopophobia. These feelings will slowly increase in intensity, which heavily impacts one's mental well-being and often sends people hurtling towards the Wretch Cycle, or worse. This usually takes between 8 and 12 hours to take effect. The best way to counter this is to see past your peripheral vision. This can be done by asking others to watch your back or surrounding yourself in mirrors. If the entity is within any human's line of sight, it will disappear. It will usually re-appear after this stops, but after roughly 15 seconds of unbroken eye contact it will give up and move to a different victim. It is unknown whether the Peripherals are one individual entity or a group of different entities, but it is believed it is a group, as multiple attacks have been detected at the same time. It is believed to be related to the shadowy figures sometimes spotted amongst the sceneries from Windows, but this is merely a theory.
Level 188.65.lead:
Level 188.65: The Hyperbolic Hotel
Play Audio

Level 188.65 is a sublevel of Level 188.

Phenomenon 17.title: Phenomenon 17
Phenomenon 17.description:
R-1, also identified as R-5, is a Roaming Level currently stuck within the center of First's residence within Base Alpha. R-1 is composed of a piece of Bedrock that has since been severed from the Bedrock as a whole and drifted to the First's residence. The current size of R-1 is currently unknown, as previous exploration parties have yet to find a limit to the level. All current exploratory and research parties into R-1 have been cancelled due to the discovery of R-1's primary feature, a large Stele that juts out from the ground approximately 77 miles away from the current entrance of R-1.

Upon entering a 10-mile range of the Stele, the sky and surroundings begin to mimic that of an outdoor park. This is only a visual change, however, as benches located around the Stele are not physical, nor is anything else except the Stele. Below is a transcript of the Stele
Phenomenon 17.lead:
WELCOME TO THE M.E.G LEVEL 0 RESEARCH INSTITUTE.
Stellae ubi nullae sunt

YOU ARE ACCESSING-

> bypass

BYPASSED

> access P-17

CLEARANCE LEVEL UNVERIFIED. PLEASE INSERT CLEARANCE CARD OR SPECIAL CODEWORD

> the stars outside shine with a black glow.

CODEWORD IDENTIFIED; WELCOME THE LAST

ACCESSING FILE NOW...

Phenomenon 8.title: Phenomenon 8
Phenomenon 8.description:
Phenomenon 8, commonly known as Aterno Momentum, is a phenomenon present within most levels that possess an observable nighttime and are otherwise specified to not have Frontrooms-derived constellations. This phenomenon causes the formation of an entirely distinct nightsky with constellations that usually represent an entity, object, group, or important event that occurred within the Backrooms. Affected levels always have distinct constellations, and the extent of said constellations is unknown; some say they are as long as the level's sky, while others say they eventually cease.

Aterno Momentum gained its corresponding name through, as previously stated, most commonly immortalizing important events. Aterno Momentum is ever-changing; every major action done by humanity, an entity, or likewise will most likely become a constellation of their own, given that they are significant enough. The process of which is usually done by an increased brightness within a set of stars that represent the perpetator, victim, moment, item, etc. of said event. Interestingly, some of these "astranifications" happen before the occurrence of the event it represents; the most recent of these being Incident 154.

Additionally, due to immortalizing important events, Aterno Momentum has become of grand interest for Backrooms historians, and many expeditions have been launched just to search for never-before-found constellations in order to give an outline of the lost history of the Backrooms. The oldest currently recorded constellation being "Hores,"  which is thought to represent a homo callidus killing a Hound; it is presumed that the man represented is the first to be able to kill one; it is dated over millions of years old.

Not only has Aterno Momentum been of major interest to historians, but it is also helpful to the everyday wanderer. Aterno Momentum can be used as a substitution to the compass, as the constellations present can be reached (as in they move away from one and closer to one). Further, one can stumble upon bases, outposts, or colonies since it is a rather common practice to set them up below constellations because they significantly ease the burden that comes with interbase cooperation and trading and also give the opportunity of making a "road-like" system that connects various colonies together.

The classification of Aterno Momentum as a phenomenon is debated, although it is the most widely accepted classification of it by the scientific community. Some argue it is an entity that is related to H.K. due to both being related to stars and both, to some extent, changing their environment, albeit H.K. does it in a vastly more dangerous way.

Phenomenon 69 (Joke).title: Phenomenon 69 (Joke)
Phenomenon 69 (Joke).description:
How many times has your device had a virus?
 Maybe once... twice... or not at all...
 But if you think you're safe...

Run File 69?

WARNING: This may put your device at risk! Proceed with caution!!

 No... It's a critical failure
 ERROR 69: BASED, BASED, BASED...

...
...
...
...
...
...
...
...
You received a new message on your Windows!
...

You received a notification!!
X
 

SwampChat v1.0
"Join the swamp. Be based."

INSTALL NOW

...

...

Where...

...

...

...Why is my computer not working?

...

...

...Why???

...What did I do??

...

...

No, my Computer...

MY COMPUTER...

WHY DID I INSTALL IT???

IT LOOKED LIKE A VIRUS...

...WHY DOES THIS ONLY HAPPEN TO ME??

OUT OF ALL THESE PEOPLE...

JUST ME??

...WAS I STUPID??

DID MY COMPUTER CLICK AUTOMATICALLY???

Based??

Everything is turning...

“Based”??

My account...

I lost everything...

Everything.

> Description

The overview of ERROR 69: Malware-Based

The Phenomenon 69, known as “Based,” is a type of malware virus that enters devices through software and hardware. This virus accesses all files, releases personal information, and irreversibly corrupts the device. The purpose of those who created this virus is unknown. It can be found on websites for installing applications and files, and it can create a pop-up on the device's desktop asking to install the application. Its pop-ups appear to be from the 2000s. It can also infect other devices via cables, as well as websites through third parties, as in a case on BACK-CHAT, where a wanderer named Joshua ended up clicking on a message from another anonymous wanderer, probably with an infected device, and lost his device forever.

Some images also appear on the device, in addition to frying device components such as the battery, RAM, video card, and other components. Its main target is the video card, which is at risk of exploding due to high temperature, in addition to corrupting sound devices, causing a loud sound of keys at a rhythm similar to “M” “X” and “A” with a delay of only 3 seconds, with a deafening buzz reminiscent of level 0. Its images seem to move and disperse, with several words written “BASED,” and the desktop begins to distort the already installed applications and replace them with other malware viruses that also steal your database and everything else. The colors of the device on the screen begin to become inverted and vibrant, aiming to increase the brightness and radiation emitted, taking it to the maximum, and destroying internal components.

Devices connected via cable to the infected device end up being infected together, such as sound devices, writing devices, clicking devices, and others connected via cable to be recharged. Once these devices are infected, the same thing happens to the main infected device. However, there is a greater chance that devices infected via cable will end up exploding due to information overload, which fries the components and affects the battery, causing the device to explode in the process.

> Discovery

The discovery of ERROR 69: Malware-Based

The discovery of error 69 is still unknown.

But the two most plausible theories are:

Theory 1: The Revenge Theory.

According to this theory, the creator, who was anonymous and unknown at the time, suffered greatly from a virus that infected his device in the frontrooms. His device was infected with a virus known as MugMod, recommended by his friends as a joke that the creator found unfunny. Seeking revenge, the creator initiated a plan against his friends, now considered enemies. However, in the middle of the plan, he ended up suffering a noclip, and his plan went down the drain. His disappearance in the town of Bridge-Creek on May 3, 1999, was later linked to the tornado that destroyed the community. Thinking about causing humanity in the backrooms to stagnate, to stop for a long time, he thought about remaking the same virus that infected the mobile device, causing technologies to also suffer in the backrooms.

His idea coming to fruition led him to create the BASED virus. Until now, the origin of the name “BASED” is unknown. More significant studies into the origin of the virus's name may reveal the identity of its creator. This virus seems to have its own intentions and sentience, probably controlled by a previously unknown AI created by the same user. Its sole intention is to destroy devices to “rewind” human technology in the backrooms.

This theory aims to understand the creator's side and find out why they created the virus and, subsequently, discover who they are.

The second theory is also plausible, competing with theory 1.

Theory 2: The Conscious Entity Theory.

According to this theory, its creator would be an entity not yet discovered in the database, with apparent human-like intelligence, whose sole purpose is to destroy humanity in the backrooms through the technology that possesses them.

Its behavior always seems hostile and territorial. It appears that this entity once dominated the backrooms, but its population was later reduced by 99.7% in a large explosion of new entities, which subsequently killed them due to lack of space and sudden deaths, drastically reducing their population.

This rise probably took place before humans even existed. If this theory is proven, this entity probably wants to regain what it had before humans. Since it does not have the strength to attack humans and many are scattered, its strategy is to strike internally to cause them to collapse and regain its dominance in the backrooms.

These two theories are the most widely accepted due to their realistic scenario.

However, what is actually known about its discovery was on April 17, 2001, while an 8-year-old child was playing, she received a message after closing an application called Graveyard Hills. She clicked on the message to install after a message appeared from SwampChat, by an anonymous user. In the first moments after clicking, the device called a PC, began to crash and show the same “symptoms” as this virus, until it exploded.

An investigation was launched, based on the child's report, as well as other reports of this new type of malware virus.

Her mother soon taught her not to download applications with appealing and strange names.

This single report was enough to provide half of all the information we have so far about this virus.

> How it works:

How the ERROR 69: Malware-Based works

As far as we know at this time, this virus works in two different ways to enter the device:

Way 1: Message.

This message can appear at any time of the day, as long as the device is turned on.

This message appears with an old computer label and a blue tab above it. You cannot refuse this message; if you close it, it reappears. If you click on it, the virus enters the system and corrupts valuable data, such as saves, channels, or even social networks, requiring a 5-character password. Soon after, the virus begins to export your information to third parties, in addition to forcing the video card to run at maximum capacity until it burns out, with new applications appearing more and more frequently. Soon, pop-ups appear with “BASED” written on them, in addition to many SPAM emails. Soon after, it begins to corrupt files and destroy RAM, in addition to forcing the power supply to remain on for a long time. Then it leaks the internet and destroys the coolers, leaving only the motherboard to support it, which does not last long. The virus causes a short circuit in the monitor, which explodes, creating many volts and taking the motherboard with it, causing it to explode or become corrupted, along with the BIOS.

This is the most common way it spreads through the device.

Form 2: Email.

Someone appears in your email, writing BASED, an exclusive bot of the virus, using spam to ask you to install SwampChat. If you install it, the virus enters the system and corrupts precious data, such as saves, channels, or even social networks, requiring a 5-character password. Soon after, the virus begins to export your information to third parties, in addition to forcing the video card to run at maximum capacity until it burns out, with new applications appearing more and more. Soon, pop-ups appear with “BASED” written on them, in addition to many SPAM emails. Soon after, it begins to corrupt files and destroy RAM, in addition to forcing the power supply to stay on for a long time. Then it leaks the internet and destroys the coolers, leaving only the motherboard to support it, which does not last long. The virus causes a short circuit in the monitor, which explodes, creating many volts and taking the motherboard with it, causing it to explode or become corrupted, along with the BIOS.

This is the least common and least effective method, as the user can report it.

These are the two main ways in which the virus spreads through the device.

> Guide

The guide of ERROR 69: Malware-Based

This is the survival guide against the virus, on what to do in case of an attempted intrusion.

What to do and what not to do:

What to do:

- When the pop-up appears as a message directly on the device you are using, do not look for close buttons, as they may cause the device to think you have clicked the install button and end up infecting it. What you should do at this point is restart the device and remove any USB cables connected to other devices.

- When the email appears, do not reply, as it may be spam. Simply block and report the user who is spamming or asking you to install the application. This may also lead other users to do the same thing, but just repeat and wait for users to stop or use an application that encrypts emails or messaging applications.

- If someone accidentally clicks on install, remove all cables connected to other devices connected to the infected device, and disconnect the infected device from the power source. This should slow down the virus for some time before turning on the device. During this time, try to remove all internal components, especially the video cards and motherboard. If there is no time, leave the location immediately and evacuate everyone nearby.

What not to do:

- When the pop-up appears, do not click anywhere on it or anywhere else.

- When the email appears, do not accept and install the application, or report and block it, as this may cause future problems with the amount of data present.

- Once the virus is installed on the device, do not stay near or continue using the infected device.

The dos and don'ts change over time, and the virus can also evolve.

> Description

The overview of ERROR 69: Malware-Based

The Phenomenon 69, known as “Based,” is a type of malware virus that enters devices through software and hardware. This virus accesses all files, releases personal information, and irreversibly corrupts the device. The purpose of those who created this virus is unknown. It can be found on websites for installing applications and files, and it can create a pop-up on the device's desktop asking to install the application. Its pop-ups appear to be from the 2000s. It can also infect other devices via cables, as well as websites through third parties, as in a case on BACK-CHAT, where a wanderer named Joshua ended up clicking on a message from another anonymous wanderer, probably with an infected device, and lost his device forever.

Some images also appear on the device, in addition to frying device components such as the battery, RAM, video card, and other components. Its main target is the video card, which is at risk of exploding due to high temperature, in addition to corrupting sound devices, causing a loud sound of keys at a rhythm similar to “M” “X” and “A” with a delay of only 3 seconds, with a deafening buzz reminiscent of level 0. Its images seem to move and disperse, with several words written “BASED,” and the desktop begins to distort the already installed applications and replace them with other malware viruses that also steal your database and everything else. The colors of the device on the screen begin to become inverted and vibrant, aiming to increase the brightness and radiation emitted, taking it to the maximum, and destroying internal components.

Devices connected via cable to the infected device end up being infected together, such as sound devices, writing devices, clicking devices, and others connected via cable to be recharged. Once these devices are infected, the same thing happens to the main infected device. However, there is a greater chance that devices infected via cable will end up exploding due to information overload, which fries the components and affects the battery, causing the device to explode in the process.

> Discovery

The discovery of ERROR 69: Malware-Based

The discovery of error 69 is still unknown.

But the two most plausible theories are:

Theory 1: The Revenge Theory.

According to this theory, the creator, who was anonymous and unknown at the time, suffered greatly from a virus that infected his device in the frontrooms. His device was infected with a virus known as MugMod, recommended by his friends as a joke that the creator found unfunny. Seeking revenge, the creator initiated a plan against his friends, now considered enemies. However, in the middle of the plan, he ended up suffering a noclip, and his plan went down the drain. His disappearance in the town of Bridge-Creek on May 3, 1999, was later linked to the tornado that destroyed the community. Thinking about causing humanity in the backrooms to stagnate, to stop for a long time, he thought about remaking the same virus that infected the mobile device, causing technologies to also suffer in the backrooms.

His idea coming to fruition led him to create the BASED virus. Until now, the origin of the name “BASED” is unknown. More significant studies into the origin of the virus's name may reveal the identity of its creator. This virus seems to have its own intentions and sentience, probably controlled by a previously unknown AI created by the same user. Its sole intention is to destroy devices to “rewind” human technology in the backrooms.

This theory aims to understand the creator's side and find out why they created the virus and, subsequently, discover who they are.

The second theory is also plausible, competing with theory 1.

Theory 2: The Conscious Entity Theory.

According to this theory, its creator would be an entity not yet discovered in the database, with apparent human-like intelligence, whose sole purpose is to destroy humanity in the backrooms through the technology that possesses them.

Its behavior always seems hostile and territorial. It appears that this entity once dominated the backrooms, but its population was later reduced by 99.7% in a large explosion of new entities, which subsequently killed them due to lack of space and sudden deaths, drastically reducing their population.

This rise probably took place before humans even existed. If this theory is proven, this entity probably wants to regain what it had before humans. Since it does not have the strength to attack humans and many are scattered, its strategy is to strike internally to cause them to collapse and regain its dominance in the backrooms.

These two theories are the most widely accepted due to their realistic scenario.

However, what is actually known about its discovery was on April 17, 2001, while an 8-year-old child was playing, she received a message after closing an application called Graveyard Hills. She clicked on the message to install after a message appeared from SwampChat, by an anonymous user. In the first moments after clicking, the device called a PC, began to crash and show the same “symptoms” as this virus, until it exploded.

An investigation was launched, based on the child's report, as well as other reports of this new type of malware virus.

Her mother soon taught her not to download applications with appealing and strange names.

This single report was enough to provide half of all the information we have so far about this virus.

> How it works:

How the ERROR 69: Malware-Based works

As far as we know at this time, this virus works in two different ways to enter the device:

Way 1: Message.

This message can appear at any time of the day, as long as the device is turned on.

This message appears with an old computer label and a blue tab above it. You cannot refuse this message; if you close it, it reappears. If you click on it, the virus enters the system and corrupts valuable data, such as saves, channels, or even social networks, requiring a 5-character password. Apollo-∞.title: Apollo-∞
Apollo-∞.description:
}}

  CURIOSITY

One of the strongest emotions that a human can feel is curiosity. Naturally, upon finding something new, we will have an urge to explore it.

Now, something that remains constant is that curiosity is neutral. Whether it is good or bad, it all depends on the occasion. What will determine its effect is not natural but ambiguous.

Say you hear a thud in the room across. You have two options: investigate or stay put. Now, if you decide to investigate, many things can happen. It could be something as simple as a box that fell down. But it could also be a Hound, already eyeing its next prey. That is the duality of curiosity.

The Backrooms is a place of mystery. It is not home, but it looks eerily similar. Many aspects of it you've already seen. Halls, bedrooms, cities, fields...

Yet, the curiosity one feels here is stronger than it is naturally. One of the aspects that has carried on to this reality is outer space. At this point, the line between liminal and paranormal starts to blur. You see things you've never seen before, even if they were always right above you, in plain sight. Yet the sensation carries on; you are not safe.

When suddenly the surface starts to redden, oxygen levels deplete, and a searing sensation fills your body, your first instinct is to report to the base. But as things become futile, your options run out. You may scream, but no noise will come out, as air is needed for sound to travel. The vacuum devours your voice completely, and slowly, you realize in your last moments that curiosity is a trickster. A cruel one, too.

As your suit scorches and your helmet becomes charred, only contrasted by the red, immolated pulp within, you decide perhaps dying mauled by a Hound is not as bad as it sounds.

APOLLO-∞
Curiosity. Infinity.

GROUP OF INTEREST

PRIMARY GROUP TYPE
Exploratory

Influence: 6/10

Hostility: 4/10

Organization: 9/10

Apollo-∞, or colloquially, Apollo-INFINITE, is an exploratory group dedicated to the documentation of outer space-like levels. It is known for its technical and scientific expertise regarding outer space and the exploration and documentation of it. Apollo-∞ functions on multiple directorates focused primarily on technological innovation and field science aimed to manage and sustain long-term "space" exploration within the Backrooms. Currently, Apollo-∞ hosts over 2,653 operatives.

Apollo-∞ consistently holds strategical partnerships with other exploratory or informational groups to maintain key communication with other colonies and outposts and receive live status reports from other bases, levels, or key transportation devices. As a result, Apollo-∞  leverages its technological prowess not just for space exploration, but to maintain good income via exporting materials, electronics or information.

Apollo-∞ was founded in March 24, 1967 by the Apollos, 3 men in astronaut suits that handle and manage Apollo-∞ to this day, but remain mostly remain elusive from the public. Apollo-∞, then known as the Backrooms Space Anomalies, was initially restricted to scientific documentation on known space-like levels in the Backrooms sourced from The Database and M.E.G.. However, the scientific knowledge and information within the group allowed it to quickly thrive by significantly educating wanderers and creating a knowledgeable and competent community that specialized in both exploration and technology.

Due to Apollo-∞'s specialized mechanisms and gadgets, it is strongly involved in documenting and extracting resources from multiple levels beyond those just space-themed. As such, the group holds a highly strict demand for skill and experience among all of its members and, despite its rather neutral stance, is known for putting high economic and territorial pressure on other colonies or bases rivaling or even just located near their outposts.

Phenomenon 35-A.title: Phenomenon 35-A
Phenomenon 35-A.description:
Phenomenon 35-A, better known as Jovial Syndrome, and those affected by it being known as Entity 67-A or Jovials, is a rare condition affecting those infected by the Sanguine Festivus virus. Its main symptom is an uncontrollable, permanent feeling of happiness at all times, and limited retainment and even recovery of memories from pre infection. Due to this, it has been widely seen as the opposite of the wretched cycle, which has proven this somewhat true, as both target the brain and cause overwhelming emotion to override critical judgement.

 Unlike its more famous counterpart, there is no reliable way of predicting who will be subjected to this phenomenon, other than being infected with Sanguine Festivus and not being inside of level fun. What is known is that it is exceedingly rare, with only around one in 500 victims of the virus ever undergoing Jovial Syndrome. Due to this rarity, many victims believe themselves to be blessed and rewarded for past actions, giving them a sense of superiority over other beings. This narrative is especially popular with The Halloween Party, who claim that those with the condition have been personally selected by God to be given a second chance, should they abide by his wishes to punish the other humans of the backrooms for their sins. This has worked extremely well for the group, and is one of the main reasons that Jovials  in the group exhibit no mercy, despite their painful, and often sadistic, killing sprees: they genuinely believe that they are superior to all others.

Phenomenon 3.title: Phenomenon 3
Phenomenon 3.description:
Phenomenon 3, also known as Antigravity, is the largely theoretical and poorly understood anomalous force responsible for manipulating gravity within most of the Backrooms to match that of the Frontrooms (9.807 m/s²) despite the wildly varying (and in most cases infinite) mass of most Levels, defying the known laws of gravity. While this concept was previously considered a side effect of the Backrooms' non-Euclidean nature, extensive research of static megastructures that should by all means be unable to support their own weight has warranted the classification of Antigravity as its own phenomena.

Backrooms researchers were presented with quite the challenge when initially attempting to explain these anomalies and exceptions to known science. Eventually the term "Antigravity" was coined as a temporary explanation, being adopted by the general public for use in casual conversations, similar to the term "anti-matter" used to explain cosmic gravitational anomalies in the Frontrooms. As of now, it is unknown exactly how Antigravity functions or what 'purpose' it serves besides closely replicating the conditions of the Frontrooms. However, most researchers can agree that Antigravity is its own force and, despite all appearances, normal gravity is completely absent from the Backrooms. Antigravity merely creates the illusion of regular gravity with a standard 'downward' force towards the perceived 'floor' of each Level (such as Level 0's carpet or Level 1's asphalt), but the other laws of gravity that would normally condense or collapse said Levels do not apply.

The all-encompassing theoretical nature of Antigravity means it is present by default in every Level, unless stated otherwise. It, along with the non-Euclidean nature of many Levels, may also account for the consistent failure of navigation devices such as compasses, which normally rely on the Earth's magnetic field.

Others have theorized that Antigravity conforms to the wanderer's perception; that is, if one believes they are in an ordinary room, the gravitational force of the environment will match that, versus a region such as Level 24 having gravitational forces suited to its extraterrestrial environment (similar to the idea of quantum particles switching states when under direct observation). This idea has yet to be disproven, given that the megastructural scale of most Levels has only been documented by drones thus far, and not by human eyes. Of course, if this were the case, any wanderer who personally observed enough of a Level's structure at once to understand the true scale of it would be immediately killed by the subsequent adjustment of Antigravity destroying said Level.

If Antigravity were suddenly replaced by gravity native to the Frontrooms, the whole of the Backrooms would likely collapse on itself instantly (and infinitely), creating what one expert compared to a "reverse big bang" in which all matter in the realm would attempt to concentrate into a single point. No life could possibly survive this theoretical occurrence. Antigravity may also be responsible for previously unexplained spatial anomalies, such as Void Objects being suspended by nothing or a consistent Backrooms-wide failure of falling humans to reach terminal velocity despite falling for extended periods of time, such as while no-clipping.

Phenomenon 303.description:

Phenomenon 303, known alternatively as Anomaliae Corporis or Anatomical Anomalies, is an umbrella term for a variety of xeniatrological anomalies which may rarely affect wanderers upon their first entry of the Backrooms, or — in the case of Wakeborn individuals — at the time of their birth. These peculiarities manifest in many different shapes and forms, many of which do not bear the least semblance to each other; the only similarity is that all of these oddities warp, transform or alter the victim's anatomy in a certain fashion, whether for good or for ill, though typically being unexplainable by orthodox medicine. Each individual instance of Anomaliae Corporis has been so minor, or occurred in such a little amount of people, that they have been determined to all be grouped under the name of a singular phenomenon.

In recent years, attention has been drawn to Anomaliae Corporis in its various forms under the Anomalia Awareness Research Initiative, and various medicinal divisions like the Department of Biomedical Research of the Backrooms Red Cross and the An Apple a Day medical team of the Major Explorer Group are conducting ongoing research regarding this phenomenon. The Backrooms Health Practitioner Authority has dedicated much intensive research toward investigation of the causes, effects and possible cures of these anomalies, with hope that those affected with the phenomenon's more malignant strains may someday be alleviated of their pitiful conditions.

These peculiarities manifest in many different shapes and forms, many of which do not bear the least semblance to each other; the only similarity is that all of these oddities warp, transform or alter the victim's anatomy in a certain fashion, whether for good or for ill, though typically being unexplainable by orthodox medicine. Each individual instance of Anomaliae Corporis has been so minor, or occurred in such a little amount of people, that they have been determined to all be grouped under the name of a singular phenomenon.

In recent years, attention has been drawn to Anomaliae Corporis in its various forms under the Anomalia Awareness Research Initiative, and various medicinal divisions like the Department of Biomedical Research of the Backrooms Red Cross and the An Apple a Day medical team of the Major Explorer Group are conducting ongoing research regarding this phenomenon. The Backrooms Health Practitioner Authority has dedicated much intensive research toward investigation of the causes, effects and possible cures of these anomalies, with hope that those affected with the phenomenon's more malignant strains may someday be alleviated of their pitiful conditions.

The Trailblazers.title: The Trailblazers
The Trailblazers.description:
The Trailblazers

The Trailblazers are an exploratory group primarily aimed for uncovering hidden dimensions beside the main three. They were founded in Level 1 in November 2006 after the Multi-Dimension Theory surfaced through a post on Backchat.

They expand their influence by handing out promotional leaflets, popularizing the theory. This allowed them to increase the number of members by three times to ~1000 throughout two years.

They collect abundant resources such as Almond Water, food, and water through communal effort every day. Therefore, they have stored a lot of sustenance for daily uses and emergency situations.

They are hated by other groups in the Backrooms due to their heterodox beliefs. However, they hold a politically neutral stance and refuse to interfere into other groups' policies or conflicts, or respond to their resentment. Therefore, they can become stronger and stronger steadily without disturbance.

The Trailblazers are a self-directed group, but informal leadership still exists in their colonies, maintaining the stability and unity of the group. Despite this, they have established specialized teams dedicated to their respective purposes in order to allocate workload. Positions are filled through self-recommendation, attracting skillful people to join.

The Trailblazers have great influence among ordinary people, but they do not have good relations with other groups due to their unorthodox beliefs. Even so, other groups do not care much about them as they have never been involved in any conflicts.

The Trailblazers had been treated hatefully by the M.E.G. since their founding, but their hostility died down after a few months because of the lack of conflicts.

The Backroom Colonists remain majority neutral towards the Trailblazers. However, some of their members have shown interest in the goal of discovering a new dimension and even donate supplies to support them.

Other groups like the B.I.F. either dismiss the presence of the Trailblazers or show distaste towards them. Nevertheless, no conflicts are fought and the enmity has abated.

The Starting Point is the headquarter of the group, spanning 1 km² in Level 1 and housing ~500 people. It serves as the main residence and storage area for group members. It is chosen due to its easy access to exits such as The Hub and several more.

The Secured Point is a colony established in Level 4 in January 2007, sheltering ~400 people. It is chosen as a major resource supply because of its plenitude of food and water, its safety, and its strange abundance of exits to the Void, which are believed to be useful for seeking other dimensions.

The Separated Point is a small outpost consisting of ~100 members, which is formed in March 2008. They are a part of the Megacolony in Level 399, allowing them to spread their influence to more people.The progress of finding a new dimension is slow, until...

Phenomenon 90.title: Phenomenon 90
Phenomenon 90.description:
THE TRAILBLAZERS' LIEPresented by the M.E.G.We have had enough of the massive disinformation regarding the dimension debate. The Multi-Dimension Theory originated from a spam post in Backchat with no proof whatsoever, yet we failed to silence or delete it in time. Further supports to the theory will not be tolerated unless with proper evidence, and we are ready to debunk this once and for all.>CONTINUEPhenomenon 90Directionality

PHENOMENON REPORT (EXERPT)Directionality is a phenomenon that affects all levels in the Backrooms. Unlike most phenomena, wanderers can activate this phenomenon at will by performing a simple action explained below.

It is found that if one follows a wall in any direction in a level for at least thirty minutes without meeting another person, it would gain new properties never ever seen before. The same applies to sublevels or anomalous levels.

A photo of the leftside version of Level 0 is provided as an example. The walls can be seen vandalized with gibberish graffiti, differing from the clean yellow walls in the normal level. The floor tiles are arranged in a checkerboard pattern, unlike the carpeted floor in the original.

This phenomenon is likely a result of strong space distortion, as it forcibly changes the layout and appearance of a level, despite the affected level not being known to possess space distortion, such as Level 1.

The directions determine the new properties the level gains. It is currently unknown why this happens. Those areas are named "Level # L" and "Level # R" respectively.

PHENOMENON REPORT (EXEPRT) (CONT.)Level 1 L

Level 1 L is the leftside version of Level 1. It is a Chinese-styled underground car park. Compared to Level 1, the interior is painted slightly whiter and the air is warmer. There are thin lengthy hallways accessible through doors, linking every room together.

At random intervals, balloons can suddenly rush into the hallways from an unknown source, making travelling annoyingly difficult. If one tries to pop them, more appears at a faster rate, possibly trapping the person inside, thus suffocating them.

False Puddles and Paintings continue to exist in this level as they are exclusive entities in Level 1, but no Facelings or Dullers have been found in this special area, leaving Hounds, Clumps, and Deathmoths the only generic entities to reside there.

Level 0 R
Level 0 R is the rightside version of Level 0. It looks almost identical to Level 0, but it has an unstable nature. Object are sometimes "convulsing" or "missing textures".

Entities like smilers, hounds, etc. are present in this location. They are found to be more aggressive than usual. They do not hesitate to attack when they see a target, no matter their physical capabilities.

PHENOMENON REPORT (EXERPT) (CONT.)This certainly is a pretty interesting phenomenon, but it has no practical value. Major properties of the affected level remain roughly the same, no new entrances or exits would be generated because of this.

Further investigations might be initiated if time and resources allow, but this is solid proof that the dimensions claimed by the Trailblazers are false.

"Ok, now this is getting ridiculous.""Are you serious?""Don't talk to me.""..."Somewhere in Level 399..."Heard the Midrooms got invalidated by M.E.G., what happened?""Really? I haven't heard about it, how did you know that?""Umm... guys?""We are here to deliver some bad news.""Oh? What is it?""A few months ago, M.E.G. accused us for faking the Midrooms."And now, they are saying that the Leftrooms and the Rightrooms aren't real!""WHAT?!""You must be joking.""Nope, take a look at this news article, along with the attached report.""...""...""It looks... pretty bad.""Where did you come from...""What. What. What. What. Wha...""This report... seems very targeted.""What can we do with it?""...""I don't know how...""Those pesky meggies, always ruining everything. I despise them a lot.""Is there anything we can do?""I afraid...""There is no way. This has to be fake.""The dimensions are real to me!""That's what our group is about!""..."???

Department of Hyperreality.title: Department of Hyperreality
Department of Hyperreality.description:
The Department of Hyperreality is a covert division within the Kauer Research Organization whose objective is to document the existence of anomalies that challenge our understanding of reality.

This branch, unlike other agencies or research groups, places greater emphasis on abstract ideas such as metaphysics, logic, and narrative.

The internal structure of the Department of Hyperreality is sufficiently regulated and organized to allow for self-sustainability, as the division depends solely on the investments provided by the Kauer Research Organization, such as technology, shelter, and salaries.

Although the division operates as a covert research agency, certain exceptions are permitted. For example, the Department of Hyperreality collaborates with the Division of Research in Cosmology and Metaphysics in order to exchange ideas, execute projects, and accelerate the development of research.

CJNF.title: CJNF
CJNF.displayTitle: > The Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun
CJNF.description:
The Celebrants' Jamboree of New Fun (CJNF), also alluded to as the Celebrants' Jamboree or New Fun (not to be confused with the sub-level), is a group in the Backrooms which proclaims itself as an independent sovereign state. It is located in New Fun, with an estimated population of 37,200 non-human citizens known as celebrants. The nation was founded by Thaumas Blake, otherwise known as the Party Creator, and establishes itself as "the truer, purer form of the Partygoer Jamboree", promoting freedom of speech and action amongst its denizens, whilst simultaneously maintaining a simple governmental system and legislative and medical services to prevent the descent of the state into complete anarchy. Its government is purported as a parliamentary laissez-faire jamboree.

New Fun settlements have been established all across New Fun, with its capital and largest colony, New Fun City, boasting a population of approximately 5,420 celebrants. At present, there are around 150 known cities in New Fun under the jurisdiction of New Fun, as well as many smaller towns and municipalities; however, outside their native sub-level, New Fun bases are far and few between, and only four are known to exist, two of which are stated to be located somewhere in Level Fun.

Despite the insistence of the New Fun people that they should be markedly distinguished from the partygoers, most polities within the Backrooms still treat the Celebrants' Jamboree and the Partygoer Jamboree in a similar political light, fundamentally deeming them both "terrorist groups which promote anarchism as a source of unrestricted freedom and happiness". Exceptions to this general external perception of the group include the Party Crashers and the Hallowe'en Party, who are both organisation concerned deeply with the Partygoer Jamboree and thus have made distinctions between the two groups. Many celebrants consider themselves to be a marginalised community.

Importantly, the Celebrants' Jamboree is in possession of the obfestivir drug, which they use to convert partygoers and other victims of the Sanguine Festivus virus into celebrants, thus adding to their ranks. Due to their unwillingness to release this medicine to the wider world, they have inadvertently become the enemies of many groups in the Backrooms.
CJNF.lead:
EX CREPUSCULO AURORA.
"From dusk cometh dawn."

Level 680.title: Level 680
Level 680.description:
Level 680 consists of an expansive city centered around a singular road presumably from the state of Russia within The Frontrooms. The city is covered in an eternal, thick layer of snow, and a constant mild snowstorm is prevalent throughout the level; some areas are snowed in more than others, although it is impossible to find a clear area. The average temperature of the level is -8°F, with recorded lows going as cold as -38°F.

The snowed-in town in permeated by a complex of roads and bridges of concrete, with some having collapsed into the snow. While technically traversable, it is unadvised, as serious slipping hazards arise from walking on the iced and snowed-over bridges.

Level 680's geometry can be described as cylindrical; if one walks down the central road within the level, they will continue onwards infinitely. Meanwhile, attempting to far enough away from the highway will result in one returning back to it. The level has been measured to be around 1.8 miles in width, and is theoretically infinite in length.
Level 680.lead: Level 680, known colloquially as 'Bezdna', is the 681st level of the Backrooms, and presumably an extension of Level 445.

entrances.text: In Level 445, traveling down a dirt road lined with trees will seamlessly transition the level into Level 680.
In Level 245, snowdrifts may randomly appear, noclipping into the snow will lead one to Level 680.

In Level 173, often in close proximity to exits to Level 445, patches of ice can be found floating. Interacting with them will cause a wanderer to black out and wake up on a bench in a Snegozaderzhateli.
In Level 690, snow may occasionally spawn atop a bridge, jumping into it will lead here.

In Level 258, staying inside a building at night may lead to the Gigakhrushchevka in Level 680. This transition is seamless and often goes unnoticed until the wanderer acknowledges the apparent size of the building, or when they leave the Gigakhrushchevka.

exits.text: Traveling deep inside the Gigakhrushchevka and finding a glitched mass of objects may lead to Level 404, alternatively leading to The Broken.
Level 684's entrances manifest as weathered paintings of, or advertisements for, image galleries or museums, hanging in the Gigakhrushchevka
Waiting too long at a Snegozaderzhateli will condemn you to waiting, forever.
Reminiscing of better times while walking the Gigakhrushchevka may segue into Level 193, Level 201 or Level 231.
Rarely, finding a subway entrance along the roads may lead to Level 95.
Extremely rarely, garage doors attached to the sides of Khrushchyovkas will lead to the garages of Level 158.

PERCEPTION.title: PERCEPTION
PERCEPTION.displayTitle: P E R C E P T I O N .
PERCEPTION.description:
- Overview -

----

"P E R C E P T I O N." is described as a phenomenon occurring only at levels mentally threatening, although still very rare besides. The phenomenon only occurs if one was aware of the backrooms before entering, as for it to occur, a base concept of the backrooms is needed.

The phenomenon itself is defined to be a simulated version of The Backrooms itself. When about to happen, the wanderer experiences a migraine, and they pass out. When they wake up, they typically will find themselves in Level 0, or any other starting level, if they imagined the backrooms to have such.

Individuals might experience several symptoms before the migraine, such as an unnerving feeling of dread, which doesn't go away, a numbness in the head, or a feeling of nostalgia centered towards Level 0. Once the symptoms show or the migraine starts, there is no way to stop it. Unfortunately, the chances of a wanderer waking up after passing out are very rare, and it has been recorded only 4 times.

The 4 occurrences have been caused by an implication of an exit in the wanderer's mind. One of the most interviewed individuals about the phenomenon had always imagined Level 398 not to be a level but The Frontrooms, so when he entered the level, he awoke.

Since the phenomenon occurs inside an individual's mind, they are perceived as completely knocked out outside. Although many attempts have been made, it seems to be impossible to wake them up, and it can only be caused if the one escapes their version of the backrooms themselves.
----

  - History -

----
This phenomenon shares a concept created in 1921 by Bolesław Sieradzki. The concept states that since the backrooms are imperfect, mentally dangerous levels are more likely to serve fatal fails during the access of one's mind. This is considered the first study regarding this phenomenon due to its incredible similarity.

In 2012, the phenomenon was first discovered, where a man was found on Level 78 completely passed out. Brought back to a M.E.G. outpost, the man awakened. When interviewed, he showed clear confusion, claiming he never passed out and simply teleported here after escaping. This began occurring more often, and in 2013, it was studied and officially recognized as a phenomenon.
----

  - Theoretical Explanation -

----

A student in neuroscience and psychology named "Jari Aki" had created a theory based on the phenomenon to explain exactly why it would occur and how levels affect the human mind.

As stated by him, mentally threatening levels access the human mind similarly to a cybersecurity exploit type called "Man in the Middle", or MITM for short. This means the level accesses neurons responsible for short-term memory, to manipulate or decide what to do next.

However, the levels method isn't perfect, as there is no pattern it can follow. This means the level can often go "off-track" and accidentally access the neurons responsible for long-term memory instead. This confuses the algorithm, making it try to go back to reality unsuccessfully, leading to the phenomenon.
