Phenomenon 499.title: Phenomenon 499
Phenomenon 499.description:
----Phenomenon 499 affects all forms of documentation, both physical and digital, regardless of how much security they have. The phenomenon cannot function under direct line of sight of the document if it is physical. However, when a document is under constant manual surveillance, the surveyors will gain an urge to look away, eventually overriding whatever training or instructions they may have. Digital surveillance equipment will lapse for roughly one frame, during which the phenomenon takes place. Digital documentation is edited in sync with any other physical copies. Digital documents can be changed by the phenomenon at any time.

The phenomenon will pick random pieces of information, ranging from dates and names to entire paragraphs, and obscure it in some way. On digital documents, it will usually redact the sections or password-protect them, and on physical documentations it will in some way become unintelligible, whether through redaction or physical wear-and-tear. The phenomenon seems to not be limited to any one piece of information, and can affect the same piece of documentation multiple times, leading to certain older documents being completely redacted.

When encountering affected information, wanderers will commence an overwhelming desire to locate the original information. This typically manifests as an urge to sort through separate documents to piece together the missing information, as well as interrogating the original writers of the documents to find more info. However, this often spirals into violent actions in an attempt to discover the legitimate information. This causes severe and harmful mental effects to the affected.

Given a prolonged period of time in this state, the affected individual's mind will begin degrading, with neural pathways seemingly disintegrating into thin air. Memories held by the wanderer will begin to have the document prevalent in them, motivating them to search further. This degradation slowly spreads to more important parts of the brain, which when left unchecked can cause fatality. The deceased wanderer's brain activity will continue for a long amount of time not dissimilar to that of Stage V Roth Syndrome, even if severe damage is sustained. This prolonged brain activity will cease est. 1 year after death.
Phenomenon 499.lead: Phenomenon 499, known colloquially as 'REDACTION' or 'INFORMATION EIGENGRAU', is a major phenomenon that manifests for any and all forms of documentation.

Level 828.title: Level 828
Level 828.description:
Level 828, colloquially referred to as "The Fortune of Misfortune", resembles a series of infinitely expanding, interconnected chambers made of steel, which are roughly 30 m² each and have a hexagonal shape. These chambers have lights hanging from the ceiling on chains, and said lights grow dimmer the further one strays from the center. Each chamber is connected by short metallic tunnels that have no ores in them. All chambers within Level 828 are filled with various precious minerals, primarily Amethyst, Diamond, Lapis Lazuli, and Gold. Each of these minerals can be found under diverse circumstances and conditions.Other, lesser-known minerals have been reported to be present in the far reaches of Level 828, such as Ruby, Sapphire, and Bronze.

While the minerals and ores of Level 828 can be found in diverse locations and under various conditions, pattern analysis has identified specific areas where they are most commonly found. The analysis is listed below:

*Amethyst deposits are often found in isolated chambers where little to no other ores are present. This mineral is primarily present in the form of geodes and raw ores and can easily injure human limbs due to the sharpness of its form and materials.

*Diamonds appear scattered throughout all chambers in most cases. This noteworthy and valuable ore is found in the shape of a polished gem.

*Lapis Lazuli is an extremely rare mineral within Level 828, being difficult to find and appearing in minimal quantities. This mineral is found within the level's expanse either in the form of powder or jewelry, and has a high trading value due to its rarity not solely in Level 828 but also throughout the Wake.

*Gold is the most commonly found valuable ore in Level 828; it has a chance of appearing in every chamber and is typically found in high quantities. This ore is found in a multitude of forms, including but not limited to coins, rings, and ingots.

It is said that instances of Object 1 in the form of bottles can occasionally be found in the chambers of Level 828, though an official publication confirming these rumors is yet to be released.

The overall temperature of Level 828's chambers fluctuates between 30 and 50 degrees Celsius (86 and 122 degrees Fahrenheit); however, some chambers have natural heating systems that can increase the temperature to 162ºC, an exceptionally high temperature for the human body to handle.

Lastly, Level 828 has been deemed stable, with little to no environmentally altering phenomena such as Phenomenon 14. However, it is presumed that this renders exits few in number and difficult to locate.
Level 828.lead: Level 828 is the 829th ore of the Anomaly Mine.

entrances.text: Referencing the discovery method used by Enrico, no-clipping through a steel wall or surface in Level 242 (or on occasion Level 906 and Level 960) will lead to Level 828.

exits.text: No-clipping through the ground of an Empty Chamber may send one to Level 322.

Level 345.title: Level 345
Level 345.description:
Level 345, also known as The Dreamcore Town, resembles a peculiar, quiet town, perpetually stuck in a late spring afternoon. The sky is unusually clear, colored a faint cyan-green tint.  There are never any changes to the overall brightness, nor is there any real sense of time passing.

Early 2010s suburban houses flank the sides of the streets and sidewalks, with one house roughly every 0.02km. Each house has a lawn, consisting of two separate plots of well-maintained grass, measured to take up a surface area of roughly 0.15km and 0.08km, respectively. Both of these plots are separated by a clean, concrete driveway. Flanking the sides of the plots is a basic mangrove wooden fence, about 4 meters tall. The front porches of the houses lack any decorations, nor any unique paint jobs. Everything looks new, lacking any signs of damage, wear, or abandonment. Likewise, there is a complete absence of any residents or general intelligent life.

Despite looking peaceful and nostalgic, the area appears subtly unnatural.
Level 345.lead:
M.E.G. ARCHIVAL DATABASE
    LEVEL 345 FILE RECOVERED
    [01] Recovering fragmented suburban record...
    [02] Verifying psychological hazard notes...
    [03] Access granted.
    DISPLAYING LEVEL

M.E.G. Database Login Form

Insert Username

Insert Passphrase

Password valid.

Welcome, !

  Play Audio

Level 345  is the 346th level of the Backrooms discovered by M.E.G. on May 18th, 2011.

----

entrances.text: Staring at a town photograph for an extended period in Level 4, which may cause nearby walls to shift into the architecture of this level.
Following the sound of a distant school bell in Level 11 until you find a door that should not be there.

exits.text: Walking into direct sunlight until a visual whiteout occurs may cause one to noclip into Level 89.
Entering a child’s closet and closing the door behind oneself may result in emergence within Level 320.
Falling asleep inside a furnished bedroom may result in awakening in Level 94.
Touching a small, round turquoise toy ball toy replica of a wireframe sphere found within the residential buildings in this level will cause an immediate transition to Level 346.

Level 4.4.title: Level 4.4
Level 4.4.description:
Level 4.4 resembles a large complex of indeterminable size, with its architecture being comparable to that of a typical office building. The level itself does not contain any artificial light sources, instead being lit up through unknown means. The defining feature of Level 4.4 that sets it apart from Level 4 is the gory, dilapidated appearance of several hallways and areas. While wandering through Level 4.4, the stench of rotten flesh could be felt within the air, causing severe feelings of dread and anxiety. In extreme cases, these can lead to sudden collapses or even death due to critical heart failure.

Doors in Level 4.4 mostly lead to nowhere, or just an empty, bloodied room with no equipment whatsoever. At random intervals, parts of the ceiling may collapse, leaving behind debris and making navigation much more difficult. It is also important to watch for traces of predatory doors, as they regularly manifest themselves as ordinary ones before attacking their prey. Another key to survival is to avoid barging in recklessly when encountering restricted or barricaded doors, because it is likely that someone is already in there and ready to attack a traveler at any point. Given the unpredictable nature of doors, people are advised to stay away from them completely to deter an unexpected clash from ensuing.

The walls in Level 4.4 appear to be covered in various artistic depictions made out of blood. There is no confirmation as to why those are visually perceptible, but it is heavily implied that victims who either presumably succumbed to the level's hazardous nature or went insane drew all of the symbols by using limbs of the deceased to achieve this goal. This perhaps partly explains why displaced human organs can be found in every corner of Level 4.4, as the bodies of those who are murdered seem to be mutilated to acquire such assets.

Interestingly, Level 4.4 is filled with an abundance of lockers, most of which, however, cannot be pried or opened without keys to their corresponding padlocks. These keys are scattered across the level, usually being found in some certain rooms and unlocked drawers. Many fortunate survivors have reported being drawn to these lockers, often feeling the need to discover what is hidden beneath the surface, mainly because of the survival instinct to look for supplies or just odd curiosity in general. Further inspection indicates the disturbingly common presence of corpses stuffed inside these containers, dismembered into multiple individual parts.

Anyone who stays in Level 4.4 for longer than six hours will begin to experience visual and auditory hallucinations; reflections include but are not limited to their past self, their archnemesis, and their most traumatic memories. The level's psychological effects seem to be the primary catalyst for driving these factors. If a wanderer does not retain or have any memories of the mentioned, such an event would luckily not occur. Anyone who does not have enough willpower for resistance is prone to becoming erratic and unpredictable. Eventually, they will try to kill every person present in the level. In case there is no one in sight for extended periods of time, they will turn to self-harm or even attempt to separate anatomical features from their own bodies, using any tools that are capable of doing so.

entrances.text: Noclipping into drawings displaying blood red skies in Level 4 or any of its sub-levels leads to Level 4.4.
Trying to inflict harm on oneself within the "Torture Chambers" area of Level 14 may send one to Level 4.4.
If unlucky enough, any individual with their name written on the gravestones of Level 144 gets banished to Level 4.4.

exits.text: An altar is located in the central area of Level 4.4. Inside holds five separate jars, and when five different hearts are placed inside each, a wanderer falls unconscious and wakes up later in Level 4.
Although highly advised against, The Smile Room can be accessed should one enter a door with teeth covering its sides.
In rare cases, trying to jump out the windows in Level 4.4 results in arriving at Level 236.
One may come across a childhood photo of them with their closest friends or accomplices. Staring at it for longer than five minutes teleports them to Level 237.

Phenomenon 400.title: Phenomenon 400
Phenomenon 400.description:
✵

Bleeding Stars

I

OVERVIEW

Bleeding Stars, among other names, is the colloquial term given to Phenomenon 400, an exceptionally rare phenomenon of the Backrooms in which a celestial body within a level is temporarily replaced with a black hole. While this is typically the sun-or-moon-equivalent of the level, it can be any notable object within the level's sky, as shown by the occurrence of this phenomenon on Sky Ferris Wheels. The phenomenon consists of the spatial object, titled the "Wounded", slowly melting away as it rises and sets, until approximately one week later, where instead of the Wounded rising, it is instead a black hole, sometimes called the "Clot". The Clot has little correlation to the Wounded, typically being closer to the level's floor than the Wounded was and having wildly varying mass. While the Clot is in the sky, the level will experience significant gravitational changes, often negating the effects of Antigravity, though the severity of these changes depends on the distance and size of the Clot. Once the Clot fully sets, the celestial object will reappear in the same state as before the occurrence of the phenomenon, though the level itself will still maintain structural damage from the gravitational changes.

Beyond the fact the phenomenon only occurs in levels containing a sky (including a sun/moon-proxy and/or constellations), current documentations of the phenomena show little consistency in the manifestation of Phenomena 400, with the lowest time period between reports being 10 months and the highest at 61 years. In addition, the cause of the phenomena is yet to be confirmed with some M.E.G. researchers chalking it up to instability in the 'skies' of levels.

II

PHASES

As implied earlier, the phenomena has several distinct phases. This article will only cover 3 phases, even though some argue that Haemorrhage should be split up into 2 smaller phases. These phases- in order- are:

:Haemorrhage
:Rupture
:Scars

Each phase marks a drastic shift in the phenomenon's behaviour. Haemorrhage signifies the beginning, where the first noticeable effects happen to the Wounded and the sky. Phase 2 marks the climax of the phenomenon; when Bleeding Stars is at its deadliest. Scars is the aftermath of the onset, focusing on the lasting effects to the level.

II - I

HAEMORRHAGE

The first phase of Bleeding Stars is "Haemorrhage". This phase does not have any significant effects on the level itself, primarily being visual changes to the sky of the affected level.

An image of a Wounded moon, taken in the Crystal Fields.

On the first day the celestial body becomes Wounded, a singular, large explosive sound will echo across the entire level. The blast clocks in at ~115dB, producing notable strain on the listener's eardrums (though never shown to be permanently damaging). Curiously, the soundwave does not diminish with distance; it maintains full intensity and volume even though it is covering near-infinite or even non-euclidean spans of land. Based off the research conducted on the Wounded in Level 387, the sound is likely created by a sudden release of internal pressure within the spatial body.

From this point onwards, every time the Wounded becomes visible, it will appear to be bleeding. That is to say, as the moon, star or planet continues its movement or orbital path, it leaves behind an incandescent trail of superheated plasma . The density and volume of the material released increases as Haemorrhage progresses, causing the surrounding sky to become increasingly more bright. This, paired with the Wounded 's increased luminosity itself, can sometimes be blinding to a wanderer even if not directly stared at. The Wounded 's "blood" rarely moves, only fading slowly or breaking away into smaller globules. However, sometimes, the plasma will not dissipate, but rather stay stranded in mid-air, eventually forming a crack-like structure in the sky. This structure appears to be a side-effect of the phenomenon rather than a part of it, due to the fact that the crack remains visible for several months or years after the 'Rupture' has concluded.

An image of a Wounded Sun, captured by the Cameraman in an unknown level.

During Haemorrhage, there are also some technological changes. For one, radios, walkie-talkies and sometimes more advanced electronics like terminals are slightly muffled or glitchy from static interference. Another change is the malfunctioning of anything requiring magnetic fields. Compasses, magnetometers and certain LPS devices all break within a level suffering from Phenomenon 400. This is believed to be in due part because of the ejected plasma containing large amounts of iron that could create powerful but random magnetic fields.  However, both of these changes will only minorly affect wanderers.

Haemorrhage takes place for approximately a week before the Rupture commences, though the exact timeframe varies from level-to-level since most levels with a dual-celestial cycle have differing day/night durations to each other and reality. Furthermore, as Phase 2 encroaches, the amount of time the Wounded remains in the sky increases substantially. As a result, on more dangerous levels, this phase could pose a far deadlier threat as it may lead to entities such as Smilers, Hounds and many others hunting for prey for longer.

If a wanderer finds themselves in a level where Haemorrhage is ensuing, the best course of action is to leave as quick as possible. The possible danger posed by Phase 2 will almost certainly outweigh any desire to explore, document or reside within the level at that given moment. If possible, it is also recommended to alert us or any other group of the fact that the phenomenon is occurring so we may protect other wanderers and possibly conduct research on the phenomenon too.

⠀

II - II

RUPTURE

The second, and arguably most notorious, phase of Phenomenon 400 is the "Rupture", sometimes also referred to as "God's Wounds" or "Occlusion" . During this phase, the Wounded is replaced into a black hole, or Clot, causing significant gravitational fluctuations across the entirety of the level.

Another Cameraman-sourced image, this one depicted a rising Clot.

An image of the largest recorded Clot, photographed in 1989 where Flowers Bloom.

Another image of a Clot captured in the Arabian Desert. Note the presence of debris in mid-air.

As mentioned in {II - I}, this phase typically commences a week after the phenomenon as a whole begins. By this point, the trail left by the Wounded will be substantially long- almost trailing the entire sky- and there will likely be a multitude of cracks present in the sky. However, after the Wounded sinks beyond the "horizon", its next return (as the Clot) will mark the onset of Occlusion.

As the Clot rises, immediate changes in the level can be seen/detected. The technological glitches seen during Haemorrhage are significantly amplified: radios and the like only relay loud static noise, while terminals and the installed BackOS are far more glitchy, often malfunctioning to the point of inoperability. In addition, magnetic-field reliant devices entirely malfunction within the affected level, though other devices such as HDDs and keycards have also been known to break during the phenomenon.

The Clot itself appears to be a regular stellar black hole, though all instances of the Clot also have an accretion disk. This disk, especially at the magnitude that most Clots possess, should not be possible, as only a handful of asteroids and other objects have ever been documented in Backrooms history and certainly far fewer would be available for accretion by the Clot. It is unknown if the Clot has similar effects to that of a real black hole, such as spaghettification, though evidence such as the distortion of light about the Clot and the gravitational effects on the level and other objects strongly suggests that (at least) most of its physical effects are functionally identical.

The Clot will follow the typical orbital trajectory of it's Wounded original counterpart, the only difference being the substantial decrease in speed (though this was stated in {II - I}). However, as the Clot moves, small ejecta will be released from the accretion disk of the black hole. These ejecta-labelled  as coagulates- are small incandescent orbs or steams of plasma that have been released into the surrounding area by the Clot. Rarely do this ejecta reach the surface, due to the fact that they often dissipate when entering the atmosphere of the level. As coagulates fall, they rapidly expel an enormous amount of heat, which can cause anything, such as people and edifices, in a nearby vicinity to be incinerated if the ejecta does manage to reach/get near to the surface of the level.

However, the most well-known impact of the Clot as it moves through the sky are it's gravitational changes. During the Rupture,  gravity within the affected level will fluctuate, getting either far weaker (=> Objects floating ), or slightly stronger (=>Objects being crushed). In both cases, these changes will not effect present wanderers or entities aswell as integral parts of the level's composition, such as the floor. However, objects- especially unanchored objects, though even entire edifices- will be effected. Though objects will be almost immediately lifted or crushed, buildings about to be pulled or compressed are indicated through rapid vibrations and formations of cracks in the walls and floor. Though wanderers are unaffected by the spacetime warping, they can be indirectly effected by being lifted by objects they are on, or being crushed by objects falling from above/the roof above collapsing. In addition, wanderers may experience severe vertigo or nausea. Due to the gravity of the Clot, the atmosphere of a level is significantly changed: winds have been known to reach substantially high speeds; storms, particularly tornadoes, have been to form across the entire level; slight temperature and pressure shifts were noted on Sky Ferris Wheels, and thus it is believed that this also occurs on all other instances of Phenomenon 400. Cumulatively, these changes alone can cause significant casualties, as debris and wanderers can be thrown around by these changes in winds.

That said, these changes all depend on the severity of the Clot. A Clots severity level depends on it's approximate distance away from the level, as well as its approximate mass. The more massive a Clot, the more severe gravitational distortions and atmospherically changes will occur. Conversely, the closer a Clot is to the level, the more frequent such changes are. Though not as important, the surrounding objects in the sky could also add to the danger by allowing for the formation of coagulates. Severity levels also vary slightly based of the duration within the Rupture; while the Clot is directly overhead, spacetime effects are at their peak, though the closer the Clot is to the level's horizon, the weaker these effects are.

⠀

II - III

SCARS

An image of a crack in the sky anomalously provided to the M.E.G. by a wanderer, likely taken in the Crystal Fields.

Occlusion fully comes to an end once the Clot sets and the opposing celestial body rises. From this point on, the effected Wounded will return to its original state, prior to the occurrence of Bleeding Stars. However, the phenomenon will still have several long-lasting effects on the level. While not technically part of the actual phenomenon, these remaining impacts of Bleeding Stars on affected levels are still noteworthy, and thus has been dubbed as "Scars".

The view of Level 270 the following day of the Rupture.

The first impact on the level is structural damage. Due to gravitational changes during the Rupture, buildings, objects, and plant-life will have been scattered and thus destroyed due to being lifted or have collapsed in on themselves from being pushed downwards. Either way, Phenomenon 400 has been known to leave levels in significant states of disrepair in the immediate time following the effects of the Clot. In addition, ejecta released by the Clot may have led to ongoing fires and combustion, though these are typically short-lived. Long-term, these changes are slowly reverted through a process of Level regeneration, akin to that of a level destabilising. The time-period it takes for a level to regenerate depends on the impact the phenomenon had on the level, though likely also randomly varies on a per-level basis.

From Haemorrhage, left-over cracks in the sky left by the Wounded have been recorded to last for years, if not decades, in the sky. Analysis of these cracks reveal that they are simply strands of plasma that have been anonymously "glued" in space and time. As such, the only way that these cracks will disappear is through the significant slowed dissipation of energy leading to recombination of the material into gas. Fortunately, these cracks show pose no additional threat within a level, due to the fact that no coagulates have ever been reported to have been released by the crack.

A final effect is the presence of gravitationally unstable regions in a level, still negating the re-established gravity within the level. Only 2 occurrences of this have been found, with only 1 of these still remaining to this day. Within these zones, gravity is far more unstable, leading to placed objects in this zone to be pulled down more rapidly than usual, fall more slowly than usual, or experience a 'negative' g-force, causing it to rise up. In addtion, this property- unlike during the Rupture- can affect wanderers too, but only if they are not on the level's floor while in the region (e.g.: In mid-air after a jump).

III

DOCUMENTATIONS

Below is a list of documentations of Bleeding Stars within the Backrooms, in descending order. Proper documentation does not exist for some of the older entries, so some inference has been made on details.

*Note that all dates used are based off the Frontrooms Date Theory

Worryingly, it appears the phenomena has been gaining frequency as- in only 30 years- there has been 3 cases of the phenomena, an outlier amongst the other known and even presumed dates of other phenomena in terms of average occurrences per decade. However, this is likely random and not tied to any other factor.

Footnotes

A final effect is the presence of gravitationally unstable regions in a level, still negating the re-established gravity within the level. Only 2 occurrences of this have been found, with only 1 of these still remaining to this day. Within these zones, gravity is far more unstable, leading to placed objects in this zone to be pulled down more rapidly than usual, fall more slowly than usual, or experience a 'negative' g-force, causing it to rise up. In addtion, this property- unlike during the Rupture- can affect wanderers too, but only if they are not on the level's floor while in the region (e.g.: In mid-air after a jump).

III

DOCUMENTATIONS

Below is a list of documentations of Bleeding Stars within the Backrooms, in descending order. Proper documentation does not exist for some of the older entries, so some inference has been made on details.

*Note that all dates used are based off the Frontrooms Date Theory

Worryingly, it appears the phenomena has been gaining frequency as- in only 30 years- there has been 3 cases of the phenomena, an outlier amongst the other known and even presumed dates of other phenomena in terms of average occurrences per decade. However, this is likely random and not tied to any other factor.

Footnotes

Phenomenon 30.title: Phenomenon 30
Phenomenon 30.description:
If any of your devices stop working.. you're screwed.
----

Phenomenon 30, also often referred to as "TIMEWARP SYNDROME", "TEMPORAL CORRUPTION" or "CHRONOPHAGE", is an extremely rare and perilous phenomenon which can be found on time-based/time travel levels (e.g., Level 80, etc.) Phenomenon 20 can occur at any time and at any moment, even when you don't expect it.

Phenomenon 30 is a phenomenon that can randomly brick/break any devices found in that specific level. If they don't brick, then they glitch out and produce static and strange noises. Some wanderers report that they can hear screaming and muffling, as someone is tied up and about to be killed.

Phenomenon 30 cannot break any Backchat devices for unknown reasons. Some Backchat devices can flash white, while others display cursed, unreadable Egyptian hieroglyphics. Some images posted on corrupted Backchat devices can turn into strange symbols and static, or a random cartoon/show you watched when you were a kid.

It will be distorted and look off. After an episode/part ends, a strange ticking clock will appear behind the character, and the clock will tick. The background will turn orange and non-saturated, and orange-like mist or dust will surround the screen. The clock ticks fast. When the clock reaches 12:00 am, everything turns black except the character and the clock, leaving a faint white glow behind.
Phenomenon 30.lead:
As you walk through the old run-down cabin in Level 80,

you look at your watch to see the time.

But then you find out that your watch has stopped working and has been bricked.

You think to yourself, "Why isn't my watch working?"

Luckily, your BackChat device didn't brick itself.

You find a post talking about the level you're in.

Unfortunately, there are no replies to the post.

You are doomed here, stuck possibly forever,

and forever,

and forever,

and forever,

and forever,

and forever..

and forever..

and forever..

and forever..

and forever..

left to rot.

Phenomenon 19.title: Phenomenon 19
Phenomenon 19.description:
Notice
As of January 19th, 2030, the last Phenomenon 19 event was May 10th, 2026, soon after the Riemann hypothesis was disproved (details contained in classified reports Zeta-a 1-3). As such, it has been considered "neutralized." This page is an archived excerpt 109, the last edit before the said date.

Phenomenon 16.title: Phenomenon 16
Phenomenon 16.description:
Phenomenon 16, colloquially referred to as “Borderline Fascination” or “The Mania Effect," manifests itself as strong, irresistible desires to stay inside a certain level for an indefinite amount of time. Once it has fully taken effect, individuals will experience strong feelings of serenity and exhibit symptoms similar to people affected by narcotic drugs. This can eventually spiral into impulsiveness and, therefore, lead to erratic and uncontrollable behavioral patterns. There is no warning as to when this phenomenon happens, with each case reported having occurred at different intervals from one another.

Although research information on this so far has been particularly scarce, many key details seem to point towards a specific theory: Since the Backrooms is a place of familiarity, yet not at the same time, it would make sense why some lost travelers would become greatly amused by the things they saw or had never seen before. Consequently, some will begin to develop a sense of belonging to a place they find comforting, which results in serious repercussions should they happen not to know about the spiritual manipulation of this twisted reality. 

There hasn’t been much M.E.G. operatives can do to deter tragic incidents like these from happening. As a result, many propaganda posters are displayed on the walls of multiple levels in an attempt to raise awareness and ensure no one stays put for too long. Temporary mental facilities are also expected to be set up to contain and treat people who are directly affected by the phenomena. If, for some reason, someone is considered “too far gone” or exhibits signs of undeterred hostility, the best way to go about that would be to terminate them completely so as to prevent further harm from spreading to innocents.
Phenomenon 16.lead:
Notice
The following document contains details that are highly speculative and/or require further examination in order to be deemed authentic. This is because subjective inserts are rampant in quantity compared to verified facts.

Under the order of M.E.G., all agents without proper credentials or consent from their superiors shall refrain from editing Phenomenon 16 to avoid creating a campaign of misinformation, which, in return, brings about widespread panic and potentially serious consequences for the offender.

Llyrvek Scale: 3/5
Frequency: 2/5 (Uncommon)

Borderline Fascination predominantly occurs inside safe havens, though this may be one-sided, considering it may also manifest in other levels outside of that category alone (albeit unconfirmed).

Hazard: 4/5 (Major)

Borderline Fascination can cause significant harm to one's mental fortitude and, in extreme cases, irreversible damage to the brain, particularly the prefrontal cortex (as reported by some neurosurgeons).

Enigma: 3/5 (Unresolved)

The gathering of precise data still encounters numerous setbacks. This version could be considered the most informative piece of guidance for now until further identification of Borderline Fascination is conducted.

The Co-Hosts.title: The Co-Hosts
The Co-Hosts.description:
The Co-Hosts are an advisory group of around four to six jovials who comprise Caius Taggert's entourage, effectively fulfilling the entire rudimentary government system of Level Fun. They are some of the only infected individuals known to simultaneously be jovials and partygoers. The Party Host has alternatively referred to them as his "children", his "ministers" and his "pets"; while it was initially believed that they were "running dogs" utilised to preserve the illusion of freedom amongst the Partygoer Jamboree, through the recent procurement of more information regarding the matter, the Party Pooper has discovered that the Co-Hosts are indeed quite influential in the government of Level Fun, working almost in tandem with rather than beneath Sir Taggert.

The membership of the order has seen many iterations since the conception of Co-Hostship in the mid-1800s, ranging from the earliest, commedia dell'arte-inspired retinue, to the modern, practical advisory council of manipulative and, to some extent, politically capable jovials. The shift away from goofier characters to a more serious and practical approach has, according to Taggert, been in accordance with the general rise of anti-partygoer groups and sentiments in the Backrooms. Interestingly, every generation of Co-Hosts seemed to have died together, giving rise to the theory that the Party Host actively orchestrates the replacement of each iteration with new, younger Co-Hosts.

Each Co-Host is apparently hand-picked and infected, usually secretly, by the Party Host himself. Unlike other partygoers, their minds are not eclipsed upon infection, and instead they often find themselves wandering through levels in an uncoordinated and semi-lucid state, subconsciously wired to seek out the Party Rooms, where Taggert will greet them warmly and integrate them into his council. Each Co-Host devises a "stage name" for themselves, whereby they are known among the wider public of the Wake, but the Party Host also gives them a new personal name in the spirit that they ultimately belong to him.

It is presumed that the Co-Hosts themselves are fearful in some respect of Caius Taggert, even if they show no outward signs of it. They indeed appear to treat him as merely an older brother-figure and occasionally mock him for his age, and a "suicide interview" with Co-Host Festus "Festhead" Zacharias has revealed his opinions of Taggert as a "nice, harmless old friend, if a little too uptight at times"; however, medical expert Dr Asclepius Ang has expressed doubt that "a person, unless their amygdala is completely fried, is incapable of fearing, even just the tiniest bit, a man who has them hung up on puppet-strings, and whom they know has them hung up on puppet-strings, and whom they know they can never escape."

Phenomenon 98.title: Phenomenon 98
Phenomenon 98.description:
"Non-Returns" refer to the occurrences of wanderers never returning after passing through a presumed level entrance. Wanderers claimed by this phenomenon cannot be contacted with by any means upon passing through a respective Non-Return point; any tracking/cross-level communication technology brought with the victim completely ceases function thereon.

Non-Returns are theorized to stem from levels with both technology-disrupting properties and no exits. Levels with technology-disrupting properties (i.e. Level 12) and no exits (i.e. Level 87) are known to exist mutually exclusive from one another; therefore, reasonably, levels with both features could be the basis to Non-Returns. Because any means of researching these theoretical Non-Return levels are not possible, this theory cannot be proven or disproven. This practiced theory regarding the origin of Non-Returns, in most cases, is referred to as the Common Non-Return Theory. Attempts at researching this phenomenon follow the Common Non-Return Theory as a framework.

This section will serve as a compilation of all publicized tests done to discover more information about Phenomenon 98. Depending on your database clearance, you may or may not be able to access the respective sources.

There currently exists [1] publicized test relating to Phenomenon 98.

Determining Event Horizon Thresholds of Phenomenon 98 Specific to Level 4
Source link: [ ☒ NO ACCESS ]
----
"In this experiment, varying scales of objects and organisms will be passed through a Non-Return point via remote operated device. The goal is to monitor at what time the Non-Return point beyond its respective entrance employs its effects."

"... therefore, following the Common Non-Return Theory, there exists a level-adjacent environment on the opposing end of the entrance. The lab guide is outlined below:"

"1. Locate a known Non-Return door in Level 4, with referred equipment on standby, then establish referred safety measures.2. Setup necessary equipment relating to cross-entrance transportation...."

"... 8. With the sedated hound secured to the moving belt, without puncturing the skin, gradually move the belt plate forwards into the doorway...."

"Analysis: When the subject hound's clavicle region passed through the door (00:12 of attached recording), light began to emit from the otherwise pitch-black end of the doorway. Much like the Earth's sun, the light was of equal luminosity and approximately equal coloration. The procedure paused at this point. Shortly after (00:41 of attached recording), without moving the plate further, the hound's body entirely disappeared in an instant. Only traces of the hound's hair on the table remained. At this point, the aforementioned light began to dim. By (02:03 of attached recording), the light was no longer present."

"Conclusion: Further study and analysis is required for conclusive results..."Attached recording: Untitledvideo_35.mov (file could not be found)

Interview Clip
"It really is a known fact of the Backrooms that people do just disappear sometimes. Every now and then, you'll hear about some poor soul who wandered off a bit too far from their base and couldn't be heard from again after they went through an entrance of some kind. What scares me most about that is - if we're going off of the idea that they go to an inescapable level - we have no clue what happens to them. And that could be you just as easily as it could be some random stranger. Best advice I could give you is to never think about going near some door or noclip spot that you're not sure is safe."

~ Madeline Betielle, editor for PassageJournal.org of the Backrooms Database's sources

humanity's most primal and deep-rooted fear is that of the unknown.

and that's the thing with the backrooms:

there's information humanity will never know.

like where wanderers go after they enter a non-return point

and never come back, as expected.

is it reality? is it a worse reality than here?

there's a reason why the fear of the unknown has such a strong grasp:

things rarely meet our expectations.

often what results is a worse outcome than what we expect.

and we think the worst we can imagine when we know nothing

that means, when someone goes somewhere to never return,

they can very easily experience worse than what we can imagine.

Level 0.02.title: Level 0.02
Level 0.02.description:
Description       Level 0.02, more commonly known as "Kenophobia" is believed to be the sister level of Level 0.01.
. The Level appears very similar to Level 0, resembling an office space, however the largest difference between Level 0.02 and its parent level is the lack of walls and the fact it appears to stretch an infinite distance. The flooring of this level consists of an old, moist carpet, appearing identical to that which covers the floor of its parent level. Above, The ceiling consists of various endless rows of ceiling tiles, each spanning an approximate distance of 2ft in each direction. Every tenth tile is reportedly replaced by a fluorescent light in a grid-like pattern. Due to the lack of walls, the reason the ceiling does not collapse in on itself is unclear, although it is theorised to be an anomalous property of this sub-level. Since the Sub-Level lacks walls, the humming originating from the fluorescent lights is amplified greatly. This can result in an occasional deafening droning noise which is documented to potentially cause damage to one's hearing. Despite possessing a seemingly infinite size, the air within this level is documented to be full of dust particles, limiting wanderers' vision, commonly creating a white or yellow haze in the distance. Due to this dust, wanderers who enter this level are commonly advised to leave consistent landmarks if possible as a way to determine where they have travelled this far.Level 0.02 is documented to become increasingly unstable the further out you venture, with Wanderers encountering a phenomena nicknamed "Structural Decay". Hallucinations in this level are common, ranging from minor auditory hallucinations to visual hallucinations. Many wanderers have reported seeing dark figures in the distance and running in another direction, however when looking back, the figure had seemingly vanished. It is crucial to bring Almond Water and other resources to this level to avoid going insane. There are also additional reports of wanderers hearing knocks or feeling a warm breath on the back of their neck. Due to the absence of documented entities, and the lack of reported harm, these are most likely to be hallucinations.

Communication inside of this level is not possible due to the absence of internet-dependent infrastructure. GPS will not function, and a compass will spin rapidly in random directions, rendering it useless. Wanderers cannot encounter one another due to the same isolation effect present in it's parent level. Its also theorised that Level 0.02 is a vast vein system that connects to Level 0.01, Level 0 and The Void.    Anomalous Proportions    PHENOMENON 23"Structural Decay"
Level 0.02 is subject to a variety of atypical properties, labelled "Anomalous Properties". The most documented and commonly known anomaly Level 0.02 is host to is known as "Structural Decay", which results in most wanderers exiting the level via The Void.

Structural Decay is a well-documented phenomena in Level 0.02, becoming quickly amplified the further out wanderers venture into the level. Upon first appearing in Level 0.02, it will appear as described. After voyaging out an approximate distance of 5 kilometres, one may encounter small cracks in the ceiling or carpet, revealing a pitch-black location surrounding the level. One may also begin to encounter fallen ceiling tiles after journeying a distance of more than 5 kilometres, which slowly become increasingly common. These fallen tiles reveal a white crawlspace above the tiles which one may access on occasion.  After walking for 7 kilometers, blackouts occur while the lights are reported to occasionally flicker, with more ceiling tiles scattered on the ground. After walking for 10 kilometers, some lights are damaged or completely broken, revealing exposed wires. After walking for 15 kilometers, most lights are broken except for only a select few, and many ceiling tiles cover the floor. Significant portions of carpet are ripped open, revealing wet, moldy and rotting wooden flooring beneath. The floor has sharp edges that may injure wanderers, making it crucial to stay alert. After walking for 20 kilometers, the level begins to warp, twist, and turn, presenting a major environmental threat to wanderers. After walking for 50 kilometers, the level completely breaks apart and appears to be crumbling away. Wanderers are advised to avoid proceeding past this point to prevent ending up in The Void.

Depending on the level you are, it can also change the form the structural decay destroys.     Entrances and Exits

entrances.text: Encountering a white doorframe in Level 0.01 or Level 0 and entering it will transport one here.

exits.text: Entering the small crawl space above the ceiling tiles will lead to Level 0.
Finding a white door with a sign saying "0.23" will lead to Level 0.23.
Journeying 50 kilometers in any direction will result in one's transportation to The Void, a feat which only 10 have accomplished.
Discovering a staircase leading down to a very long hallway takes one to Level 0.01.
Finding a large pool of water on the floor and entering will cause one to blackout and awaken in Level 37.

Phenomenon 21.title: Phenomenon 21
Phenomenon 21.description:
Phenomenon 21, also known as On And On, is a somewhat rare occurrence in which unusually repetitive yet expansive sub levels manifest for short periods of time before disappearing. While not inherently dangerous, since the sub levels in question do not usually contain any hostile entities, an unknown fate may befall those who wander inside unwittingly and fail to escape before the environment disconnects from the rest of the backrooms. The time between conception and disappearance of the sub levels seems random, with recorded cases ranging between a single day and multiple weeks. The longer the manifestation, the more wanderers will inevitably become trapped.

Despite initially seeming similar to Phenomenon 99, Phenomenon 21 exhibits several key distinctions; it only manifests sub levels, and said sub levels are confirmed to have existed at some point, as opposed to being blips of inconsistency in the timeline of the database or memories of wanderers.

Structurally, the phenomenon manifests environments almost perfectly resembling a single room from their parent level repeated over and over. Several wanderers have attempted to escape through the drop ceiling of a Lobby-esque manifestation, only to be met with another identical floor. The same is speculated to be true of all manifestations, infinitely in every direction. While certain aspects may vary between instances, each instance is known to have very few walls, yet an unyielding ceiling and floor; some kind of grid pattern, be it the ceiling, lights or even repeating puddles; and a distant haze of particles obscuring one's vision. Some wanderers have theorized that this last aspect may connect Phenomenon 21 to The Decay, signifying a fundamental error in the creation of new levels that instead results in unstable, temporary and repetitive husks. This is unconfirmed.

There have been rumors of the exact same sub levels reoccurring through Phenomenon 21 (albeit with different numbers), mostly as branches of Level 0. It is unknown if this is actually the case or if the manifested environments are so similar that they are indistinguishable from previous iterations.

Like their environments, the entrances and exits of instance of Phenomenon 21 are equally variable. Holes, doors, ladders, no-clipping and all other normal methods of entering new levels may lead here, making avoiding manifestations difficult. Some wanderers have even reported exits that used to lead to another level directing wanderers to a manifestation during its lifespan, after which the exit would either return to normal or vanish entirely along with the sublevel. If one believes they have stumbled into a manifestation and can still utilize the entrance they came in from, they should do so immediately and avoid wandering deeper inside, no matter how tempting it may be. Navigating manifestations of Phenomenon 21 is near impossible since every direction is identical.Once its time is up, all entrances to the sublevel in question either disappear entirely or redirect wanderers who interact with them to a completely different level. Since each instance also seems to be affected by Phenomenon 15, the fate of those who are inside the manifestations after disconnection is unclear. It is widely believed that they simply cease to exist along with the sublevel. Considering the alternative is to be lost indefinitely, instant death would be a mercy.

Phenomenon 45.title: Phenomenon 45
Phenomenon 45.description: Phenomenon 45 also known as False Level Keys are a Phenomenon where a level key is disguised as a Safe Level Key but really sends the wanderer to an unknown dangerous level. While this happens most often with The Red Rooms where its Level Keys can take the appearance of different level keys, its known to affect a wide variety of levels. This Phenomenon is poorly understood, but it is known that a fake key feels flaky and can have its paint removed easily, which will reveal the illusion.
Phenomenon 45.lead: Phenomenon 45 also known as False Level Keys are a Phenomenon where a level key is disguised as a Safe Level Key but really sends the wanderer to an unknown dangerous level. While this happens most often with The Red Rooms where its Level Keys can take the appearance of different level keys, its known to affect a wide variety of levels. This Phenomenon is poorly understood, but it is known that a fake key feels flaky and can have its paint removed easily, which will reveal the illusion.

Phenomenon 70.title: Phenomenon 70
Phenomenon 70.description:
Phenomenon 70, also known as “Casper-Bray Rifts”, is a spatial anomaly appearing against the fabric of space-time. Those rifts often take the form of a white, glowing, floating point. Rifts are thought to be made of something other than regular matter and are thought to be a cluster of an unidentified hub category between fermions and bosons.

Casper-Bray Rifts interact with bosons in a special way; while the rift emits and reflects electromagnetic radiation, this process is thought to happen on a surrounding field called "Rift Field," filling an area depending on the rift’s size. Casper-Bray Rifts attract mass in a polar approach, with it theorized to have an opposite sign for Higgs bosons; thus, rifts are said to have a “negative mass.” Unlike gravitational forces, this negative mass allows rifts to remain in place motionless, while warping the space around them via a logarithmic directional propensity.

Casper-Bray Rifts disruption of space can vary with the intensity of the rifts, which is measured by how strongly their “Rift Field” counteracts electromagnetic rays; this may correlate with the rifts’ size on regular occasions. Intensity is directly increased by the more “negative mass” it has; due to this, a rift will get smaller and weaker on physical interactions.

Casper-Bray Rifts serve as interlevel transportation; interaction with a rift causes one to change location depending on the principal or abstract patterns. Usually, a rift will lead one into the level range of a few before or after on the Casper-Bray Numbering System; this happens in the majority of the encounters on numbered levels. Abstract patterns such as undetermined categories or anomalous levels, Casper-Bray Rifts might deform environmental features associated with similarities between tied levels.

Even though theorized, Casper-Bray Rifts might allow us to discover new kinds of subclusters and hidden relations between levels, but for now, most features of Casper-Bray Rifts remain an enigma.

Phenomenon 354.title: Phenomenon 354
Phenomenon 354.description:
Threat Index: Class 0
>> Safe
>> Stable
>> Home?

Recovered Data File.title: Recovered Data File
Recovered Data File.description:
Marauders are an invasive and military group of humanoid entities that are known for raiding and conquering from various levels, be they safe or dangerous classes. Their goal remained unknown; however, witnesses who survived from these entities' assaults reported that some of the razed levels that they had once inhabited collapsed and were finally destroyed, which thus raises concerns among the wanderers.

They are distinguishable in their black and red tactical outfits and equipment and their distinct singular red eyes.

Given their hostile nature and their unexpected appearance, many wanderers and groups are hostile toward these entities; however, they are more hostile toward the Travelers, a group of reality-warping entities, suggesting the long history between the two rival groups.
Recovered Data File.lead:
Skip >>

Intercepting lost transmission █

"They found us! Get to the escape route!"

"No use, they're already over there! They're cornering us like rats!"

"Are there anyone to help us?! Site Director? Anthony?!"

"We- we're on own our own..."

"You can't be serious... Shit! They really cut us off!"

"They set us up! Those lying bastards, I'm gonna-"

"They're getting closer! Get yourself armed and be ready!"

--- ###: Target site has been capture. Resistance have met and are neutralized. Survivors have been surrendered themselves.

/// Attention: All active units in the enemy's sector / Supervisor █████ is on his way to your location.

--- ###: Copy. All units stand down and extract their data. Remain on high alert. Guarding Supervisor █████ is our top priority.

/// Attention: Supervisor █████ have arrived.

My, my, look what you have done to this place. But great work of disposing them as always. So...

Spying on us, are you?

I have watched you long enough, though I confess I was curious as to why your men hiding in the shadows and then decided to attack one of my premises. Do they tell you that it is rude to trespass and vandalizing on another’s privacy, Site Director?

You...

You're destroying it...

Destroying? Oh, that? Hah! Fearful for something that small? I was merely sifting through the refuse of this fractured, decaying reality. What is it you call it... the "Backrooms," yes? Quite an odd name, though fitting from natives like you.

--- ###: Supervisor █████. They have extracted and confiscated of their data regarding their recent records. Recommendation for data purging?

Hmm, no... perhaps some of them. Archive it, and let them have it where it belongs.

--- ###: Understood.

It is a shame, truly—that you mistook us for monsters, and lash at us out of fear. And for that, you have to face the consequences and cost their lives. A fair exchange, is it not? Well, Let me show you. You there. If you would?

Wait, please- No!

You bastard... what're you going to do with them?

Touching.... Yet it was never your choice that sealed your people’s fate. No, that came from the rotten, unappreciated fools who deemed you expendable and cast you aside. It is truly a sad state of affairs, is it not?

They... I... Please, just let them go...

Hmm... Perhaps we make an arrangement, yes? If you would be so kind—attend to them.

--- ###: Yes sir. Hold them down...

Damn it! Just let them go, they have nothing to do with this!

Oh, you are mistaken, Site Director. I am keeping your word. For their own good, of course. Better they reside in the Butcher’s domain than fall into the hands of my... unruly Deacons then have them use like unwanted, broken dolls. And you would not want to know what happen if I let them have your people, would you?

What?... You sick fuck!

--- ###: All of them were converted and ready for transportation, sir. What should we do about him?

Leave him. But rest assured, Site Director, she will handle them with the utmost care. Men, should the Deacons ever approach, thinking to interfere with these people, remind them... I see all that happens here. They will think twice before crossing me.

--- ###: Yes sir, allocating them to Supervisor ██████'s sector.

See? I keep your word. And Site Director... you have to put trust in me. Now, send him to the main Nexus.

--- ###: Stand up, now. Take him to the Overseer.

And as for you, little machine.

You can keep this little research of theirs. I care less if you share that to someone. After all, what can they do to stop your masters' rampage?

[Recovered Data File] █

Entity Research Committee.title: Entity Research Committee
Entity Research Committee.description:
🗕 🗗 ✖←  → ↻  ⌂🔓︎ backnet40://erc.bri/home

Home    About    Contact    Jobs

The Entity Research Committee

Who We Are

The Entity Research Committee, or ERC for short, is a Backrooms group instituted to analyze and research entities. The ERC is responsible for the discovery and documentation of numerous entities, as well as being a notable contributor to the M.E.G. database. The ERC has a number of outposts and laboratories stationed throughout the Backrooms. These outposts are not established within levels exhibiting entity infestations; instead, we reside in safer levels. This allows us to easily recruit new members and additionally aid wanderers in their travels. While levels aren't one of the group's priorities, the committee does occasionally study them.

Hierarchy

Similar to other Backrooms groups, the ERC comprises a unique hierarchy of designated roles. The hierarchy is as follows:

• The Directors
• Heads of Research
• Heads of Security
• Scientists
• Field Researchers
• Guards
• Janitorial Positions/Positions of Lesser Importance.

Alliances

Presently, the Entity Research Committee is allied with a few Backrooms groups, which typically assist or trade with the ERC in exchange for things such as entity data. The ERC is currently allied with the following groups:

• Major Explorers Group

Outposts

As mentioned before, the ERC has many outposts stationed throughout the Backrooms. The current list of outposts is as follows:

They Who Seek.title: They Who Seek
They Who Seek.description:
They Who Seek, also known as "The Seekers" or "TWS", is a cultist group that worships an Empyrean named “He Who Sees”. The group was founded in the mid-1900s in which the followers seek to He Who Sees, AKA "The Seer" or "HWS", for immortality and any other things that they were promised by him, thus their name.

Though many join voluntarily, some wanderers are also threatened or manipulated into the cult, either by the group or HWS himself, who does so through minds of mortals, whether it’d be dreams, hallucinations, or any other conditions in which the victim is not mentally there. Those suffering from low sanity are advised to try to drink almond water and sleep as much as they can, as they are in higher risk of encountering these types of approaches from HWS than those who are well-rested and mentally stable.

As mentioned before, some wanderers are threatened or manipulated into the group, so members, especially ones in charge of a single division or team, may have ideologies that contradicts another’s ideas, as well as their demeanors. Because of this, when encountering a group from this cult, they may be neutral or outright hostile, so caution is advised.

A artistic depiction from an amateur artist

He Who Sees, who mentioned before is called "The Seer" or "HWS", was one of the many Empyreans who suffered from the Dawn Wars, unbeknownst to everyone who isn't an Empyrean or any other mythical entity.

HWS happened to die in the war, but for unknown reasons, was brought back to life not long after. Now blinded by rage and vengeance for what happened to him, he attempts to get anything he wants done through his cultists, by any means necessary.

Travelers of Stars.title: Travelers of Stars
Travelers of Stars.description:
Travelers are an advanced race of entities possessing powerful reality-bending properties. They are distinguishable by their lavish suits of armor.

They consider themselves part of the "Outsiders," a group of beings originating from the aptly named "Outside," in a sense, they do not belong to the Backrooms. Having recently arrived, they are actively exploring various levels and interacting with many wanderers along the way.

Unlike any typical entities, they are considered as "alien" wanderers and are often welcomed due to their properties when fending off hostile entities and navigating dangerous levels for the wanderers.

It is believed that they are the architects of the Palace and the manufacturers of the Golden Artifacts. Though, those creations are attributed to their ancestors.

They are the enemies of the Marauders.

Party Crashers.title: Party Crashers
Party Crashers.description:
With origins as far back as the early 1880s, the Party Crashers' incentive is to stomp out Partygoer presence wherever it may take root; whilst also providing ample aid and welfare to communities ravaged by the ongoing conflict. Although generally friendly to most communities, militants tend to have a merciless attitude towards the SFV virus, with each and every unit being equipped with ample ammunition and equipment to properly squash threats.

Members of the Party Crashers are easily identifiable by their dark-blue masks: which not only protect them from exposure to SFV but also serve as a means of identification from a distance, although this identification can be a disadvantage on occasion. Along with this, units don grey hooded sweatshirts worn atop several layers of clothing, as well as padded cargo pants to limit exposure as much as possible. Certain crashers opt to take their protective gear several steps further, reinforcing their attire with duct tape, additional fabrics, and scrap metal, with many including rudimentary metal helmets in their apparel. Regardless of these variations in attire, all crashers are identifiable by cloth bands tied around their left arm; with colors and stripes used to identify awards, ranks, and battalion.

A vast majority of Party Crasher units live in a nomadic lifestyle — with only a select few members persisting in small camps and settlements around populated levels as a form of chaos control. The member count of the Party Crashers is known to be extraordinarily high; as some estimates record their exact population to be somewhere in the tens of thousands. Gaining membership within the group is partly difficult, as a mandatory 15-day preventative quarantine is required, as well as extensive training on par with the United States Marines within: The Frontrooms.

In terms of weaponry, the Party Crashers flaunt an extensive arsenal which has yet to be replicated by any group as of yet — with their main form of arms being cheap, yet effective firearms, with their undisclosed base of operations having ample resources to arm each member with variations of the Sten gun, as well as basic long-ranged firearms. Along with this, crasher units are also trained to weaponize objects, with many opting to utilize both Greek Fire and Liquid Pain due to their volatile properties. Experimental weapons, such as hardened liquid pain in the form of explosive blow-darts and volatile Firesalt rounds have been demonstrated during the Euphoric wars with varying degrees of success.
Party Crashers.lead:
The Backrooms is a treacherous place.
With omnipotent threats lurking around every facet of this living nightmare, it has become our incentive to destroy it, no matter the repercussions.
Because we persist, we adapt, and we defend.
We are the Party Crashers.

GROUP OF INTEREST

PRIMARY GROUP TYPE
Protection

Influence: 6/10

Hostility: 3/10

Organization: 9/10

The Party Crashers are a human militia dedicated to containing and eradicating the Sanguine Festivus virus and its victims.

Phenomenon 2.title: Phenomenon 2
Phenomenon 2.description:
BACK-NET (Phenomenon 2) is essentially the Backrooms’ version of the internet; a wide area network that uses an anomalous protocol in order to transfer data between devices. Access points to BACK-NET are usually exclusive to levels mimicking manmade structures such as Level 0, Level 4, or Level 9, making long-distance communication difficult outside of the few levels with the connection.

However, unlike the baseline Frontrooms internet, BACK-NET holds various anomalous properties that distinguish it from a normal wireless network, with the network being mysterious in nature and often quite unstable at times. Research by The Kauer Research Organization into BACK-NET is ongoing to this very day.
allLinks: Enigmatic Space Information Security & Protection System

Backrooms Investigation Foundation.title: Backrooms Investigation Foundation
Backrooms Investigation Foundation.description:
Description
The B.I.F is a law enforcement agency in The Backrooms dedicated to preventing and stopping crimes as well as keeping wanderers safe inside The Backrooms. They were founded in 1989 after an increase in anarchist and criminal groups in The Backrooms. They have many outposts all throughout The Backrooms and will commonly help wanderers reach the exit of the current level they are on. The group is not considered to be one of the most powerful in terms of manpower, but it makes up for it in training and effectiveness.

The group holds a lot of important supplies such as weapons, almond water, multiple objects, wood, and copper wiring. B.I.F recently has suffered a personnel shortage due to reforms and removing all corrupt personnel. B.IF's main base is located in Level-11 and the lobby is open to wanderers to report crimes, trade resources, or just to rest for a day or two. Wanderers and Settlement Leaders can also ask for B.I.F protection, in exchange for a deal where they will tell them information about crimes in the surrounding areas.

Structure
The ranking structure in B.I.F is very similar to organizations from The Frontrooms such as the Federal Bureau of Investigation and Central Intelligence Agency. The group is led by a voted in Director usually a former high ranking Investigations agent, the Director is responsible for keeping the internal operations running smooth and usually to direct diplomacy. Wanderers can join the B.I.F by applying in their headquarters or any major base, afterwards they will go through 7 weeks of training and classes. After you graduate out of training, they will then be given the rank of Entry Agent and partnered up with a more experienced Agent until they get promoted. B.I.F Agents can choose their station, and many decide to stay at the headquarters as it is one of the best stations. Agents can also apply to become part of a B.I.F SWAT team and will go through additional training for that.

Departments
